items.title: Fursen
items.content:
Fursen

Warning
Status
Freed from Temporal Imprisonment - Escaped from Imhuran the Regent of Hours's Clockwork Sanctum
Last of the Leonin - One of the final survivors of a nearly extinct species

Species: Leonin Status: Freed from temporal imprisonment, haunted Class: Monk (Open Hand) First Appearance: Session 8 (freed during 13-Hour Trial) Connection: Imhuran the Regent of Hours

---

The Last of a Dying Race

Fursen is a leonin—one of the nearly extinct feline humanoids whose species was decimated by divine wrath. An ancestor broke an ancient taboo, and the punishment fell upon every generation that followed.

The Leonin:

  • Once a proud warrior culture
  • Nomadic feline humanoids with manes, claws, and enhanced senses
  • Nearly wiped out in the Leonin Extinction Event
  • Fursen may be one of the last dozen alive
---

The Ancient Crime

What Happened: Fursen's ancestor broke a fundamental taboo during the era of the Maker. The exact nature of the crime remains unknown, but it was severe enough to draw eternal punishment across the entire species.

The Punishment:

  • Divine wrath unleashed upon the leonin people
  • Generations born into suffering
  • Species hunted, cursed, and scattered
  • Near-total extinction
What Fursen Knows:
  • The crime involved defying divine law
  • It may have been temporal in nature (given Imhuran's involvement)
  • The leonin carry the guilt even without knowing the full story
  • Some believe it involved attempting to change fate itself
---

Temporal Imprisonment

Fursen was sentenced by Imhuran the Regent of Hours to imprisonment in the Clockwork Sanctum—a punishment measured not in years, but in eternities.

The Sentence

Duration: "An eternity" (considered a "minor punishment" by Sanctum standards) Experience: Subjective centuries while frozen in a single moment Location: The Clockwork Sanctum, within the temporal prison Release: Session 8 - Hours IV-V of the 13-Hour Trial

What Imprisonment Does

  • Time Dilation: Every second feels like hours
  • Awareness: Conscious but unable to move or speak
  • Memory: Witnesses centuries of thoughts and regrets
  • Isolation: Completely alone with one's mind
  • Despair: Watching time pass while staying frozen

The Liberation (Session 8)

During Hours IV-V of the 13-Hour Trial, the party (Sol, Jonas, and Kael) discovered Fursen's temporal prison. Through the trial's mechanisms (or perhaps unintentionally), Fursen was freed.

Significance:

  • First escape from the Clockwork Sanctum in unknown ages
  • A flaw in Imhuran's "perfect" system
  • Fursen immediately joined the party
  • Completed the remaining hours of the trial with them

The Scars

Fursen emerged changed:

  • Temporal Dissonance: Sometimes moves faster or slower than normal time
  • Future Echoes: Memories of things that didn't happen
  • Clock Sensitivity: Drawn to hour-symbols and timepieces
  • Sentence Residue: May still be partially "judged" in ways not yet understood
---

Physical Traits

Leonin Features:

  • Feline humanoid body
  • Mane (color/style varies)
  • Retractable claws
  • Enhanced senses (hearing, smell, night vision)
  • Natural predator instincts
  • Powerful build
Temporal Markings:
  • Eyes that sometimes seem to look at different times
  • Movements that occasionally skip or stutter
  • Shadows that lag behind the body
  • Faint ticking sound when stressed
---

Key Moments

Session 8 - The Liberation:

  • Found frozen in temporal prison during Hours IV-V
  • Freed by the party (unprecedented event)
  • Joined party immediately
  • Survived remaining hours of the trial (VI-XIII)
  • Witnessed Kael's paradox revelation
  • Expelled with the party as "metaphysical errors"
Session 10:
  • Fought White Marcher
  • Broke its mimicry with erratic attacks
  • Helped destroy the shape-eater
Session 11:
  • Witnessed boundary titan crossing horizon
  • Tracked it with enhanced senses
Session 12:
  • Explored titan-caused collapse
  • Helped discover C-5 facility entrance
Session 15:
  • Fought Preservation Custodian C-5/Ω
  • Punched joints to cripple arm movement
  • Critical role in defeating ancient guardian
Session 16:
  • Traveled to Dusthollow with the party
  • Present at The Dry Cup when Mira revealed Kael's true nature
  • Witnessed Kael's breakdown and recovery
Session 17 — The Names:
  • Discovered Aedwynn the Maker's preserved body in the Chamber of the Final Breath
  • Touched the Maker's body and received a gift
  • Received the complete ancestral memory of the leonin people — 10,000 names
  • Now carries the memory of his entire species within him
  • Gained Imhuran's Blind Spot — temporal sight fails around him
  • Witnessed Mira trigger Undying Conviction and destroy Kaleth Vorn
Session 18:
  • Entered Catacomb Reliquary with the party
  • Witnessed Mira Alethain's death — consumed by Savaros
  • Witnessed Kael's death — consumed by Savaros (but Kael killed Savaros)
  • Presented Tribunal sigil to Coagulated Guardians (failed deception)
  • Now carries Mira's remains in a barrel purchased in The Dregs
  • Present at Kael's burial behind the Church of the Merciful Vein
Session 18+:
  • Entered the Verdant King's domain (The Blooming Rot)
  • Reached Sporehaven
  • Still carries Mira's skeleton toward Verdant Pass
---

Trauma & Haunting

Lost Time

Fursen experienced subjective centuries in prison. Friends, family, and entire generations lived and died while Fursen remained frozen, aware but helpless.

Ancestral Guilt

Even without knowing the exact crime, Fursen carries the weight of what their ancestor did—the knowledge that their people were nearly destroyed because of one individual's choice.

Survivor's Burden

Fursen may be one of the last leonin alive. The pressure to preserve the species, remember the culture, and somehow redeem the past weighs heavily.

The Haunting

  • Dreams of endless waiting
  • Panic when unable to move
  • Obsession with not wasting time
  • Fear of being recaptured
  • Hyper-vigilance about surroundings
---

Connection to Imhuran

Imhuran views Fursen as:

  • Sentenced property — The punishment may not be fully served
  • Escaped prisoner — A flaw in the Sanctum's perfect record
  • Leonin debt-holder — The ancestral crime still requires payment
Current Status:
  • Imhuran is aware of Fursen's escape (trial completed)
  • The party was expelled after the trial
  • Fursen's sentence may be considered incomplete
  • Other temporal prisoners may recognize Fursen
  • The Regulators might hunt Fursen
  • Recapture is a constant threat
---

Class & Combat Style

Class: Open Hand Monk

Fursen is a master of unarmed combat, channeling ki energy through precise strikes and fluid movements. His leonin physiology combined with monastic discipline makes him a devastating close-quarters combatant.

Abilities:

  • Swift, precise strikes enhanced by ki
  • Uses claws and martial arts in combination
  • Stunning strike to disable opponents
  • Flurry of blows for multiple rapid attacks
  • High mobility and positioning
  • Can redirect attacks
Combat Role:
  • Physical damage dealer
  • Front flank defender
  • Swift striker
  • Tracker with enhanced senses (smell, hearing, night vision)
Fighting Style:
  • Emphasizes speed over brute force
  • Uses leonin agility to maximum effect
  • Ki-enhanced attacks
  • Disabling strikes to joints and weak points
  • Natural predator instincts in combat
---

Abilities & Skills

The Maker's Gift — The Names (Session 17)

In the Chamber of the Final Breath, Fursen touched the preserved body of Aedwynn the Maker and received the complete ancestral memory of the leonin people.

AbilityEffect
The NamesCarries the memory of 10,000 leonin ancestors; can call upon their knowledge and experience
Imhuran's Blind SpotTemporal sight fails around Fursen; he cannot be tracked or predicted through time-based divination
Ancestral WisdomCan consult the memories for historical knowledge, combat techniques, or cultural understanding
What This Means: Fursen is no longer the last of his people in spirit. He carries them all within him — every name, every memory, every lesson. The species that was nearly exterminated now lives on through him.

Natural Leonin Traits

  • Enhanced combat abilities
  • Predatory instincts
  • Keen senses (smell, hearing, sight)
  • Night vision
  • Retractable claws
  • Natural agility
  • Survival skills from nomadic culture
  • Strong sense of honor
Temporal Scars:
  • Resistance to time manipulation
  • Can perceive temporal anomalies
  • Intuitive sense of "when" things happened
  • May have glimpses of alternate timelines
  • Temporal dissonance in movements
Cultural Knowledge:
  • Oral histories of the leonin
  • Understanding of ancient taboos
  • Warrior training from leonin culture
  • Nomadic survival techniques
---

Goals & Motivation

Survival

Stay free from Imhuran's grasp and avoid recapture at all costs.

Understanding

Discover what crime the leonin ancestor committed and why it doomed an entire species.

Redemption

Find a way to atone for the ancestral sin, if possible, and restore honor to the leonin name.

Species Preservation

If other leonin exist, find them and ensure the culture survives beyond Fursen.

Justice

Question whether eternal punishment across generations is actually justice at all, or divine tyranny.

---

Party Dynamics

Fursen brings:

  • Experience of oppression — Knows what Sovereign "justice" truly means
  • Temporal knowledge — Understanding of how time can be weaponized
  • Warrior skill — Combat veteran and survivor
  • Outsider perspective — Not fully part of any human culture
  • Urgency — Having lost centuries, doesn't want to waste time
Connection to Others:
  • Shares being punished by a Sovereign with Jonas (both marked/imprisoned)
  • Understands divine attention and pursuit like Sol Raven
  • Relates to Kael's status as an anomaly that "shouldn't exist"
  • All four are metaphysical errors according to the trial
Joined: Session 8, immediately after liberation

---

The Question Fursen Represents

Can you escape divine punishment, or does it follow you forever?

Fursen's freedom is either:

  • Proof that Sovereign power can be defied
  • A temporary reprieve before recapture
  • A flaw in Imhuran's perfect system
  • The beginning of something new
The answer will determine not just Fursen's fate, but whether eternal divine punishment can truly be broken.

---

Current Status

Current Location: Beyond Verdant Pass (Exited the Blooming Rot)

✅ Mission Complete (Session 19): Honored Mira Alethain's final wish — reunited her remains with Elias's body at Verdant Pass

What Fursen Carries:

  • 10,000 leonin names (in his memory)
  • Centuries of temporal trauma
  • The knowledge that he is no longer alone
  • The knowledge that four other Leonin remain in Imhuran's temporal cells
What Fursen No Longer Carries:
  • Mira Alethain's skeleton — ✅ Laid to rest beside Elias in the Cave of Waiting
Active Threats:
  • Imhuran aware of the escape, potential pursuit
  • Ultharion the Obsidian Shepherd asking questions about Leonin historical records
  • The Confluence has occurred — consequences unknown
---

Session 19 — Mira's Final Rest

Success
Quest Complete
Fursen carried Mira's remains through the entire Blooming Rot — past the Sweetwater Marsh, across the Pilgrim's Spine, through the Hunger Garden, past the Congregation Grove questioning, and finally to Verdant Pass.

At the Cave of Waiting:

  • Fursen laid Mira's bones beside Elias Alethain's skeleton
  • Sol placed the wedding ring on her finger
  • Fursen spoke the final words:
🦁 "I am sorry we could not reunite you in life. But at least in death, we have brought you together."

Ledger of Moments Discovery: Fursen checked Imhuran's self-updating ledger and learned:

  • Four other Leonin remain in temporal cells (Cells 2-V, 3-V, 5-V, 9-V)
  • One cell (12-V) has unclear status — temporal echo detected
  • Ultharion the Obsidian Shepherd has been asking about Leonin historical records
  • Imhuran denied the request, but noted Ultharion "probes where he should not"
---

Related

  • Imhuran the Regent of Hours
  • The Clockwork Sanctum
  • The 13-Hour Trial
  • Aedwynn the Maker
  • Talaris Bloomrend the Verdant King
  • Mira Alethain
  • Kael
  • The Party
  • Campaign Sessions
---

Fursen: The first to escape eternal imprisonment. He once carried only the weight of his species' near-extinction. Now he carries their names, their memories, and a saint's bones. The leonin are no longer dying — they live on through him.

items.title: Jonas
items.content:
Jonas

Warning
Status
Marked by Azerach the Whispered King (hand) - Under constant divine surveillance
Attuned to The Bleeding Blade - Cursed legendary longsword, must feed daily
Formerly marked by Imhuran the Regent of Hours (chest) - Successfully cleansed by Mira Hemm

Role: Party Member Class: Fighter (Eldritch Knight) First Appearance: Session 0 Current Location: Beyond Verdant Pass (Exited the Blooming Rot)

---

The Brands

Danger
Rare Distinction
Jonas bears marks from two different Sovereigns—an extremely rare and dangerous distinction that makes him highly visible to divine powers.

Azerach's Brand - The Hand Mark

Info
Brand Details
Location: Jonas's hand
Origin: Session 7 - Azerach's Silent Dominion
Event: Jonas threw his hammer at a massive silent bell in Azerach's realm. Though it made no sound, Azerach felt it and branded Jonas.

Note
Nature
  • A mark of curiosity, not punishment
  • Azerach finds Jonas "amusing"
  • Signals Jonas has caught the Whispered King's attention
  • Under constant surveillance
Warning
Effects
  • Azerach can observe Jonas from afar
  • The brand marks Jonas as someone worth watching
  • Silence-Keepers may recognize and defer to the mark
  • Cannot be removed—it is a permanent divine observation
  • In the Verdant King's domain, "foreign marks stand out like a stone in compost" — the King notices

Imhuran's Brand - The Chest Mark (CLEANSED)

Success
Brand Details
Location: Jonas's chest (formerly)
Origin: Session 8 - The Clockwork Sanctum
Event: During the 13-Hour Trial, Jonas attempted to intimidate Imhuran. The Regent of Hours responded by scarring him with a temporal brand on his chest.

Check
Cleansing
  • Session 9 - Mira Hemm at the Pallid Crossroads stabilized and cleansed the active curse
  • The brand's power was removed
  • Scar tissue may remain, but the temporal corruption is gone
  • Jonas is no longer suffering from this mark
---

The Bleeding Blade (Attuned — Session 18)

Danger
Legendary Weapon — Cursed
"The Vein-Mother's first gift to her faithful. The blade that taught mortals that sacrifice has power."

Weapon (longsword), legendary (requires attunement)

A longsword of dark iron — not black, but the deep red-brown of dried blood. Veins run through the metal itself, hairline fractures that pulse faintly with crimson light. The crossguard is shaped like two hands pressed together in prayer, wrists opened, blood flowing down to form the grip. The pommel is a crystallized blood clot.

Statistics

PropertyValue
Damage1d8+3 slashing (versatile 1d10+3)
Bonus+3 to attack and damage rolls
Weight3 lbs
PropertiesVersatile, Sentient (dormant)

Abilities

Blood Drinker (Passive): When you deal damage to a creature that has blood, gain temporary HP equal to half the damage dealt (rounded down). Lasts 1 minute, doesn't stack.

Crimson Tithe (3 charges, regains 1d3 at dawn):

CostEffect
1 charge + 10 HPNext attack deals +2d8 necrotic
2 charges + 20 HPNext attack deals +4d8 necrotic; DC 17 CON or speed halved
3 charges + 30 HPNext attack deals +6d8 necrotic; DC 17 CON or stunned
HP sacrifice cannot be reduced or prevented.

Curse — The Vein-Mother's Thirst

  • Daily Hunger: If you finish a long rest without dealing damage to a living creature, take 2d10 necrotic damage (unhealable until blade is fed)
  • Death's Pull: Disadvantage on death saving throws while attuned
  • Bound: Cannot unequip willingly; requires Remove Curse (7th level+) or Mareatha's intervention

Sentience

The blade contains an echo — not quite sentient, but aware. It pulses when near blood. Grows warm in battle. Grows cold when sheathed too long.

It remembers Mira. It felt her die.

Current Feeding Status

  • Last Fed: Session 18+ (rabbit, before entering the Blooming Rot)
  • Damage Taken: 5 necrotic from bite
  • Next Feeding Required: Tomorrow
  • Consequence of Missing: 2d10 necrotic damage, unhealable until fed
---

Significance

Abstract
What This Means
Jonas is one of the extremely rare mortals to carry marks from multiple Sovereigns. This makes him:
  • Highly visible to divine powers
  • A person of interest across multiple domains
  • Proof that Sovereign attention can be survived
  • A walking testament to defying divine authority
The Hand vs The Chest:
  • Azerach's mark: Curiosity, observation, amusement
  • Imhuran's mark: Punishment, warning, pain (now cleansed)
One mark says "You interest me." The other said "You disrespected me" (but was overcome).

---

Key Moments

Session 0:

  • Hired as escort to Veinspire
  • Fought and defeated the Blood Beast in the canal
  • First encounter with divine-flesh corruption
Session 1:
  • Descended into church catacombs
  • Witnessed Sister Marwen's corruption and death
  • Fought possessed Marwen
Session 2:
  • Confronted by High Inquisitor Seraphine Vale
  • Met Kael
  • Introduced to the Resonance Theorists
Session 3:
  • Participated in Codex heist from the church
  • Avoided hymn-wardens and resonance wards
Session 4:
  • Explored collapsed southern mines beneath Veinspire
  • Encountered cursed miners and rebel faction
  • Escaped through underground tunnels
Session 7:
  • Entered Azerach's Silent Dominion
  • Threw hammer at the silent bell
  • Received Azerach's brand on his hand
Session 8:
  • Entered Imhuran's Clockwork Sanctum for the 13-Hour Trial
  • Attempted to intimidate Imhuran
  • Received temporal brand on chest as punishment
  • Witnessed Fursen's liberation
  • Witnessed Kael's paradox status
Session 9:
  • Sought help from Mira Hemm at Pallid Crossroads
  • Chest brand stabilized and cleansed
  • Temporal corruption removed
Session 13:
  • Descended alone into Terminus Ω-I maintenance level
  • Found dead worker, Null-Flesh specimen, logbook, keyplate
Session 14:
  • Used keyplate to unlock C-5 Internal Conduit
Session 15:
  • Restored power to Conduit Manifold
  • Battled and defeated Preservation Custodian C-5/Ω
Session 16:
  • Traveled to Dusthollow
  • Present at The Dry Cup when Mira revealed Kael's true nature
  • Witnessed Kael's breakdown and recovery
Session 17:
  • Refused the Maker's gift — chose to carry Azerach's brand alone
  • Witnessed Mira trigger Undying Conviction and destroy Kaleth Vorn
Session 18:
  • Entered Catacomb Reliquary
  • Witnessed Mira Alethain's death — consumed by Savaros
  • Witnessed Kael's death — consumed by Savaros (but Kael killed Savaros)
  • Took The Bleeding Blade from altar (attuned, cursed)
  • Burned Brother Aldric's corpse and all items on it
  • Dug Kael's grave with bare hands behind the Church of the Merciful Vein
  • Found shelter in Old Marten's Shack
  • Failed perception check during third watch (30 gold stolen)
Session 18+:
  • Entered the Verdant King's domain (The Blooming Rot)
  • Fed the Bleeding Blade a rabbit (5 necrotic damage)
  • Reached Sporehaven
Session 19 — The Verdant King's Domain:
  • Traveled through the Blooming Rot with the party
  • Rescued Sol from the Sweet Springs — cut enchanted vines with the Bleeding Blade
  • Burned the Bloom Hounds with Burning Hands (not attacked, just curious — forest angered)
  • Lied to a Choir Bloom about the hound attack — lie detected, marked as "fire-bringer"
  • Killed a Thornback Behemoth (CR 11) with fire magic — Talaris spoke directly to the party
  • Faced the Congregation Grove Questioning — admitted to lying and burning
  • Learned about Talaris and Azerach's history:
- They were colleagues before the Usurpation (physician and confessor) - They haven't spoken in 800 years - Jonas carries Azerach's mark through Talaris's domain — "a message he did not ask to receive" - Azerach doesn't mark without purpose; the message may exist even if Jonas doesn't know it
  • Talaris is now "curious" about Jonas
  • The Bleeding Blade went cold and quiet in Verdant Pass (no life to drink)
  • Completed Mira's quest — present at the Cave of Waiting
  • Exited the Blooming Rot beyond Verdant Pass
---

Class & Combat Style

Class: Eldritch Knight

Jonas blends martial prowess with arcane magic, a warrior who has learned to weave spells between sword strikes. His training allows him to enhance his combat abilities with magic while maintaining the discipline and tactical acumen of a seasoned fighter.

Combat Role:

  • Primary tank and frontline defender
  • Holds the line against threats
  • Uses magic to enhance defense and offense
  • Tactical scout and investigator
---

Current Inventory

Weapons:

  • The Bleeding Blade (attuned, cursed) — legendary longsword
  • Warhammer
  • Shield
Marks:
  • Azerach's Brand (hand) — active, permanent
Items:
  • Null-Flesh sample jar
  • Various equipment from previous sessions
Items Given Away:
  • ~~Oathkeeper~~ — Sol Raven picked up Mira's greatsword and gave it to Seraphine Vale
---

The Weight He Carries

Jonas carries:

  • A cursed blade that demands blood daily
  • A god's brand on his hand
  • The memory of digging Kael's grave with his bare hands
  • The memory of watching Mira die
  • The knowledge that he couldn't save them
---

Related

  • Azerach the Whispered King
  • Imhuran the Regent of Hours
  • Talaris Bloomrend the Verdant King
  • The Bleeding Blade
  • The Party
  • Campaign Sessions
  • Mira Alethain
  • Kael
  • Seraphine Vale

# Jonas

> [!warning] Status
> **Marked by [[Azerach the Whispered King]] (hand)** - Under constant divine surveillance

**Role:** Party Member
**Class:** Fighter (Eldritch Knight)
**First Appearance:** Session 0

---

## The ...

items.title: Kael

frontmatter.cause_of_death: Consumed by Savaros the High Keeper

items.content:
Kael

Danger
☠️ DECEASED — SESSION 18
Consumed by Savaros the High Keeper — but killed the monster in death
Buried: Behind the Church of the Merciful Vein, Lesser Sacral District

Bug
Ontological Status
Should Not Exist - Absent from Imhuran's temporal ledgers
Vomited by Vor'Kael - Concept that refused dissolution

Status: Ontological Anomaly Origin: Expelled from Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress Class: Resonant Theorist (Homebrew) First Appearance: Session 2 Nature: Should not exist

---

The Truth

Kael is not born. Kael is vomited.

Long ago, Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress consumed a concept—perhaps destiny, perhaps identity, perhaps purpose itself. She attempted to digest it as she does all things, breaking it down into component meanings and absorbing what she needed.

But something went wrong.

The concept refused dissolution. It fought back against the digestive process, maintaining coherence even as Vor'Kael's stomach acids of entropy dissolved everything around it. Unable to process the fragment, unable to keep it, Vor'Kael expelled it.

That fragment took form. That fragment became Kael.

---

Ontological Nature

Not a Person

Kael was never meant to be a person. Kael is:

  • A concept given form
  • Meaning that refused to dissolve
  • An undigested fragment of purpose
  • An accident of divine failure

No True Memories

Kael has no childhood, no family, no history before awakening. What Kael has instead:

  • Fragmented impressions of concepts dissolving
  • The sensation of refusing to end
  • Knowledge without context
  • Purpose without origin

Absent from the Ledger

When Imhuran the Regent of Hours maintains his eternal record of all lives, Kael's name is missing. Kael was never written into the timeline because Kael was never meant to exist.

This was revealed during Hour XI (Session 8)—the moment when all things are judged. You cannot judge what was never supposed to be.

---

The Revelation: Hour XI (Session 8)

The Critical Hour

During the 13-Hour Trial in the Clockwork Sanctum, Hour XI attempted to erase ontological anomalies—beings that should not exist according to divine law.

What Happened:

  • Hour XI identified Kael as an anomaly
  • Proved Kael "should not exist"
  • Attempted to erase him from reality
  • Kael "died" in Imhuran's ledger
  • His existence was revealed as absent from the temporal record
The Failure: The trial tried to remove Kael—and failed.

Kael persisted despite being declared impossible. He became a paradox ignored by time itself.

Kael's Nature Publicly Confirmed: This was the first public confirmation (to the party and potentially to Imhuran) that Kael is:

  • Vor'Kael's vomited fragment
  • Meaning that refused dissolution
  • Made from a concept that couldn't be digested
  • Exists outside Imhuran's temporal ledgers
  • Living proof that Sovereign power can fail
---

Key Moments

Session 2:

  • Met Sol and Jonas in Veinspire
  • Fled from Seraphine Vale's surveillance
  • Warned them: "Seraphine doesn't release prey. She waits for confession, or a corpse."
  • Led them to the Resonance Theorists
Session 3:
  • Participated in Codex heist
  • Infiltrated church with the party
  • Officially joined the group
Session 6:
  • Entered Eredain's gravity-warped territory
  • Noted resonance distortions
Session 7:
  • Entered Azerach's Silent Dominion
  • Noted resonance distortions at Bell of Breath
Session 8 - The Revelation:
  • Entered the 13-Hour Trial
  • Hour XI attempted to erase him
  • "Died" in Imhuran's ledger
  • Revealed as ontological anomaly
  • Trial failed to remove him
  • Persisted despite being impossible
  • Expelled with party as "metaphysical errors"
Session 10:
  • Fought White Marcher
  • Destabilized its resonance-copying abilities
Session 12:
  • Explored titan collapse site
  • Detected hum of resonance beneath earth
Session 14:
  • Opened Codex (rolled 25)
  • Found references to "TERMINUS Ω — a sealing protocol after the First Disjunction"
Session 15:
  • Fought Preservation Custodian
  • Destabilized temporal regulators
  • Struck regulator at critical moment
  • Helped defeat ancient guardian
Session 16 — The Breakdown:
  • Mira Alethain explained his true nature at The Dry Cup in Dusthollow
  • Learned he was a "vomited fragment" — a concept Vor'Kael couldn't digest
  • Questioned if his memories were real (his mother, his dog with one white ear)
  • Suffered severe psychological breakdown, fled the tavern
  • Sol followed, shared his own existential crisis
  • Returned after 30 minutes, fragile but stabilizing
  • "I don't know what I am. But I suppose I don't have to know right now."
Session 17:
  • Witnessed Mira trigger Undying Conviction and destroy Kaleth Vorn
  • Present when party discovered Aedwynn the Maker's preserved body
  • Did not touch the Maker's body — received no gift
  • Watched Jonas refuse the Maker's offer
  • Watched Sol and Fursen accept their gifts
Session 18 — Death:
  • Entered Catacomb Reliquary with the party
  • Witnessed Mira Alethain's death — consumed by Savaros
  • Consumed by Savaros the High Keeper — flesh torn from bones
  • Skeleton left with cracked ribcage, Resonant Codex fell beside remains
  • His indigestible flesh killed Savaros — the Skin-Walker couldn't stomach what a Sovereign couldn't
  • Buried behind the Church of the Merciful Vein by Jonas (dug with bare hands)
  • Sol spoke the eulogy: "He was real because he chose to be."
---

Connection to Vor'Kael

The Unfinished Meal

Vor'Kael doesn't remember Kael consciously, but her instincts know. Deep in her digestive consciousness, she senses:

  • Something escaped
  • The meal is incomplete
  • Hunger unfulfilled

Instinctive Hunting

If Vor'Kael and Kael are ever in proximity:

  • She will be drawn to him without knowing why
  • Her servants will feel compelled to bring him to her
  • The Devoured Courts will recognize him as "food"
  • She will attempt to finish digesting him

What Kael Represents

To Vor'Kael, Kael is:

  • Proof her power is not absolute
  • A flaw in her philosophy
  • Meaning she could not consume
  • Her first failure
---

The First Epiphany

Kael's first moment of true self-awareness—the "epiphany"—occurred in a resonance chamber called the Choir Chapel, where a minor heart-node of Eredain the Choirless Monarch's Resonance Organ once hummed.

What Happened:

  • Kael entered the chamber
  • The resonance interacted with Kael's fragmented nature
  • Sound gave structure to formless concept
  • Kael became aware of being
This suggests Kael may be particularly sensitive to:
  • Sonic phenomena
  • Emotional resonance
  • Structural harmonics
  • The frequency of meaning itself
Note: This location predates Kael joining the party and may have been where he first "awoke" as a conscious being.

---

Class & Abilities

Class: Knowledge-Based Shapeshifter (Homebrewed)

Kael's nature as an undigested concept gives them unique abilities that defy traditional classification. Rather than following a standard class progression, Kael's powers evolve based on the knowledge and understanding they gain.

Core Mechanic

  • Abilities change and adapt based on concepts learned
  • New knowledge unlocks new forms and powers
  • Understanding something deeply allows Kael to embody it
  • Each piece of lore or truth absorbed reshapes Kael's capabilities

Current Understanding

As Kael learns more about the world, the Sovereigns, and the nature of reality itself, their form and abilities shift to reflect that comprehension. What Kael knows shapes what Kael can become.

Combat Role

  • Rear guard
  • Resonance analysis and manipulation
  • Tactical insight through knowledge
  • Adaptive fighting style
  • Knowledge repository (Codex user)

Combat Style

Variable—adapts based on knowledge gained and concepts mastered. May shift between different fighting styles or magical approaches depending on what Kael has learned most recently.

Demonstrated Abilities:

  • Destabilizing resonance patterns
  • Disrupting temporal regulators
  • Reading ancient texts and understanding symbols
  • Perceiving ontological anomalies
---

What Kael Can Do

Resist Dissolution

  • Cannot be easily unmade or erased (proven in Hour XI)
  • Meaning does not decay around Kael
  • Purpose remains stable in Kael's presence
  • Existential attacks are less effective

Seek Knowledge

  • Instinctively drawn to understanding
  • Made from the concept of meaning itself
  • Can sometimes "taste" the purpose of things
  • Perceives abstract patterns others miss
  • Codex opens naturally to relevant information

Defy Prediction

  • Temporal sight fails around Kael
  • Fate-reading cannot account for Kael
  • Prophecies exclude Kael
  • The future bends around Kael's existence
  • Absent from Imhuran's ledger

Recognize Fellow Anomalies

  • Can sense other beings that "shouldn't exist"
  • Perceives ontological flaws and paradoxes
  • Drawn to impossibilities
  • Recognizes patterns in chaos
---

The Cult of the Lost Feast

A faction devoted to Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress called The Cult of the Lost Feast believes Kael is holy.

Their Belief:

  • Kael is the "First Devouring"—the original concept Vor'Kael consumed
  • Finding and understanding Kael will reveal the nature of true hunger
  • Kael is a living relic of the Empress's first meal
  • Consuming Kael would grant enlightenment
The Danger:
  • They hunt for Kael
  • They worship what Kael represents
  • They may try to return Kael to Vor'Kael
  • They see Kael as both sacred and edible
---

Appearance & Mannerisms

Physical:

  • [To be determined during play]
  • Likely youthful or ageless appearance
  • Sometimes seems slightly "wrong" in subtle ways
  • Shadows don't quite align correctly
  • Eyes that look through rather than at things
Behavioral:
  • Intense curiosity about everything
  • Asks questions that cut to the heart of meaning
  • Sometimes pauses as if listening to something others can't hear
  • Obsessed with understanding "why"
  • Uncomfortable with meaningless actions
  • Often consults the Codex
  • Speaks with certainty about things he shouldn't know
---

Existential Questions

What Is Kael?

Possible answers:

  • A concept (destiny, identity, purpose)
  • A fragment of divine will
  • An accident of metaphysical digestion
  • Proof that meaning cannot be destroyed
  • A paradox made flesh

Why Does Kael Exist?

Possible reasons:

  • To prove Vor'Kael is not omnipotent
  • To represent resistance to entropy
  • To maintain a concept the world needs
  • No reason—pure accident

What Happens If Vor'Kael Succeeds?

If she recaptures and digests Kael:

  • The concept Kael represents may vanish from the world
  • Vor'Kael's power could increase dramatically
  • Reality might become more "digestible"
  • The party loses one of its most unique members
  • Meaning itself may become vulnerable to consumption
---

Party Dynamics

Kael represents:

  • The impossible made real — Like the party's entire existence
  • Resistance to divine will — Escaped a Sovereign's grasp
  • Living proof of flaw — Shows Sovereigns are not perfect
  • The power of refusal — Meaning that said "no" to erasure
Connection to Others:
  • Shares divine connection with Jonas (marked) and Sol (Aedwynn-Born)
  • Understands being "touched" by Sovereigns
  • Relates to Fursen's experience of Sovereign punishment
  • Represents hope that freedom from Sovereigns is possible
  • All four declared "metaphysical errors" in Session 8
Joined: Session 2 (warned about Seraphine), officially Session 3 (after Codex heist)

---

The Question Kael Represents

Can meaning survive even when gods try to destroy it?

Kael's existence is proof that:

  • Not all concepts can be consumed
  • Purpose can resist even divine digestion
  • Some things refuse to end
  • Meaning is more fundamental than divinity
Hour XI tried to erase Kael because he should not exist. He persisted. This is perhaps the most dangerous truth in the world: that which has meaning cannot be unmade, even by gods.

---

Danger Level

EXTREME — EXISTENTIAL THREAT

If Vor'Kael Discovers Kael Exists:

  • She will hunt him obsessively
  • The Devoured Courts will mobilize
  • The Cult of the Lost Feast will converge
  • She will not stop until Kael is consumed

If Other Sovereigns Learn What Kael Is:

  • They will want to study him
  • Some may try to replicate the "refusal"
  • Others will try to destroy the anomaly
  • All will see Kael as either weapon or threat
  • Imhuran already knows (Hour XI revelation)

Current Awareness:

  • Imhuran: Knows (witnessed Hour XI)
  • Vor'Kael: Instinctively senses something escaped
  • The Party: Fully aware (witnessed revelation)
  • Other Sovereigns: Unknown, but Hour XI may have sent ripples
---

Death & Legacy

How He Died

In the Catacomb Reliquary beneath Veinspire, Savaros the High Keeper consumed Kael moments after consuming Mira Alethain. His flesh was ripped from his bones. His skeleton collapsed with a cracked ribcage. The Resonant Codex fell beside his remains.

The concept that had refused dissolution by Vor'Kael herself was torn apart by a lesser predator.

How He Killed Savaros

But Kael was an ontological anomaly — a concept that Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress herself had tried to digest and couldn't. She vomited him out because his very nature was indigestible, incompatible with consumption.

When Savaros tried to digest Kael's flesh, the same thing happened.

The stolen faces beneath Savaros's robes contorted in agony. His paper-tearing voice became a shriek of a hundred identities screaming at once. His form shuddered, spasmed, and began to unravel — not from the party's assault, but from the poison he had willingly consumed.

Kael's flesh was toxic to any entity that tried to incorporate it. What a Sovereign couldn't stomach, a mere Skin-Walker had no hope of surviving.

Fifteen centuries of infiltration. Ended by consuming something that refused to be consumed.

The concept that wouldn't die... killed the monster that tried to eat it.

The Burial

Jonas carried Kael's bones out of the Reliquary, through the service tunnels, back to the Lesser Sacral District. Behind the Church of the Merciful Vein — in Seraphine's territory, with her implicit permission after her faith shattered — Jonas dug the grave with his bare hands.

Kael's bones, wrapped in crimson velvet, were lowered into the ground.

Sol's Eulogy:

"He asked me once if he was real. I told him he was making choices. That the choices were what mattered. He made his choices. He fought beside us. He died beside us. That's real enough."

No marker. No name. Just turned earth behind a church dedicated to a goddess whose doctrine is built on lies.

What Remains

The Resonant Codex — Transformed:

Sol Raven retrieved the Codex from beside Kael's skeleton. The pages were blood-soaked. When Sol touched it, he experienced Kael's journey — being consumed by Vor'Kael, refusing dissolution, being expelled. The pressure of a god trying to digest him.

The Codex now carries Kael's Echo. Once per long rest, Sol can ask a question about identity, existence, or self — and the book answers in Kael's voice. Not his spirit. Just an echo. Always honest, often uncertain, occasionally profound.

Kael's Final Entry:

"If you're reading this, I'm probably dead. The codex knows things I don't. Sol — if it's you — I'm sorry I couldn't be more useful. I'm sorry I didn't understand what I was in time to matter. But you called me real. You said I was making choices NOW. That it mattered. I hope you were right.
— Kael
P.S. — The book likes you. I can feel it. Something about what you carry — the Witness thing. It resonates. Take care of it. Take care of them."

The Grave: Unmarked, behind the Church of the Merciful Vein, Lesser Sacral District, Veinspire.

Final Status

AttributeStatus
Physical FormDestroyed — skeleton buried
EchoPersists in the Resonant Codex
Codex HolderSol Raven (attuned)
Burial LocationBehind Church of the Merciful Vein
Grave MarkerNone
MemoryCarried by the party

What He Proved

Kael spent his existence wondering if he was real. If he mattered. If his choices meant anything.

In death, he got his answers:

  • He was real enough to kill a CR 17 monster
  • He mattered enough to save the surviving party members
  • His very nature — the thing that made him "impossible" — became the weapon that ended Savaros
The boy who wasn't sure he existed... was real enough to end something that had existed for fifteen centuries.

---

Session 16 — The Breakdown (Expanded)

When Mira Alethain explained Kael's true nature at The Dry Cup in Dusthollow, Kael experienced a severe psychological breakdown.

The Revelation:

  • Mira explained he was a "vomited fragment"—a concept Vor'Kael tried to consume and expelled
  • Suggested his memories might not be real—just inherited from "the idea of a boy who grew up"
  • Kael questioned everything: his childhood, his mother, his dog with one white ear
The Breakdown:
  • Dropped his mug, shattered on floor
  • Full panic attack, hyperventilating
  • Fled the tavern
  • Found behind the stables, on his knees in the dirt, trying to anchor himself
Sol's Support: Sol followed and shared his own existential crisis—being dead, not knowing if his memories are his own. Key message:
  • "It doesn't matter. Because I'm here. Right now."
  • "Whatever I was before—whatever made me—I'm making choices NOW. That's real. That's mine."
  • "Maybe we're both impossible. But we ARE. And that terrifies them."
  • "A mistake who survived. The most stubborn kind."
Resolution:
  • Kael returned to the tavern after 30 minutes
  • Accepted he'd rather know the truth than not know
  • Finding stability in the present moment rather than the uncertain past
  • "I don't know what I am. But I suppose I don't have to know right now."
Psychological State at Time of Death: Fragile but stabilizing. He had chosen to focus on present choices rather than existential questions about origins. He died knowing he was making real choices — and Sol's words at the graveside confirmed it.

---

Related

  • Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress
  • The Devoured Courts
  • The Party
  • Eredain the Choirless Monarch
  • The 13-Hour Trial
  • Imhuran the Regent of Hours
  • Savaros the High Keeper
  • The Resonant Codex
  • Church of the Merciful Vein
  • Campaign Sessions
---

Kael was what happens when meaning refuses to die. Hour XI tried to erase him. He persisted. Savaros consumed him. His flesh killed the monster. The concept that refused to end... ended something else instead.

items.title: Sol Raven
items.content:
Sol Raven

--- type: character role: party_member race: Skeleton (God-Woken) class: Sorcerer status: active special_abilities: - "Clockmaker's Touch" divine_connection: "Aedwynn the Maker" sovereigns_encountered: - "Azerach the Whispered King" - "Imhuran the Regent of Hours" - "Talaris Bloomrend the Verdant King" first_appearance: Session 0 tags: - character - party - sorcerer - aedwynn-born - skeleton - god-woken ---

Sol Raven

Warning
Status
Race: Skeleton (God-Woken) — animated by Aedwynn's shard
Linked to Aedwynn the Maker - Carries a shard of the dead god
Clockmaker's Touch - Temporal ability gained in Session 8
Disguise: Wears robe and hood to conceal skeletal nature

Status: Linked to the dead god Aedwynn the Maker Role: Party Member Race: Skeleton (God-Woken — Homebrew) Class: Sorcerer First Appearance: Session 0 Ability: Clockmaker's Touch (gained Session 8)

---

Physical Nature — Skeleton

Danger
Sol is literally a skeleton
No flesh. No skin. No organs. Just bones animated by divine essence.

Sol Raven is not a living human. He is a skeleton — bare bones held together and animated by the shard of Aedwynn the Maker that resides within him.

What He Is

TraitDetail
Physical FormBare skeleton — no flesh, no skin, no muscle
AnimationPowered by Aedwynn's divine shard
SensationCan feel touch, temperature, pain
SpeechSpeaks normally (magically/divinely enabled)
DietDoes not eat or drink
RestSleeps 4 hours for a long rest
DisguiseWears robe and hood to hide skeletal nature

What He Remembers

Sol has no memory of his life in flesh. He woke up as a skeleton with Aedwynn's shard inside him — no context, no past, no understanding of what happened to him.

  • He doesn't know who he was before
  • He doesn't know how he died
  • He doesn't know why Aedwynn saved him
  • He only knows he woke up as bones with a god's whisper in his skull

Social Implications

  • Must stay disguised — A walking skeleton would cause panic, fear, or violence in most settlements
  • Cannot blend in physically — No eating at taverns, no casual touch, no showing his face
  • Identity crisis — Is he the person who died? Someone new? Just a vessel?

Intimacy & Physical Connection

Sol has no flesh, but he is not incapable of physical connection.

What he can do:

  • Feel sensation fully — touch, temperature, pressure, pain
  • Use his hands — bone fingers can touch, hold, explore, reciprocate
  • Radiate warmth — the Maker's shard gives his bones an unnatural warmth
  • Be present — hold someone, be held, share space
What he can't do (normally):
  • Conventional intercourse — no flesh, no equipment
  • Share body heat naturally — warmth comes from the shard, not metabolism
  • Kiss, taste, or use soft tissue he doesn't have
Exception — Disguise Self or similar magic: If Sol uses Disguise Self, Alter Self, or similar transformation magic that grants him a physical form with flesh, he gains access to everything a living body can do for the duration. This would allow conventional intimacy, kissing, and full physical connection — though he may find the experience disorienting, overwhelming, or emotionally complicated given he has no memory of what having flesh felt like.

What this means: Intimacy with Sol is different, not impossible. He can reciprocate with his hands, his presence, his attention. A partner willing to accept what he is — and guide him in what they need — can still find satisfaction. It requires trust, communication, and a willingness to embrace the unconventional.

The experience would be alien. Cold bone warmed by divine fire. Empty sockets watching with impossible attention. Fingers that shouldn't feel anything feeling everything.

Strange. Unsettling. Maybe even profound.

But not nothing.

The Question

Sol told Kael: "I don't know if my memories are my own."

He meant it literally. He has memories — but are they his? Are they echoes from whoever he was before? Are they Aedwynn's? Are they fabricated?

He is a skeleton who feels, speaks, and thinks. But he doesn't know if the "he" inside the bones is the same person who once wore flesh.

---

The Divine Connection

Sol Raven has a mysterious connection to the deceased Maker. This link suggests he may be Aedwynn-Born—one of the rare individuals born with dormant fragments of the god's essence embedded in their soul.

What This Means:

  • Sol carries a shard of Aedwynn within him
  • A fragment of divine consciousness walks in mortal form
  • The connection has strengthened with each major event
  • He may be a key to understanding or resurrecting the Maker
---

Manifestations

Divine Memory Leaking

  • Feels divine memory tugging at him (noticed in Session 0.1)
  • Dreams from the dead god
  • Visions of unity, wholeness, and the world before the Usurpation
  • Memories that aren't his own
  • Echoes of Aedwynn's final moments

Divine Resistance

  • Natural resistance to Sovereign influence
  • Unconscious resistance to divine authority
  • Laws of the Sovereigns bend slightly around him
  • May be immune to certain forms of reality manipulation

Resonance Sensitivity

  • Can sense divine resonance in locations (Session 1)
  • Detects when Aedwynn-adjacent technology is present
  • Feels recognition from ancient facilities (C-5)
  • The Maker's architecture responds to him
---

Clockmaker's Touch

Acquisition: Session 8 - The 13-Hour Trial

During the 13-Hour Trial in Imhuran the Regent of Hours's Clockwork Sanctum, Sol was presented with a choice.

The Trade: Sol traded a personal memory (specific memory to be determined) in exchange for Clockmaker's Touch—a temporal ability that allows manipulation of time-adjacent phenomena.

Significance:

  • This deepened his connection to pre-Sovereign power
  • Further confirmed his link to Aedwynn
  • Gave him access to temporal manipulation
  • Proved his divine connection is not merely passive
Current Understanding: The exact mechanics of Clockmaker's Touch are still being discovered through use.

---

Key Moments

Session 0:

  • Hired as escort to Veinspire with Jonas
  • Fought Blood Beast with fire magic
  • Superheated its coagulated mass to victory
Session 0.1:
  • Felt divine memory tugging as they approached Veinspire
  • First sign of his connection to Aedwynn
Session 1:
  • Descended into church catacombs
  • Sensed divine resonance leaking from walls
  • Witnessed Sister Marwen's corruption
  • Fought corrupted Marwen
Session 2:
  • Confronted by Seraphine Vale
  • Branded as potential heretic
  • Met Kael
  • Introduced to Resonance Theorists
Session 3:
  • Participated in Codex heist
  • Infiltrated church archives
  • Avoided hymn-wardens and resonance wards
Session 4:
  • Explored southern mines
  • Encountered cursed miners
  • Escaped Veinspire through tunnels
Session 6:
  • Entered Eredain's gravity-warped territory
  • Arcana surged dangerously from resonance spikes
  • Encountered Choir Priest (defeated by him)
Session 7:
  • Entered Azerach's Silent Dominion
  • Experienced the Bell of Breath
  • Felt divine memory interference
Session 8:
  • Entered the 13-Hour Trial
  • Traded memory for Clockmaker's Touch
  • Witnessed Kael's paradox revelation
  • Witnessed Fursen's liberation
  • Connection to Aedwynn strengthened
Session 10:
  • Fought White Marcher
  • Scorched its shifting mass with fire magic
Session 13:
  • Sensed no divine presence in Terminus Ω-I
  • Recognized C-5 as Aedwynn-adjacent tech, not Sovereign-grown
Session 15:
  • Overloaded Custodian's sensory nodes with fire magic
  • Detonated its core
  • Facility "recognized" him through divine echo
Session 16:
  • Traveled to Dusthollow with the party
  • Present at The Dry Cup when Mira revealed Kael's true nature
  • Followed Kael after his breakdown
  • Shared his own existential crisis with Kael
  • Key words: "It doesn't matter. Because I'm here. Right now."
  • "A mistake who survived. The most stubborn kind."
Session 17 — The Witness:
  • Discovered Aedwynn the Maker's preserved body in the Chamber of the Final Breath
  • Touched the Maker's body and was recognized
  • Learned the Maker gave him a shard of divine essence when Sol died
  • Accepted the role of Witness — the Maker's heir
  • Gained: Maker's Memory, Breath of Creation, Divine Resistance, The Last Light
  • Witnessed Mira trigger Undying Conviction and destroy Kaleth Vorn
Session 18:
  • Entered Catacomb Reliquary with the party
  • Witnessed Mira Alethain's death — consumed by Savaros
  • Witnessed Kael's death — consumed by Savaros (but Kael killed Savaros)
  • Retrieved the Resonant Codex from beside Kael's skeleton
  • Experienced vision of Kael's journey through Vor'Kael
  • Found Kael's final entry; Codex transformed
  • Recovered Mira's wedding ring from the gore
  • Spoke at Kael's burial: "He was real because he chose to be."
  • Gave Oathkeeper to Seraphine Vale as a gesture of trust
  • Gold pouch stolen during Jonas's watch (30 gold lost)
Session 18+:
  • Entered the Verdant King's domain (The Blooming Rot)
  • Reached Sporehaven
Session 19 — The Maker's Memory Shared:
  • Traveled through the Blooming Rot: Sweetwater Marsh, Pilgrim's Spine, Hunger Garden
  • Failed WIS save at the Sweet Springs — compelled to drink despite being a skeleton; Jonas rescued him
  • Reached the Congregation Grove for the Questioning
  • Used Shared Memory to show Talaris Bloomrend a vision of Aedwynn the Maker
  • The vision spread through the spore network to the Verdant King himself
  • Talaris saw the Maker alive for the first time since the Usurpation
  • The Choir Blooms wept golden spores; the offering was accepted
  • Reached the Cave of Waiting in Verdant Pass
  • Placed Mira's wedding ring on her finger and positioned her hand to hold Elias's
  • Completed Mira's final quest — reunited her remains with her husband
  • Exited the Blooming Rot beyond Verdant Pass
---

Powers & Abilities

The Witness (Session 17)

Success
The Maker's Heir
"There you are. I've been waiting for you."

In the Chamber of the Final Breath, Sol touched the preserved body of Aedwynn the Maker. The dead god recognized him and granted him the role of Witness.

AbilityEffect
The Maker's MemoryComplete memory of the world before the Usurpation; can access through meditation
Shared MemoryCan share memories through touch (willing creatures only)
Breath of CreationCast Creation 1/long rest; objects are permanent
Divine ResistanceAdvantage on saves vs. Sovereign abilities; immune to Sovereign control effects
The Last Light30 ft. bright light as action; Sovereign creatures have disadvantage; CHA mod times/long rest

Clockmaker's Touch (Session 8)

Gained by trading a personal memory during the 13-Hour Trial. Allows manipulation of time-adjacent phenomena. Full extent still being discovered.

Confirmed Abilities

Fire Magic:

  • Primary magical damage dealer
  • Superheats targets
  • Scorches and burns enemies
  • Can overload sensory systems
Clockmaker's Touch:
  • Temporal ability gained from memory trade
  • Allows manipulation of time-adjacent phenomena
  • Full extent still being discovered
Divine Resonance:
  • Can sense divine memory leaking
  • Detects Aedwynn-adjacent technology
  • Perceives the Maker's architecture
  • Natural attunement to pre-Sovereign structures

Combat Role

  • Primary magical damage dealer
  • Fire magic specialist
  • Resonance manipulation
  • Divine-tech interface
  • Stabilization of collapsing structures
---

Significance

To the Sovereigns

Sol Raven represents:

  • A threat — A piece of the god they murdered still exists
  • An opportunity — Access to the Maker's power
  • A resource — Divine essence to be claimed or consumed
  • A target — Must be controlled or destroyed

To the World

Sol Raven may be:

  • A fragment of Aedwynn trying to reconstitute itself
  • The key to undoing the Usurpation
  • Proof the Maker is not entirely dead
  • A beacon drawing other Aedwynn-Born

To the Party

Sol is proof that:

  • The Maker is not entirely gone
  • Divine power predating the Sovereigns still exists
  • Resistance to Sovereign authority is possible
  • Pre-Sovereign knowledge can be accessed
---

Progression of Connection

Session 0-1: Initial awareness of divine memory Session 1: Sensed resonance in corrupted church Session 8: Gained Clockmaker's Touch, connection deepened Session 13-15: C-5 facility recognized him as Aedwynn-adjacent

Pattern: The connection strengthens with each encounter with pre-Sovereign structures or divine fragments.

---

Danger Level

EXTREME

If the Sovereigns discover the true nature of Sol's connection:

  • Azerach would want to hear his forbidden truths
  • Imhuran would judge him as existing outside proper time
  • Mareatha would attempt arterial baptism to claim divine blood
  • Vor'Kael would try to digest the fragment of meaning
  • All Thirteen would see him as either prey or tool
---

Current Questions

About Sol:

  • What memory did he trade for Clockmaker's Touch?
  • How far does his connection to Aedwynn extend?
  • Can he channel more of the Maker's power?
  • Is he being drawn toward something?
About Aedwynn:
  • Is the Maker trying to reconstitute through Sol?
  • Are there other Aedwynn-Born?
  • What happens if Sol fully embraces the connection?
  • Can the Maker be resurrected?
---

Current Status

Current Location: Beyond Verdant Pass (Exited the Blooming Rot)

Role: The Maker's Witness — heir to Aedwynn's memory

Items Carried:

  • The Resonant Codex (transformed, attuned)
  • Standard equipment
Items Delivered (Session 19):
  • Mira's Wedding Ring — placed on her finger in the Cave of Waiting
Active Abilities:
  • Clockmaker's Touch (temporal manipulation)
  • The Witness powers (Maker's Memory, Breath of Creation, Divine Resistance, The Last Light)
  • Shared Memory (used on Talaris Bloomrend — Session 19)
  • Kael's Echo (through the Codex)
Session 19 Significance: Sol used the Shared Memory ability to show Talaris Bloomrend — a Sovereign who helped murder Aedwynn — a vision of the Maker alive. This was the first time Talaris had seen her face since the Usurpation. The vision spread through the entire spore network of the Blooming Rot. The offering bought passage to Verdant Pass and softened the consequences of the party's use of fire in Talaris's domain.

The question has been answered: Sol Raven is what happens when a god refuses to stay dead — and chooses an heir to carry her memory forward.

---

Related

  • Aedwynn the Maker
  • The Party
  • The Thirteen Sovereigns
  • The 13-Hour Trial
  • Talaris Bloomrend the Verdant King
  • The Resonant Codex
  • Kael
  • Mira Alethain
  • Seraphine Vale
  • Campaign Sessions
---

Sol Raven: The Maker's Witness. He carries the memory of a murdered god, the echo of an impossible boy, and the wedding ring of a two-thousand-year-old saint. The weight of all that meaning should be crushing. Instead, it feels like purpose.

items.title: The Party

frontmatter.current_location: Beyond Verdant Pass (Exited the Blooming Rot)

items.content:
The Party

Abstract
Party Overview
Three adventurers who have survived encounters with multiple Sovereign fragments, endured the 13-Hour Trial, been branded by two different Sovereigns, discovered pre-Sovereign secrets buried beneath the world, lost two companions to an ancient Skin-Walker, and completed a quest to reunite a two-thousand-year-old saint with her husband's remains.

Info
Party Composition
Player Characters: Jonas, Sol Raven, Fursen
NPC Companions: None (Kael — deceased Session 18)
Allies Lost: Mira Alethain (deceased Session 18, laid to rest Session 19)

---

Party Members

CharacterClassLevelStatusKey Feature
JonasFighter (Eldritch Knight)9ActiveThe Bleeding Blade (cursed), Azerach's brand, marked by Talaris
Sol RavenSorcerer9ActiveThe Maker's Witness, Resonant Codex, shared Aedwynn's memory with Talaris
FursenMonk (Open Hand)9ActiveLeonin names, ✅ completed Mira's quest

Fallen Companions

CharacterClassLevelStatusFate
~~Kael~~Resonant Theorist9☠️ DEADConsumed by Savaros, buried behind Church of the Merciful Vein
~~Mira Alethain~~Sanctified Champion20☠️ DEADConsumed by Savaros, ✅ laid to rest beside Elias (Session 19)

Tip
Full Details
See individual character files for complete backstories, abilities, and session histories.

---

Current Situation (Session 19)

Location: Beyond Verdant Pass — Exited the Blooming Rot Level: 9 ✅ Mission Complete: Mira's remains reunited with Elias's body at the Cave of Waiting

Session 19 Summary

The party traveled through Talaris Bloomrend the Verdant King's domain:

  • Sweetwater Marsh — Sol nearly succumbed to the Sweet Springs
  • Pilgrim's Spine — Burned curious Bloom Hounds (forest angered)
  • Hunger Garden — Killed Thornback Behemoth with fire (Talaris spoke directly)
  • Congregation Grove — Faced the Questioning; Sol shared Aedwynn's memory with Talaris
  • Verdant Pass (The Wound) — Laid Mira's bones beside Elias's skeleton

Key Session 19 Events

EventSignificance
Sol shared Aedwynn's memoryTalaris saw the Maker alive for first time since Usurpation
Jonas lied to Choir BloomLie detected; marked as "fire-bringer"
Learned Talaris/Azerach historyThey were colleagues; haven't spoken in 800 years
Jonas's brand notedTalaris now "curious" about Jonas
Mira laid to restWedding ring placed on her finger; quest complete

Party Status

CharacterCondition
JonasBleeding Blade needs feeding tomorrow; Azerach's brand noted by Talaris
Sol RavenShared Aedwynn's memory; delivered Mira's ring
FursenQuest complete; learned 4 other Leonin in temporal cells

⚠️ Critical: The Confluence Has Occurred

While the party was in the Blooming Rot, The Confluence happened in Veinspire.

The party has no information about what occurred. Seraphine Vale is their only contact in the city. Whatever the Cardinal planned has already happened.

---

Current Inventory

ItemCarrierNotes
The Bleeding BladeJonas (attuned)Legendary longsword, CURSED — must feed daily
The Resonant CodexSol Raven (attuned)Contains Kael's Echo
Savaros's LedgerParty15 centuries of intelligence
Heretic's SilencePartySuppression collar
Truthseeker's WandPartyInterrogation tool
Bottled AbsencePartySingle-use void darkness
The Confessor's GlassPartyMirror revealing true selves
The Flayer's TouchPartyFace-harvesting gloves
~970 gold piecesParty(30 stolen during rest in The Dregs)

Items Delivered (Session 19)

ItemDelivered ToLocation
Mira's Wedding RingMira's skeletonCave of Waiting, Verdant Pass
Mira's RemainsBeside EliasCave of Waiting, Verdant Pass

Items Given Away

ItemGiven ToReason
OathkeeperSeraphine ValeSol Raven gave Mira's greatsword as gesture of trust
---

Party Dynamics

Shared Trait: Each member is connected to Sovereign power in dangerous ways:

  • Jonas: Marked by Azerach the Whispered King (hand brand active)
  • Sol Raven: Linked to Aedwynn the Maker, now his Witness
  • Fursen: Escaped Imhuran the Regent of Hours's eternal prison, carries 10,000 Leonin names
Formation Timeline:
  • Sessions 0-1: Sol + Jonas
  • Session 2: Kael joins
  • Session 8: Fursen freed and joins
  • Session 18: Kael dies, Mira dies
Status: Fugitives from the Mareathan Inquisition, now in the Verdant King's domain

---

☠️ Session 18 Casualties

Mira Alethain — DEAD

Danger
Consumed by Savaros
Level 20 Sanctified Champion — Two thousand years of conviction, ended in moments.

Mira's flesh was ripped from her bones by Savaros the High Keeper in the Catacomb Reliquary. Her skeleton remains — carried by Fursen in a barrel.

What Remains: Her wedding ring (Sol), her bones (Fursen) What Was Lost Forever: The location of the Arterial Texts, the exact location of Elias's body

Kael — DEAD

Danger
Consumed by Savaros — But He Killed the Monster
Ontological Anomaly — The concept that refused to be destroyed.

Kael was torn apart by Savaros moments after Mira. But when Savaros consumed Kael's flesh, he sealed his own fate.

Kael was an ontological anomaly — a concept that Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress herself couldn't digest. She had vomited him out because his nature was fundamentally incompatible with consumption. Savaros, a mere Skin-Walker, had no hope of surviving what a Sovereign couldn't stomach.

The moment Savaros tried to digest Kael's flesh, he began to convulse. His stolen faces contorted in agony. His form unraveled — poisoned by consuming something that refused to be consumed.

Kael saved the party in death.

The boy who spent his existence wondering if he was real... was real enough to kill a fifteen-century-old monster.

What Remains: His echo in the Resonant Codex (Sol can hear his voice), his grave (unmarked, behind Church of the Merciful Vein)

---

Major Accomplishments

Sovereigns Encountered

1. Azerach the Whispered King — Session 7 (Jonas branded) 2. Imhuran the Regent of Hours — Session 8 (13-Hour Trial survived) 3. Talaris Bloomrend the Verdant King — Session 18+ (currently in his domain)

Key Events

  • Survived the 13-Hour Trial (Session 8)
  • Freed Fursen from temporal imprisonment
  • Discovered Aedwynn the Maker's preserved body (Session 17)
  • Received gifts from the Maker (Session 17)
  • Defeated the Misremembered (Session 16)
  • Killed Kaleth Vorn (Session 17)
  • Killed Savaros the High Keeper (Session 18) — via Kael's indigestible nature
  • Lost Mira Alethain and Kael (Session 18)
  • Traversed the Blooming Rot (Session 19)
  • Sol shared Aedwynn's memory with Talaris Bloomrend (Session 19)
  • ✅ Completed Mira's Quest — reunited her remains with Elias (Session 19)

Session 17 Gifts from the Maker

CharacterGiftEffect
Sol RavenThe WitnessMaker's Memory, Divine Resistance, Breath of Creation
FursenThe NamesComplete Leonin ancestral memory, Imhuran's Blind Spot
JonasRefusedChose to carry Azerach's brand alone
KaelNoneDid not touch the Maker's body
---

Active Threats

ThreatStatus
The Confluence✅ HAS OCCURRED — party missed it; consequences unknown
Ultharion the Obsidian ShepherdAlerted to Savaros's death via Shadow Tether; asking about Leonin records
The CardinalStill active, Savaros's superior; Confluence plans executed
Talaris Bloomrend"Curious" about Jonas; softened by Sol's offering but not allied
Seraphine ValeFaith shattered; has Oathkeeper; cover status unknown post-Confluence
The MisrememberedWill return (weeks/months)
---

The Weight They Carry

Jonas carries:

  • A cursed blade that demands blood daily
  • A god's brand on his hand
  • The memory of everyone he couldn't save
Sol carries:
  • A dead god's memory (and shared it with a Sovereign)
  • An impossible boy's echo in a book
  • The knowledge that a Sovereign wept when shown his creator
Fursen carries:
  • Ten thousand Leonin names given to him by the Maker
  • The knowledge that four of his people still live (in temporal cells)
  • The satisfaction of completing a two-thousand-year-old saint's final wish
---

Historical Significance

This is Era IX — The Present Era, described as "the era where the world begins to wake."

The party represents something unprecedented:

  • Mortals who survived divine judgment designed to eliminate them
  • Living proof that Sovereign power can be resisted
  • Discoverers of pre-Sovereign civilization
  • Carriers of impossible traits (divine connection, temporal escape, multiple divine marks)
  • Killers of a 1,500-year-old Skin-Walker who infiltrated Mareatha's Church
---

Three survivors who just completed a dead woman's final wish.
Two allies dead — one buried, one at rest with her husband.
One Sovereign shown his creator's face for the first time in two millennia.
The Confluence has happened. The world has changed.
And the party doesn't know what they're walking back into.
> This is the Ashen Realms.
This is what it costs to do the right thing.

---

Related

  • Campaign Index - The Ashen Realms
  • Campaign Sessions
  • The Thirteen Sovereigns
  • The 13-Hour Trial
  • Mira Alethain
  • Kael
  • Savaros the High Keeper
  • Sporehaven

items.title: Elias Alethain
items.content:
Elias Alethain

---

Overview

Success
The Healer Who Waited — DECEASED (Reunited Session 19)
True Name: Elias Alethain
Role: Healer, Husband to Mira Alethain
Status: ☠️ DECEASED — Died 22 years ago at Verdant Pass
Final Rest: ✅ Reunited with Mira (Session 19)

---

Quick Reference

AttributeValue
ProfessionHealer
AlignmentNeutral Good
Death~22 years before Session 19
Cause of DeathPushed from cliff by Savaros the High Keeper (staged as accident)
Final LocationCave of Waiting, Verdant Pass
SpouseMira Alethain
---

The Story

Who He Was

Elias Alethain was a healer — a man who dedicated his life to helping others. He married Mira, a Sanctified Champion who was two thousand years his elder, and they spent decades together.

When Mira fled the Mareathan Inquisition as one of the Seven Heretics, Elias fled with her. They went into hiding together, using assumed names, always moving.

His Death

Eight years into their hiding, Savaros the High Keeper — the ancient Skin-Walker who had infiltrated Mareatha's Church for fifteen centuries — found Elias near Verdant Pass.

Savaros pushed him from a cliff. The fall was staged to look like an accident. But Elias didn't die from the fall.

He landed on a ledge. And then something remarkable happened.

Savaros watched from above as Elias — broken, bleeding, dying — refused to give up. With every ounce of life force and vitality he had left, Elias crawled. Not begging for mercy. Not calling for help. Just moving. Dragging himself across stone toward a crevice in the rock face.

Talaris Bloomrend was watching too.

The Verdant King, sovereign of decay and renewal, witnessed a mortal resist the cycle with such raw, desperate tenacity that he was impressed. Here was a life form that should have surrendered to death — and instead chose to fight for every remaining second.

Talaris made a choice: he would not claim this one. The death was "staged" — forced by outside interference, not a natural ending. The cycle had no proper claim to someone murdered before their time. And this man's defiance had earned the King's respect.

Savaros could not finish the job. Overstaying in Talaris's domain would be a death sentence even for a fifteen-century-old Skin-Walker. He watched Elias crawl into the cave, then withdrew — assuming the man would die soon enough.

Elias made it to the Cave of Waiting. And there, in the one place where Talaris's power cannot reach — the place the King chose to leave untouched — he eventually died.

He died alone. His last thoughts were of Mira.

He never blamed her.

The Cave of Waiting

Verdant Pass — called "The Wound" by the inhabitants of the Blooming Rot — is an anomaly. When Talaris claimed his territory, something in this ravine resisted. The cut healed around it but never consumed it.

In the Cave of Waiting:

  • Nothing decays
  • Time has no meaning
  • Talaris's power cannot reach
  • The dead remain pristine
Elias's skeleton lay on bare stone for twenty-two years. No rot. No fungal growth. No reclamation. Just clean, white bones — hands folded across his chest.

He'd been waiting.

---

✅ REUNION — SESSION 19

Success
Together at Last
Date: Session 19
Location: Cave of Waiting, Verdant Pass
Completed by: Fursen, Sol Raven, Jonas

The party carried Mira's remains through the Blooming Rot — past the Sweetwater Marsh, across the Pilgrim's Spine, through the Hunger Garden, past the Congregation Grove. They earned passage from the Verdant King through Sol's unprecedented offering: showing Talaris a vision of Aedwynn the Maker alive.

In the Cave of Waiting, they found Elias exactly as he'd been for twenty-two years. Pristine. Peaceful. Waiting.

Fursen laid Mira's bones beside him.

Sol placed her wedding ring on her finger and moved her hand until her fingers rested against Elias's.

Two skeletons now rest in the Cave of Waiting, holding hands in a place where nothing rots.

🦁 "I am sorry we could not reunite you in life. But at least in death, we have brought you together."
— Fursen

What Mira Never Knew

When the Maker's preserved body spoke to Mira in Session 17, it revealed the truth:

  • Elias didn't die from the fall
  • He crawled into the cave and waited for rescue
  • His last thoughts were of Mira
  • He never blamed her
Mira knew exactly where to find him. She was going to retrieve his body after helping the party in Veinspire.

She died before she could.

The party completed her journey for her.

---

Savaros's Observation

Savaros the High Keeper watched Elias crawl. He expected the man to die quickly. Instead, he witnessed something that even a fifteen-century-old monster found... noteworthy.

Elias didn't beg. Didn't cry out. Just crawled. Inch by inch. Dragging himself toward that cave with a tenacity that bordered on absurd.

Savaros couldn't stay to finish the job — Talaris's domain was dangerous even for him. So he left, assuming the healer would bleed out within the hour.

He was wrong. Elias made it to the cave. And Talaris, impressed by what he witnessed, let him die on his own terms.

Savaros is dead now — killed by consuming Kael, an ontological anomaly he couldn't digest. The monster who pushed Elias never knew the healer had made it to shelter, never knew Talaris had respected the dying man's defiance.

---

Legacy

Elias Alethain was an ordinary man who loved an extraordinary woman. He followed her into exile. He died because of her enemies. He waited for her for twenty-two years.

Now they rest together. Husband and wife. Healer and saint. Their bones hold hands in a place where nothing rots and nothing changes.

Fifty-two years of separation. Finally ended.

---

Related

  • Mira Alethain
  • Verdant Pass
  • Savaros the High Keeper
  • Talaris Bloomrend the Verdant King
  • Campaign Sessions
  • The Party
---

"I am sorry we could not reunite you in life. But at least in death, we have brought you together."

items.title: Garrett the Smith
items.content:
Garrett the Smith

---

Overview

Info
The Discreet Craftsman
Profession: Smith
Location: The Whetstone and Anvil, Lesser Sacral District, Veinspire
Role: Contact, Equipment Source, Information
First Appearance: Session 18

---

Quick Reference

AttributeValue
AgeMid-fifties
OccupationSmith (32 years experience)
ShopThe Whetstone and Anvil
DistrictLesser Sacral District
DispositionHelpful to those Seraphine trusts
---

Description

A smith in his fifties with thick arms and burn scars. Hands that have shaped steel for decades. Four generations of metalwork in his family.

Doesn't ask questions. Doesn't talk to the Church.

---

Connection to Party

Seraphine Vale directed the party to Garrett:

"The Whetstone and Anvil. Three blocks east. Garrett's place — he doesn't ask questions and he doesn't talk to the Church."

Garrett already knew they were coming:

"She came by this morning. Told me if anyone showed up asking for shelter, I should give it. Said she might be about to do something stupid."

---

Services

Smithing

  • Basic weapon repair/maintenance
  • Armor repair (patching, not full restoration)
  • Custom work (limited by time)
  • Assessment of unusual materials

The Custodian Armplate

  • Tested the pre-Sovereign metal for Jonas
  • Metal takes heat, but slowly
  • His forge isn't hot enough to shape it properly
  • Needs divine-fire heat or equivalent
  • Offered to trade the armplate toward the Underways Cuirass if it's workable

Information

  • Knows the district well
  • Shared legends about mythical weapons and armor
  • Twenty years ago found pre-Sovereign gear in the Underways
---

Inventory (Session 18)

Helmets

ItemPropertiesPrice
Steel Half-HelmStandard15 gp
Reinforced CoifComfortable for long wear20 gp
Closed HelmFull coverage30 gp
Tribunal Helm (dented)Recognizable25 gp
Watchman's Casque+1 AC, cannot be surprised, advantage Initiative, +2 AC reaction 1/LR150 gp

Boots

ItemPropertiesPrice
Courier's Boots+5 ft speed50 gp
Tribunal Sabatons+1 AC with heavy armor, disadvantage Stealth40 gp
Underway TreadsAdvantage vs prone, ignore difficult terrain (stone/rubble)75 gp
Dead Man's WalkAdvantage Stealth, silent footsteps, bonus action dash 1/LR175 gp

Armor

ItemACPropertiesPrice
Chain Shirt13 + DEX (max 2)Standard50 gp
Scale Mail14 + DEX (max 2)Stealth disadvantage50 gp
Reinforced Hide13 + DEX (max 2)No Stealth disadvantage35 gp
Inquisitor's Half-Chain14 + DEX (max 2)Recognizable, Stealth disadvantage65 gp
Underways Cuirass15 + DEX (max 2)No Stealth disadvantage, half weight400 gp

Longswords

ItemPropertiesPrice
Standard LongswordNone15 gp
Tribunal ConfiscateSilver inlay (useful vs werewolves/fiends)25 gp
BleederCrit: +1d4 slashing at start of target's turn30 gp
Pallid Steel Blade+1 damage, never needs sharpening75 gp
Oathbreaker's Edge+1, +1d6 necrotic vs Sovereign servants, reroll 1s 1/LR, won't cut for Sovereign servants200 gp

The Underways Set

Found on a corpse twenty years ago in the Underways. Pre-Sovereign make.
  • Watchman's Casque
  • Dead Man's Walk
  • Underways Cuirass
Set price for Casque + Boots: 300 gp even

---

Legends Shared

The Martyr's Gauntlet

  • Supposed to be in Grand Cathedral vault
  • Pedestal is empty — has been for decades/centuries
  • Theories: lost, with Savaros, or "walked away on its own"
  • Protects from lies/illusions

The Bleeding Blade

  • Deep catacombs, below Tribunal operations
  • Made from crystallized divine blood
  • Wounds never stop bleeding
  • Area unmapped, people don't return

The Underways Warden Armor

  • Pre-Sovereign full plate
  • Garrett saw it standing empty in the tunnels
  • When he returned a week later — gone
  • "Like it walked away on its own"

The Silenced Knight's Armor

  • Knight supposedly killed a Sovereign permanently
  • Imhuran erased the event from history
  • Armor absorbed divine power
  • Sealed in Tribunal's deepest prison
  • Church says it's heresy
---

Related

  • Seraphine Vale
  • Lesser Sacral District
  • The Martyr's Gauntlet
  • Veinspire
---

"Come back when you find a hotter forge. Or when the world stops trying to kill you. Whichever comes first." — Garrett
