items.title: Mira Alethain
items.content:
Mira Alethain

---

Overview

Danger
The Last Heretic — DECEASED
True Name: Mira Alethain (née unknown — she took her husband's surname)
Known As: Mira Hemm (alias used for 22 years)
Role: Former Paladin of Mareatha, Heretic, Last Survivor of the Seven
Status: ☠️ DECEASED — Consumed by Savaros the High Keeper (Session 18)
Level: 20 (Sanctified Champion — Paladin/Cleric Hybrid)

---

Quick Reference

AttributeValue
ClassSanctified Champion 20 (Paladin/Cleric Hybrid)
AlignmentNeutral Good
Age at Death~2,250+ years (appeared 50s)
Final LocationCatacomb Reliquary, Veinspire
Cause of DeathConsumed by Savaros the High Keeper
RemainsSkeleton in barrel, carried by Fursen
HusbandElias Alethain (deceased — murdered Year 8 of her hiding)
---

☠️ DEATH — SESSION 18

Danger
The End of Two Thousand Years
> Date of Death: Session 18
Location: Catacomb Reliquary, Veinspire
Cause: Consumed by Savaros the High Keeper
Remains: Skeleton in a barrel, carried by Fursen

How She Died

In the Catacomb Reliquary beneath Veinspire, Mira Alethain faced Savaros — the ancient Skin-Walker who had infiltrated Mareatha's Church for fifteen centuries.

Despite her Level 20 power, three levels of exhaustion from triggering Undying Conviction against Kaleth Vorn left her vulnerable. Savaros was a Skin-Walker — an entity that steals identities by consuming flesh.

Her flesh was ripped from her bones.

Savaros consumed her flesh, adding her to his collection of stolen identities. Her skeleton collapsed to the cold stone floor, stripped clean. Her wedding ring — the ring Elias gave her — remained in the gore. Sol Raven retrieved it.

This is the same method Savaros used on Kael moments later. But while Mira's flesh was digestible, Kael's flesh killed Savaros — an ontological anomaly that even Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress couldn't digest.

Two thousand years of conviction. Thirty years of hiding. Six friends murdered. A husband she never got to bury. It ended on cold stone in a monster's lair.

The Tragic Irony

Mira Alethain — Level 20 Sanctified Champion, two thousand years old, survivor of the Usurpation — was consumed by a monster.

And that monster was then killed by a boy who wasn't sure he was real.

She died first. Kael died second. But Kael's death was the one that ended Savaros.

What Remains

ItemStatusCurrent Holder
Wedding Ring✅ Placed on her fingerCave of Waiting, Verdant Pass
Oathkeeper (greatsword)Given awaySeraphine Vale (via Sol Raven)
Skeleton✅ Laid to rest beside EliasCave of Waiting, Verdant Pass
Conviction's Mantle (+2 Plate)Destroyed with flesh—
Healer's SatchelDestroyed—
Knowledge of the Arterial Texts' locationLOST FOREVER—

What Was Lost Forever

  • The Arterial Texts: Only Mira knew their location. That knowledge died with her.
  • Two Thousand Years of Memory: Everything she witnessed — the Usurpation, the old world, the rise of the Sovereigns.

✅ FINAL REST — SESSION 19

Success
Reunited at Last
Location: The Cave of Waiting, Verdant Pass
Date: Session 19
Completed by: Jonas, Sol Raven, Fursen

The party carried Mira's remains through the Blooming Rot — past the Sweetwater Marsh, across the Pilgrim's Spine, through the Hunger Garden, and past the Congregation Grove. They faced the Verdant King's questioning and earned passage to the Wound.

In the Cave of Waiting, they found Elias Alethain — his skeleton pristine after twenty-two years in the one place Talaris cannot reach.

Fursen laid Mira's bones beside her husband. Sol placed her wedding ring on her finger and moved her hand until her fingers rested against Elias's.

🦁 "I am sorry we could not reunite you in life. But at least in death, we have brought you together."
— Fursen

Two skeletons now rest in the Cave of Waiting, holding hands in a place where nothing rots. Husband and wife. Healer and saint. Fifty-two years of separation, finally ended.

"Elias waited twenty-two years. He can wait a little longer."

Those were among her last words. He doesn't have to wait anymore.

Legacy

"Aldric. Elias. Corwin. Daven. Bram. Adelaide."
> Now add one more name to the litany:
> Mira.

The Seven Heretics are all dead.

The party carries her bones, her ring. They will remember.

She died fighting beside people she'd known for less than a week, in a hole beneath a church that had hunted her for thirty years, trying to stop a conspiracy she'd only begun to understand.

She died as she lived: refusing to run.

The Bleeding Blade remembers her. It felt her die.

---

Portrait

Portrait
Physical Appearance
A weathered woman in her apparent fifties, though her true age stretched back before the Sovereigns' ascension — she witnessed the Usurpation itself. Grey-streaked dark hair pulled back severely. Sharp, assessing eyes that had seen too much death. Hands that healed but remembered how to kill. She moved with a healer's gentleness and a warrior's readiness — never quite one or the other.
> A faded scar ran along her left forearm — a reminder of when she cut the Mareathan brand from her own flesh.
> She always wore her wedding ring.

---

Backstory

Before the Sovereigns

Mira was alive before the Usurpation — before the Thirteen murdered Aedwynn and claimed divine power. She witnessed the old world, walked under true divine light, and watched thirteen mortals butcher a god and reshape reality around their stolen organs.

When the Sovereigns ascended, Mira chose to serve Mareatha — believing the new order was necessary, or at least unavoidable. For over two millennia, she devoted herself to the Vein-Mother's church, rising to become one of its most skilled and respected healers. At a certain point of power and conviction, her aging simply... stopped. She willed herself to continue, and her body obeyed.

Her oath was simple: Truth. Justice. Protection of the Faithful.

She believed in it completely.

The Discovery

Thirty years ago (a mere moment in her long existence), Mira and six other priests discovered something that shattered her faith:

The Sanguine Doctrine was a lie.

Deep in the Arterial Archives, they found the Arterial Texts — Mareatha's own writings from before the Usurpation. Her mortal words. Her doubts. Her failures. Her uncertainty about whether killing Aedwynn was right.

The Texts proved:

  • Mareatha was fallible before divinity
  • The Sanguine Doctrine was written by mortals AFTER the Usurpation
  • The Church's foundation was political consolidation, not divine mandate
  • The Vein-Mother herself had questioned the murder of the Maker

The Seven

The seven priests who discovered this truth:

NameRoleFate
Aldric SarneTheologian — discovered the inconsistencies☠️ Killed, Year 3
Elias AlethainHealer — Mira's husband☠️ Killed, Year 8
Corwin MarshScholar — translated pre-Usurpation texts☠️ Killed, Year 12
Daven HoltChronicler — documented their findings☠️ Killed, Year 17
Bram AshfordWarrior-Priest — protected the group☠️ Killed, Year 21
Adelaide DunneSeer — received contradicting visions☠️ Killed, Year 26
Mira AlethainPaladin — the last survivor☠️ Killed, Session 18
They tried to reform the Church from within. They were branded heretics and hunted. They fled Veinspire and scattered.

For thirty years, Mira believed they had simply disappeared. Bad luck. Dangerous roads. The cruelty of time. She didn't know the Tribunal had systematically murdered every single one of them — Kaleth Vorn killed five; Savaros killed Elias.

Elias

Mira married Elias Alethain during their flight from Veinspire — finding love in the midst of being hunted. Despite her ancient age, she had never truly loved before him. She took his name.

In Year 8, Elias traveled near Verdant Pass. He never came back. Word reached Mira that he'd died in the mountain passes — a travel accident, they said. His body was never found.

The truth: Savaros the High Keeper found Elias and pushed him from a cliff. Savaros watched as Elias — with extraordinary tenacity — crawled toward a cave in the rock face. Talaris, impressed by Elias's refusal to surrender to death, chose not to claim him. Savaros couldn't finish the job without risking Talaris's wrath, so he withdrew. Elias made it to the cave and eventually died there, alone.

Mira grieved a husband lost to bad roads and worse luck. She moved to Pallid Crossroads. She kept healing. She kept his name.

For twenty-two years, she believed it was an accident.

Then the Maker revealed the truth in Session 17.

The Oathbreaker

When Mira realized the Church she had served for millennia was built on lies she had witnessed being constructed, she faced an impossible choice:

  • Blind obedience to a corrupted institution
  • Truth at the cost of everything she believed
She chose truth.

Her oath to the Church shattered. But her oath to truth — to protecting the innocent, to justice — that remained.

She was not a fallen Oathbreaker in the corrupted sense. She broke her oath to an institution that betrayed its own principles. Her divine power flowed from pure conviction — her unshakeable belief in truth itself. The raw force of a will that refused to break across two thousand years.

The Church called this heresy. She called it the only honest path left.

Thirty Years of Hiding

For three decades, Mira lived as "Mira Hemm" — a healer in Pallid Crossroads. She:

  • Delivered children
  • Buried parents
  • Taught medicine to the next generation
  • Watched her friends disappear one by one (not knowing they were murdered)
  • Never stopped being hunted
She knew they would find her eventually. She just hoped to outlast them out of spite first.

They found her anyway.

---

Personality & Motivations

Core Traits

  • Pragmatic survivor — two thousand years taught harsh lessons
  • Fierce protector — would die for those she considered hers
  • Unshakeable conviction — her faith in truth could not be broken
  • Controlled fury — millennia of grief compressed into cold purpose
  • Widow — she lost her husband to what she thought was bad luck; she learned it was murder

What She Wanted

1. Kill Kaleth Vorn — the Tribunal Hand who hunted the Seven and murdered five of her friends ✓ COMPLETE 2. Reunite with Elias — lay her bones beside her husband's ✓ COMPLETE (by the party) 3. Protect the Arterial Texts — the truth must survive (location now lost) 4. See the party survive — they reminded her why she kept fighting

What She Feared

  • That she'd led four young people to their deaths (like the other six)
  • That the Texts would be destroyed and the truth would die with her
  • That she'd become the monster the Church already thought she was

Quotes

"Aldric. Elias. Corwin. Daven. Bram. Adelaide. I am still here."

"I broke my oath to an institution. I never broke my oath to what mattered."

"Down here, pride is a luxury. Precision keeps you alive."

"Seraphine Vale? She's a stepping stone. Kaleth Vorn is who I'm here for."

"Twenty-two years. I thought Elias died to a mountain road. Twenty-two years."

"I watched them kill the Maker. I watched them build their churches on his corpse. I served them for two thousand years. And they lied about all of it."

---

Relationships

With the Party

CharacterRelationship
Sol RavenRecognized something ancient in him. Watched him carefully. "You're connected to something older than the Sovereigns. Be careful what that means."
JonasRespected his directness, frustrated by his recklessness. "Defiance without wisdom gets people killed."
FursenGentle with him — recognized someone still adapting to freedom after imprisonment.
KaelStudied him longest. Knew what he was. Told him the truth even though it hurt. "You're something else entirely."

With Antagonists

CharacterRelationship
Kaleth VornPrimary target. The Tribunal Hand who coordinated the hunt and murdered five of her friends. (Savaros killed Elias.) She ended him. ✓
Seraphine ValeSecondary obstacle. Level 12 — posed no real threat to Mira. "She's dangerous to you. Not to me."
The TribunalThe enforcement arm of the Sanguine Doctrine. Hunted her for 30 years.

With the Sovereigns

SovereignRelationship
MareathaFormer deity. Mira still respected what Mareatha was — but not what the Church became.
The ThirteenKnew them when they were mortal. Watched them murder Aedwynn. Never forgot.
---

The Two Auras

Info
Mareathan Power vs True Conviction
> Mira carried two sources of power, visible as two distinct auras to those with divine sight or magical perception.
> ### The Red Aura (Mareathan Divine Power)
> Despite breaking her oath to the Church, Mira retained divine power from her two millennia of service to Mareatha the Vein-Mother. This manifested as a crimson aura — the color of arterial blood.
> Properties:
- Normally subtle, barely visible even to magical detection
- Grew more intense as she approached Mareathan holy sites
- Near the Catacomb Gate, she blazed with crimson light visible to mundane sight
- This was the source of her Cleric/Paladin spellcasting, Lay on Hands, and divine auras
- Could be drained by Tribunal artifacts like the Sanguine Siphon
> In Session 17, Kaleth Vorn's Sanguine Siphon successfully drained this aura. The red light was gone. She appeared "empty" to divine sight.
> ### The Orange Aura (Undying Conviction)
> Mira's true power — her Undying Conviction — had nothing to do with Mareatha or any Sovereign. It was entirely hers, forged from:
- Two thousand years of refusing to break
- Thirty years of grief compressed into purpose
- Six names burned into her soul
- Love that outlasted death and betrayal
> This manifested as an orange-gold glow — warm like sunrise, terrible like wildfire.
> Properties:
- Invisible until Undying Conviction activated
- Could not be drained, suppressed, or stolen by any divine artifact
- Was not divine power — it was mortal will made manifest
- The Sanguine Siphon could not touch it because there was nothing to siphon
- Represented everything she was, not everything she was given
> ### The Dramatic Irony
> Kaleth Vorn believed the red aura was her power. He built the Sanguine Siphon specifically to drain it. He succeeded. And then he learned that the red light was never what made her dangerous.
> She rose in Undying Conviction and annihilated him.

---

Seraphine Vale

Info
The Child She Delivered
> Thirty-four years ago, Mira Alethain — still a devoted servant of Mareatha — delivered Seraphine Vale into the world. Difficult birth. Frightened mother. A baby who cried loud enough to echo through the temple halls.
> Mira blessed that child in Mareatha's name. She believed it then.
> Now that child is High Inquisitor. That child hunted her as a heretic.
> ### Mira's Feelings
> Mira did not hate Seraphine. She couldn't. When she looked at the High Inquisitor, she saw:
- A victim of the same lies she once believed
- A child raised without the chance to question
- Herself, two thousand years ago, full of certainty and faith
- Someone who deserved the truth, not death
> ### What Happened
> The party encountered Seraphine in Session 17. Rather than fight, they revealed truths that shook her faith. Sol Raven later gave Seraphine Mira's sword, Oathkeeper — a gesture of trust that Mira herself might have approved of.

---

D&D 5e Statistics

Example
Full Stat Block (Sanctified Champion 20)
> ### Mira Alethain
Medium humanoid (human), neutral good
> ---
> Armor Class 22 (+2 plate armor, +2 from conviction aura)
Hit Points 284 (20d12 + 140)
Speed 30 ft.
> ---
> | STR | DEX | CON | INT | WIS | CHA |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| 22 (+6) | 14 (+2) | 24 (+7) | 16 (+3) | 22 (+6) | 24 (+7) |
> ---
> Saving Throws All saves +6 (Aura of Protection adds CHA to all saves)
- STR +12, DEX +8, CON +13, INT +9, WIS +12, CHA +13
Skills Medicine +12, Insight +12, Persuasion +13, Religion +9, Athletics +12, History +9, Perception +12
Damage Resistances Radiant, Necrotic; Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing from nonmagical attacks
Condition Immunities Disease, Frightened, Charmed (while conscious)
Senses Truesight 30 ft., passive Perception 22
Languages Common, Celestial, Abyssal, Primordial (pre-Usurpation tongue), Infernal
Challenge 21 (33,000 XP) — or CR 23 during Undying Conviction
> ---
> ### Legendary Resistance (3/Day)
> If Mira failed a saving throw, she could choose to succeed instead. Two thousand years of refusing to break had made her will nearly unassailable.
> ---
> ### Epic Boons (Accumulated Over Millennia)
> Boon of Fortitude. HP maximum increased by 40 (included above).
> Boon of High Magic. Gained one additional 9th-level spell slot.
> Boon of Resilience. Resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks.
> Boon of Irresistible Offense. Could bypass damage resistance with weapon attacks.
> ---
> ### Class Features (Sanctified Champion)
> The Sanctified Champion is a unique hybrid class combining the martial prowess and divine smites of a Paladin with the full spellcasting progression of a Cleric. Mira developed this path through two millennia of devotion and thirty years of conviction.
> Divine Sense (At Will). Mira could detect celestials, fiends, undead, and consecrated/desecrated areas within 120 feet.
> Lay on Hands. Pool of 100 HP for healing or curing disease/poison (5 HP removes one disease or neutralizes one poison).
> Fighting Style: Great Weapon Fighting. Reroll 1s and 2s on damage dice for two-handed weapons.
> Improved Divine Smite. All melee weapon attacks dealt an additional 1d8 radiant damage.
> Divine Smite. When hitting with a melee weapon, Mira could expend a spell slot to deal additional radiant damage (2d8 + 1d8 per slot level above 1st, maximum 6d8). Against fiends and undead, add another 1d8.
> Divine Health. Immune to disease.
> Aura of Protection (30 ft). Mira and allies within 30 feet added +7 to all saving throws (CHA modifier).
> Aura of Courage (30 ft). Mira and allies within 30 feet could not be frightened.
> Cleansing Touch (7/day). End one spell affecting herself or a willing creature she touched.
> Channel Divinity (3/day). Could use:
- Turn Undead/Fiend (DC 21, destroy CR 4 or lower)
- Conviction's Radiance (60-ft burst, 8d8 radiant, DC 21 CON half)
- Dictum of Truth (as Zone of Truth but 60 ft, no save, lasts 10 minutes)
> Divine Intervention (1/Long Rest). Automatic success. Her conviction WAS divine at that point.
> Timeless Body. Could not be magically aged; immune to effects that would reduce ability scores or HP maximum; did not need food, water, or sleep (but could benefit from rest).
> ---
> ### Spellcasting (Full Cleric Progression + Paladin Enhancement)
> Spell Save DC: 21 (8 + 6 proficiency + 7 CHA)
Spell Attack Modifier: +13
> Spell Slots:
| Level | Slots |
|-------|-------|
| 1st | 4 |
| 2nd | 3 |
| 3rd | 3 |
| 4th | 3 |
| 5th | 3 |
| 6th | 2 |
| 7th | 2 |
| 8th | 1 |
| 9th | 2 (Boon of High Magic) |
> Cantrips: Sacred Flame (4d8), Spare the Dying (30 ft range), Thaumaturgy, Light, Toll the Dead (4d8/4d12), Word of Radiance (4d6)
> Always Prepared (Domain/Oath): Bless, Cure Wounds, Lesser Restoration, Spiritual Weapon, Revivify, Beacon of Hope, Death Ward, Guardian of Faith, Raise Dead, Circle of Power, Heal, Resurrection
> Additional Prepared Spells (13):
- 1st: Shield of Faith, Command, Detect Evil and Good
- 2nd: Aid, Zone of Truth
- 3rd: Dispel Magic, Remove Curse, Speak with Dead, Spirit Guardians
- 4th: Banishment
- 5th: Greater Restoration, Holy Weapon
- 6th: Harm, Word of Recall
- 7th: Divine Word, Plane Shift
- 8th: Holy Aura
- 9th: Mass Heal, True Resurrection, Gate
> ---
> ### Equipment (At Time of Death)
> Oathkeeper (+3 Legendary Greatsword, Summonable, Requires Attunement) — Now held by Seraphine Vale
> Forged from two thousand years of conviction, Oathkeeper was less a weapon and more a philosophy given edge. It first manifested the day Mira chose truth over obedience.
> - Attack Bonus: +15 to hit (+6 STR, +6 proficiency, +3 weapon)
- Damage: 2d6 + 9 slashing (+6 STR, +3 weapon) plus 2d8 radiant (Improved Divine Smite + weapon property)
- Summonable: Could be summoned to hand as a bonus action from any plane of existence
- Conviction's Edge: Dealt an additional 1d8 radiant damage (included above as 2d8 total)
- Soulbound: Could not be disarmed or stolen; if separated, teleported to her hand at the start of her turn
- Truth-Seeker: Attacks against creatures who had lied to Mira within the last 24 hours dealt an additional 2d6 radiant damage
- Grief-Fire: During Undying Conviction, the blade ignited with cold white flames, dealing an additional 3d8 radiant damage and shedding bright light in a 30-foot radius
> Conviction's Mantle (+2 Plate Armor, Requires Attunement) — Destroyed when Savaros consumed her
> Ring of Elias (Wedding Ring, Non-magical) — Now held by Sol Raven
> Healer's Satchel (Wondrous Item) — Destroyed when Savaros consumed her
> ---
> ### Actions
> Multiattack. Mira made three attacks with Oathkeeper.
> Oathkeeper. Melee Weapon Attack: +15 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 18 (2d6 + 9) slashing damage plus 9 (2d8) radiant damage.
- With Divine Smite (5th-level slot): +6d8 radiant damage (27 average)
- Against liars (Truth-Seeker): +2d6 radiant damage (7 average)
- During Undying Conviction: +3d8 radiant damage (13 average)
> Maximum Single Attack Damage: 18 + 9 + 27 + 7 + 13 = 74 damage (or 222 on a round with all three attacks smiting)
> Summon Oathkeeper (Bonus Action). Mira called Oathkeeper to her hand from any distance or plane.
> Healing Touch. Mira could expend Lay on Hands points to heal a creature she touched. Could also cure diseases or neutralize poisons (5 HP per effect).
> Conviction's Radiance (Channel Divinity). All creatures of Mira's choice within 60 feet had to make a DC 21 Constitution saving throw, taking 36 (8d8) radiant damage on a failure, or half on a success. Undead and fiends had disadvantage.
> ---
> ### Bonus Actions
> Spiritual Weapon (2nd+ level slot). Created a floating spectral greatsword that attacked for 1d8 + 7 force damage. Could move 20 feet and attack as a bonus action each turn.
> Holy Weapon (5th level slot, concentration). Oathkeeper dealt an additional 2d8 radiant damage for 1 hour. When concentration ended (or as action), 30-ft burst for 4d8 radiant (DEX DC 21 half) and blindness.
> Command Spell (1st+ level slot). Single-word command, WIS DC 21 or obey.
> ---
> ### Reactions
> Sentinel Strike. When a creature within 5 feet attacked a target other than Mira, she could make a melee attack against that creature.
> Wrath of the Conviction. When Mira or an ally within 30 feet was hit by an attack, she could use her reaction to impose disadvantage on that attack roll (potentially causing it to miss). Usable 7/day (CHA modifier).
> ---
> ### Legendary Actions (3/Round)
> Mira could take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action could be used at a time, and only at the end of another creature's turn. She regained spent legendary actions at the start of her turn.
> Attack (1 Action). Mira made one attack with Oathkeeper.
> Healing Word (1 Action). Mira cast Healing Word (healed 1d4 + 7) without expending a spell slot.
> Move (1 Action). Mira moved up to half her speed without provoking opportunity attacks.
> Cast Cantrip (1 Action). Mira cast Sacred Flame (4d8 radiant, DEX DC 21) or Toll the Dead (4d8/4d12 necrotic, WIS DC 21).
> Cleansing Light (2 Actions). Mira ended one spell effect or condition affecting herself or an ally within 30 feet.
> Smiting Strike (2 Actions). Mira made one attack with Oathkeeper with automatic Divine Smite at 3rd level (+4d8 radiant) without expending a spell slot. Usable 3/day.

---

Special Ability: Undying Conviction

Danger
Undying Conviction (1/Long Rest)
> "Aldric. Elias. Corwin. Daven. Bram. Adelaide. I am still here."
> ---
> Trigger: When Mira was reduced to 0 hit points or would be killed outright.
> Reaction: Mira refused death.
> ---
> ### The Transformation
> As Mira fell, something in her broke — or perhaps finally became whole. She rose, eyes blazing with cold radiance, and began muttering the names of her fallen friends in an endless, grief-stricken litany. Each name was a blade being drawn. Each name was thirty years of loss made manifest.
> "Aldric... Elias... Corwin... Daven... Bram... Adelaide..."
> She repeated them over and over, faster and faster, a prayer that had become a weapon. Oathkeeper ignited with grief-fire — cold white flames that burned without heat. She entered a state of blind, righteous fury — focused entirely on those who took everything from her.
> She gained 142 temporary hit points (half her maximum) and entered her Avatar of Vengeance state for 1 minute.
> Her CR increased to 23 during this state.
> ---
> ### Vengeful Presence
> When Mira transformed, all creatures within 60 feet who could see or hear her had to make a DC 21 Wisdom saving throw or become frightened of her for the duration.
> - Frightened creatures could not willingly move closer to Mira
- Frightened creatures had disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks while Mira was in line of sight
- A creature could repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success
- Creatures that succeeded were immune to this effect for 24 hours
- Creatures immune to fear were not immune to this effect — Mira's grief transcended magical resistance
> ---
> ### Avatar of Vengeance
> While in this state, Mira gained the following benefits:
> | Effect | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Immune | All conditions; all damage types except force and psychic |
| Regeneration | Regained 20 HP at the start of each of her turns |
| Maximum Smites | Divine Smites dealt maximum damage (no roll) — 48 damage per smite at 5th level |
| Four Attacks | Multiattack included four attacks instead of three |
| Grief-Fire | Oathkeeper dealt an additional 3d8 radiant damage (average 13) |
| Radiant Aura | Hostile creatures within 30 feet took 4d8 radiant damage at the start of Mira's turn |
| Relentless | Could not be moved against her will; immune to prone; speed could not be reduced |
| The Litany | Continuously muttered the names of her fallen friends — could not be silenced by any means |
| Legendary Actions | Gained 5 legendary actions per round instead of 3 |
> Maximum Round Damage During Avatar: Four attacks (4 × 18 slashing + 4 × 22 radiant + 4 × 13 grief-fire + 4 × 48 max smite) + Radiant Aura (18) = 422 damage in a single round (if all attacks hit and all smites used)
> ---
> ### Aftermath
> When the transformation ended, Mira dropped to 1 HP and gained 4 levels of exhaustion.
> The names stopped. She remembered every moment.
> ---
> ### Use in Campaign
> Mira triggered Undying Conviction in Session 17 when Kaleth Vorn's Sanguine Siphon drained her crimson aura. She rose wreathed in orange-gold fire and annihilated Kaleth and all his forces in seconds.
> The aftermath left her with 3 levels of exhaustion going into Session 18 — which is why Savaros was able to kill her. Her greatest strength became the vulnerability that ended her.

---

Combat Tactics (Historical Reference)

General Approach

  • Supportive first, aggressive second — kept party alive, then dealt damage
  • Smite conservatively — saved high-level slots for critical moments or finishing blows
  • Aura positioning — always aware of who was within her 30-foot protective radius
  • Legendary action economy — used LA healing to keep allies up between turns

Against Kaleth Vorn (Session 17)

  • This was personal — Kaleth had hunted the Seven for thirty years
  • Kaleth had the Sanguine Siphon — successfully drained her crimson aura
  • He thought that would end her
  • Undying Conviction triggered — she rose and destroyed him
  • She burned every spell slot and smite to end him
  • This man represented everything that had taken her friends from her. She got her vengeance.
---

Related

  • The Seven Heretics
  • Kaleth Vorn
  • Seraphine Vale
  • Mareatha the Vein-Mother
  • The Arterial Texts
  • The Sanguine Doctrine
  • Savaros the High Keeper
  • Campaign Sessions
  • History & Timeline
  • Aedwynn the Maker
  • Oathkeeper

items.title: Seraphine Vale
items.content:
Seraphine Vale

---

Overview

Warning
The Shattered Believer
Title: Former High Inquisitor of the Mareathan Inquisition
Allegiance: Uncertain — Faith shattered, currently cooperative
Role: Ally (uncertain), former antagonist
First Appearance: Session 2
Turned: Session 18
Level: 12 (Cleric 8 / Paladin 4)
Current Possession: Oathkeeper (Mira Alethain's greatsword)

---

Quick Reference

AttributeValue
ClassBlood Domain Cleric 8 / Oath of Devotion Paladin 4
AlignmentUncertain (was Lawful Neutral)
Age34
StatusFaith shattered, cooperative with party
LocationChurch of the Merciful Vein, Lesser Sacral District
Key ItemOathkeeper (Mira's greatsword)
CoverIntact — guards believe her false report
---

Portrait

Portrait
Physical Appearance
A striking woman in her mid-thirties with sharp features and eyes the color of dried blood — a mark of Mareatha's favor. Her dark hair is worn in a severe braid, and she carries herself with the absolute certainty of someone who has never questioned their purpose. Her Inquisitor's vestments are immaculate white trimmed with arterial red, and she wears the holy symbol of Mareatha openly at her throat.
> There is no cruelty in her face. That's what makes her dangerous. She genuinely believes she is saving souls.
> Mira Alethain remembers when those blood-colored eyes were just a child's brown. Before the Church got to her.

---

Backstory

The Vale Family

The Vale family has served the Mareathan Church for six generations. Faithful. Devout. Unquestioning. Seraphine's great-great-grandmother was a minor priestess. Her grandmother was a respected healer. Her mother died in service to the Vein-Mother when Seraphine was seven.

And Mira Alethain delivered Seraphine into the world.

Thirty-four years ago, before Mira fled Veinspire, before she became "Mira Hemm," before the Seven discovered the truth — she was one of the Church's most skilled healers. She attended Seraphine's mother through a difficult birth. She placed the newborn in her mother's arms. She blessed the child in Mareatha's name.

She remembers the weight of that infant. The cry. The mother's exhausted smile.

Now that infant hunts her as a heretic.

The Perfect Servant

Seraphine was raised in the Church after her mother's death. The clergy became her family. The Sanguine Doctrine became her truth. She never had a chance to question — the faith was woven into her bones before she could walk.

She rose through the ranks through:

  • Genuine devotion — she believes every word of doctrine
  • Exceptional skill — talented in both divine magic and combat
  • Absolute certainty — has never experienced doubt
  • Results — her conviction rate is impeccable
At thirty-four, she became the youngest High Inquisitor in three centuries. The Church celebrates her as proof that faith conquers all.

Mira sees her as proof that the Church devours its children.

Current Mission

Danger
Kaleth's Orders
> Kaleth Vorn tasked Seraphine with a straightforward assignment: capture ALL the heretics — the party AND Mira Alethain — and bring them to him by any means necessary.
> From Seraphine's perspective, this is standard Inquisition work:
- The Party: Confirmed heretics who stole sacred texts, fled judgment, and defied the Vein-Mother
- Mira Alethain: The last of the infamous Seven Heretics — the most dangerous of them all
- The Mission: Capture them all for interrogation and judgment
> The Seven Heretics are legendary among the Inquisition — apostates who discovered "forbidden knowledge" and tried to corrupt the faith thirty years ago. Six have been "brought to justice" over the years (Seraphine doesn't know this means murdered by Kaleth). Mira is the last. Capturing her would be the crowning achievement of Seraphine's career.
> She has no idea Kaleth's interest is specifically in extracting information about documents she's never heard of. She doesn't know about the Arterial Texts. She doesn't know Kaleth is a cynical murderer who finds her faith amusing.
> She's simply doing her job. Righteously. Faithfully. Completely in the dark about what she's actually serving.

---

Personality & Motivations

Core Traits

  • True believer — has never experienced genuine doubt
  • Righteous certainty — knows she is doing holy work
  • Patient predator — prefers to study prey before striking
  • Silver-tongued — turns interrogations into theological debates
  • Incorruptible — cannot be bribed, seduced, or swayed (because her faith is absolute)

What She Wants

1. Complete her mission — capture ALL the heretics for Kaleth 2. Serve Mareatha — fulfill her sacred duty 3. Bring the last of the Seven to justice — Mira Alethain's capture would be legendary

What She Fears

  • Failing in her duty
  • Heresy spreading unchecked
  • (She should fear the truth, but she doesn't know enough to fear it)

Quotes

"I don't hate heretics. I pity them. They've lost their way, and it falls to us to guide them back — or grant them the mercy of ending their suffering."

"The Doctrine is not cruel. Reality is cruel. The Doctrine simply acknowledges what must be done."

"You fled divine judgment. That tells me everything I need to know about your guilt."

"Mira Alethain. The last of the Seven. I've waited my entire career for this."

"Kaleth Vorn requires your presence. All of you. You can walk, or you can be dragged. The choice is yours."

---

Relationship with Mira Alethain

Info
The Woman Who Delivered Her
> ### What Seraphine Knows
- "Mira Alethain" is the last of the Seven Heretics — the most dangerous apostates in living memory
- She was once a respected healer in the Church before her fall
- She and six others tried to corrupt the faith with forbidden knowledge
- Capturing her would be the greatest achievement of Seraphine's career
> ### What Seraphine Doesn't Know
- Mira delivered her when she was born
- Mira knew her mother, blessed her cradle, watched her take her first steps
- Mira is over two thousand years old
- Mira could kill her without effort
- Mira doesn't want to kill her — she wants to save her
> ### Mira's Perspective
> Mira remembers:
- The difficult birth, the mother's fear, the relief when Seraphine cried
- Blessing the child in the name of a faith she still believed in
- Hearing, years later, that the girl had joined the clergy
- Learning that the child she delivered had become High Inquisitor
> When Mira looks at Seraphine, she doesn't see an enemy. She sees:
- A victim of the same lies she once believed
- A child raised without the chance to question
- Herself, two thousand years ago, before she knew better
- Someone who deserves truth, not death
> Mira will not kill Seraphine unless there is absolutely no other choice.

---

The Confrontation

Example
"Stand Aside, Child"
> When the party reaches the Church of the Merciful Vein, Seraphine will be waiting. She has her orders from Kaleth — capture ALL the heretics. She has her faith. She has her certainty. And she has the chance to capture the legendary last member of the Seven.
> And then Mira will step forward.
> ### The Moment of Recognition
> Seraphine will see the culmination of her career — Mira Alethain, the last of the Seven, finally within reach. She will demand surrender in Mareatha's name.
> Mira will see a baby she once held.
> ### Mira's Words
> "Seraphine. Seraphine Vale."
> A pause. Mira's voice is tired. Ancient.
> "I delivered you. Did you know that? Thirty-four years ago. Your mother was afraid — the birth was difficult. But you were strong. You cried so loud the priests in the next chamber heard you."
> Seraphine will falter. This isn't in the briefings. This isn't how heretics are supposed to speak.
> "I blessed you in Mareatha's name. I believed it then. I believed everything they told me, just like you do now."
> Mira steps closer. Her red aura flickers — crimson light reflecting off the cathedral walls.
> "You're going to stand aside now. Not because I'm threatening you — though I am. But because you are a thousand years too early to challenge me, and I don't want to hurt the child I brought into this world."
> ### Seraphine's Choice
> Seraphine has two options:
> 1. Stand aside — Let Mira pass. Receive the note directly from Mira's hand.
2. Attack anyway — And learn exactly how outmatched she is.

---

The Note

Warning
Mira's Message
> Regardless of Seraphine's choice, she will receive a note from Mira.
> ### If Seraphine Stands Aside
Mira will hand her the note directly, pressing it into her palm.
> "Read this when you're alone. Then decide what you believe."
> ### If Seraphine Attacks
Mira will disable her in seconds — disarm, knock unconscious, restrain. CR 21 vs CR 8. It won't be a fight; it will be a lesson.
> Before leaving, Mira will slip the note into Seraphine's vestments, tucked against her heart.
> "When you wake, read this. Then decide what you believe."
> ### The Note's Contents
> [To be determined — but it contains enough truth to shake Seraphine's faith. Perhaps:]
- Where to find evidence of the Church's lies
- Questions she should ask Kaleth about what he actually believes
- The truth about her birth and Mira's blessing
- A location where Seraphine can find answers if she chooses to seek them
> ### The Aftermath
> The note will plant seeds. Seraphine will have questions she can't silence. She'll ask Kaleth what he believes — and if he's honest (or arrogant enough to mock her faith), she'll see the contempt in his eyes.
> Eventually, Seraphine will leave the Church of her own free will.
> Not because Mira convinced her. Because Mira gave her the tools to convince herself.

---

Relationship with Kaleth Vorn

Warning
The Faithful and the Faithless
> Seraphine believes Kaleth Vorn is:
- A devoted servant of the Tribunal
- A skilled operative who handles "sensitive" matters
- A true believer who does difficult work for the faith
- Her superior who gave her orders to capture ALL the heretics
> She has no idea he's a cynical murderer who finds her faith amusing.
> Kaleth sees Seraphine as:
- Useful
- Naive
- A true believer in a system built on lies
- Privately hilarious
- Disposable if necessary
> If Seraphine ever learned what Kaleth actually thinks — that he's killed in the name of a faith he considers a joke, that he keeps trophies and monologues about his artistry — it would shatter her.
> Mira's note may tell her to ask Kaleth what he really believes.

---

History with the Party

Session 2: The Declaration

After the events beneath the church (Sister Marwen's death), Seraphine arrived swiftly at the head of the Mareathan Inquisition.

Her Judgment:

  • Declared Sol and Jonas "potential heretics"
  • Deemed them "useful enough to keep alive"
  • Forbade them from leaving Veinspire
  • Placed them under surveillance

Session 3: The Codex Heist

The party, with Kael's help, infiltrated the church and stole the sacred Resonant Codex from under Seraphine's nose. This was done while she believed she had them contained.

Session 4: The Escape

The party fled Veinspire through the southern mines, escaping Seraphine's immediate grasp.

Status: Seraphine now actively hunts them as confirmed heretics who stole sacred texts and fled divine justice.

Current Status

Location: Church of the Merciful Vein (positioned to intercept the heretics) Orders: Capture ALL the heretics (party AND Mira) and deliver them to Kaleth Vorn Resources: 2-4 Inquisition guards, full authority of the Church

---

D&D 5e Statistics

Example
Full Stat Block (Cleric 8 / Paladin 4)
> ### Seraphine Vale, High Inquisitor
Medium humanoid (human), lawful neutral
> ---
> Armor Class 20 (plate armor + shield)
Hit Points 102 (8d8 + 4d10 + 36)
Speed 30 ft.
> ---
> | STR | DEX | CON | INT | WIS | CHA |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| 16 (+3) | 10 (+0) | 16 (+3) | 12 (+1) | 18 (+4) | 16 (+3) |
> ---
> Saving Throws WIS +8, CHA +7
Skills Insight +8, Persuasion +7, Religion +5, Medicine +8, Intimidation +7
Damage Resistances Necrotic (Blood Domain)
Senses passive Perception 14
Languages Common, Celestial
Challenge 8 (3,900 XP)
> ---
> ### Cleric Features (Blood Domain — Homebrew)
> Sanguine Divinity. Seraphine's eyes are blood-red, a mark of Mareatha's favor. She has advantage on saving throws against being frightened.
> Channel Divinity: Blood Price (2/rest). As an action, Seraphine deals 3d6 necrotic damage to herself to deal 6d6 necrotic damage to a creature within 60 feet (WIS save DC 16 for half).
> Channel Divinity: Turn Undead. DC 16, destroy CR 1 or lower.
> Crimson Blessing. When Seraphine casts a healing spell, she can choose to deal that much necrotic damage to a creature within 30 feet instead (WIS save DC 16 for half).
> Arterial Sight. Seraphine can sense the presence of blood within 30 feet. She knows the location of any creature with blood in that range, even if hidden or invisible.
> ---
> ### Paladin Features (Oath of Devotion)
> Divine Sense (4/day). Detect celestials, fiends, and undead within 60 feet.
> Lay on Hands. Pool of 20 HP.
> Fighting Style: Protection. When a creature within 5 feet is attacked, impose disadvantage as a reaction (requires shield).
> Divine Smite. Expend spell slot for +2d8 radiant damage (+3d8 vs undead/fiends).
> Divine Health. Immune to disease.
> ---
> ### Spellcasting
> Spell Save DC: 16
Spell Attack Modifier: +8
> Cleric Spell Slots: 1st (4), 2nd (3), 3rd (3), 4th (2)
Paladin Spell Slots: 1st (3)
> Prepared Spells (Cleric — 12):
- Cantrips: Sacred Flame, Toll the Dead, Thaumaturgy, Guidance
- 1st: Cure Wounds, Inflict Wounds, Command, Shield of Faith, Bane
- 2nd: Hold Person, Silence, Spiritual Weapon, Zone of Truth
- 3rd: Dispel Magic, Spirit Guardians, Speak with Dead, Revivify
- 4th: Death Ward, Banishment
> Prepared Spells (Paladin — 4):
- 1st: Divine Favor, Compelled Duel, Wrathful Smite
> ---
> ### Equipment
> Inquisitor's Mace. +1 mace that deals an additional 1d6 radiant damage against creatures who have lied within the last hour.
> Judgment's Aegis. +1 shield. Once per day, can cast Zone of Truth without expending a slot.
> Holy Symbol of Mareatha. Focus for divine spells. Glows faintly in the presence of heretics (subjective).
> Manacles of Confession. Magical restraints. Bound creature cannot knowingly speak lies.
> ---
> ### Actions
> Multiattack. Seraphine makes two attacks with her Inquisitor's Mace.
> Inquisitor's Mace. Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) bludgeoning damage plus 3 (1d6) radiant damage (against liars).
> Inflict Wounds (1st level). Melee Spell Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one creature. Hit: 33 (6d10) necrotic damage.
> ---
> ### Reactions
> Protection. When a creature within 5 feet is attacked, impose disadvantage on the attack roll (requires shield).
> ---
> ### Tactics
> Seraphine fights with righteous conviction:
- Opens with Spirit Guardians for area control
- Uses Hold Person on spellcasters
- Focuses on capturing, not killing (Kaleth wants them alive)
- Will prioritize Mira — capturing the last of the Seven would be legendary
- Will use Banishment to remove powerful threats temporarily
- Will not retreat — her faith doesn't allow for doubt
> Against Mira: Seraphine has no chance. CR 8 vs CR 21. Mira could end the fight in a single round without breaking a sweat. Seraphine's only hope is that Mira doesn't want to kill her — which she doesn't.

---

Related

  • Mira Alethain
  • Kaleth Vorn
  • Mareatha the Vein-Mother
  • The Sanguine Doctrine
  • Church of the Merciful Vein
  • The Seven Heretics
  • Oathkeeper
  • Kael — buried behind her church
  • Sol Raven — gave her Oathkeeper
  • The Party
  • Campaign Sessions
---

"He called me an instrument. Fourteen years of service, and that's what I was to him. A tool." — Seraphine Vale, Session 18

She carries Oathkeeper now — a blade forged to cut through lies, given to a woman who just learned her entire life was one.

---

SESSION 18 UPDATE — FAITH SHATTERED

The Confrontation

Seraphine waited at the Church of the Merciful Vein as ordered by Kaleth Vorn. When Mira and Sol approached:

  • Mira revealed she killed Kaleth
  • Mira revealed she delivered Seraphine thirty-four years ago
  • Sol told her directly she'd been indoctrinated her entire life

The Evidence

Mira showed Seraphine Kaleth's journal and letters:

  • Kill list with six crossed-out names
  • Journal entry calling Seraphine "the ideal instrument"
  • Kaleth's admission he didn't believe any of the doctrine
  • Evidence of his murders spanning thirty years

Her Reaction

Seraphine's response to learning Kaleth was dead: "Good."

She dismissed the guards with a false report, kenneled the Blood-Hound, and set aside the Obedience Draught she was supposed to use on Mira.

Intelligence Provided

After one hour of processing, Seraphine shared:

  • Savaros — High Keeper of the Catacomb Reliquary, Kaleth's superior
  • The Cardinal — Position above Savaros, may not be human, ordered the hunt for the Seven
  • The Confluence — Ritual at the new moon, days away, Kaleth feared it
  • The Martyr's Gauntlet — Cathedral vault is empty; may be with Savaros

Current Status

Allegiance: Uncertain — Faith cracked but not destroyed Location: Church of the Merciful Vein, Lesser Sacral District Threat Level: Minimal — currently cooperative Cover: Intact — guards believe false report

Oathkeeper

After the events in the Catacomb Reliquary, Sol Raven gave Seraphine Oathkeeper — Mira Alethain's legendary greatsword.

The blade that served a two-thousand-year-old saint now rests in the hands of a thirty-four-year-old woman whose faith just shattered.

Why Sol gave it to her: Sol believed Seraphine needed something to hold onto — something that represented conviction and purpose. Mira's blade, forged to cut through lies, seemed fitting for someone who just learned her entire life was built on them.

Current Status: Oathkeeper is now carried by Seraphine Vale.

Potential Futures

Seraphine is at a crossroads: 1. Full defection — Joins the party actively against the Tribunal 2. Silent ally — Provides information but maintains cover 3. Crisis of faith — Paralyzed by doubt, unpredictable 4. Return to faith — Convinces herself the evidence is heretical lies

Her decision will likely depend on what happens next and how the party treats her.

---

⚠️ STATUS UNKNOWN — POST-CONFLUENCE (Session 19)

Warning
The Party Has No Information
While the party was in the Blooming Rot completing Mira's quest, The Confluence occurred in Veinspire.
> Seraphine's current status is unknown:
- Is her cover still intact?
- Did she survive the Confluence?
- Did she participate, resist, or flee?
- What happened to Oathkeeper?
> The party is 3-4 days travel from Veinspire. Whatever happened, they weren't there to help or witness it.

Last Known Status (Session 18):

  • Faith shattered by Kaleth's journal
  • Cover intact with false report to guards
  • Carries Oathkeeper (Mira's greatsword)
  • Provided intelligence on Savaros, The Cardinal, and The Confluence
Current Status: UNKNOWN

---

"He called me an instrument. Fourteen years of service, and that's what I was to him. A tool." — Seraphine Vale, Session 18

items.title: Thessaly
items.content:
Thessaly

---

Overview

Info
The Keeper of Forbidden Knowledge
Profession: Bookseller (Official), Dealer in Forbidden Texts (Actual)
Location: The Frayed Codex, Lesser Sacral District, Veinspire
Role: Information Source, Rare Text Dealer
First Appearance: Session 18

---

Quick Reference

AttributeValue
AgeImpossibly old
ShopThe Frayed Codex
DistrictLesser Sacral District
Public TradeHymnals, devotional texts
Actual TradeRestricted texts, pre-Usurpation fragments
---

Description

Old. Impossibly old. Skin like crumpled parchment, hair like white cobwebs, fingers like bundled twigs. Wears spectacles with lenses so thick they magnify her eyes to unsettling proportions.

Despite her frail appearance, she moves through her maze of books with the certainty of someone who knows exactly where everything is.

---

The Shop

The Frayed Codex: A narrow shopfront between a chandler and a boarded-up residence. Sign barely legible — faded paint depicting an open book with curling pages.

Inside: larger than it looks. Every surface covered in books, scrolls, and papers. Stacks reaching to the ceiling. A narrow path winds through the literary labyrinth.

The door bell has no clapper — removed intentionally.

Smells of old paper, older ink, and dried lavender hanging from the rafters.

---

Connection to Mira

Thessaly recognized Mira immediately:

"Thirty years, girl. Give or take."

She knew Mira before she fled Veinspire. Called her "girl" despite Mira being over two thousand years old — suggesting Thessaly may be even older, or simply irreverent.

"You look tired. And you've lost something. The red light you used to carry. Finally stopped pretending to be one of them?"

---

Knowledge & Warning

About the Resonant Codex

When she saw Kael carrying the Codex:

"That book. Where did you get it?"
"Child, that book is a Resonant Codex. There are perhaps four in existence. They don't get 'found' — they choose."
"It's quiet now. Content. I've never seen one content before."

About the Cardinal

"The Cardinal isn't like Kaleth. Kaleth was a blade — pointed, direct, predictable. The Cardinal is a web. You won't see the threads until you're already tangled."

---

Books Sold (Session 18)

The Doctrine of Still Air (75 gp) — Purchased by Sol

Thin book bound in leather dyed to look like silence itself — grey, muted, absorbing light.

Contents:

  • Azerach's philosophy of silence
  • How silence reveals truth
  • Why the Whispered King watches those who make noise
  • The nature of the brand (a door that opens both ways)
  • The Litany of Unmaking (forces truth for 1 hour, costs speaker's voice for 1 day)
Warnings:
"Don't read it aloud unless you want your tongue to forget how to lie. Permanently."
"The Litany is dangerous. The listening-back is more dangerous still. Don't attempt either without preparation."

The Unfinished Meal (100 gp) — Not Purchased

Book that shouldn't exist. Pages blank until looked at, words dissolving when you try to read them.

Contents:

  • Meditation on being consumed by Vor'Kael
  • Text changes based on reader — shows what the Empress would find delicious about YOU
  • Some people aren't in it at all — the book doesn't know what to show them
Thessaly warned Sol not to open it fully.

The Ledger of Moments (FREE) — Given to Fursen

Heavy tome bound in clockwork — tiny gears in the cover still turning.

Contents:

  • Partial transcription of Imhuran's temporal records
  • Names, crimes, sentences
  • Updates itself — new names appear, old names vanish
  • Contains Leonin history — including Fursen's mother Vaelith
Price: Free, with a promise to return with the truth about why the Leonin were really exterminated.

---

The Deal with Fursen

When Thessaly saw a living Leonin:

"I haven't seen a Leonin in... forty years? Fifty? They came to me sometimes, in the old days. Looking for the same thing you're looking for. They never found it."

CORRECTION: This is impossible — Leonin have been extinct for centuries/millennia. Thessaly's reaction was actually:

"That's... that's not possible."
"Leonin. An actual Leonin. Your kind has been gone for... how long? A thousand years? Two thousand?"
"I've read about your people my entire life. Collected every fragment, every rumor, every half-burned document the Church tried to destroy."
"I never thought I'd see one."

She gave Fursen the Ledger for free — proof that Imhuran's purge wasn't complete was worth more than gold.

The Promise: When Fursen discovers the truth about why the Leonin were exterminated, he returns to tell Thessaly.

"I've spent my whole life collecting forbidden knowledge. The truth of the Leonin extinction is the one thing I've never been able to find."
"Imhuran erased it. Not just the people — the WHY."

---

Related

  • Mira Alethain
  • Fursen
  • The Leonin
  • Imhuran the Regent of Hours
  • Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress
  • Azerach the Whispered King
  • The Cardinal
---

"Reading them changes you — not always for the worse, but always permanently." — Thessaly

items.title: Vela Thorne
items.content:
--- type: character role: ally allegiance: none (formerly Mareathan Church) status: active location: Pre-Usurpation Vault first_appearance: Session 17 tags: - npc - ally - archivist ---

Vela Thorne

---

Overview

Info
The Archivist Who Found Too Much
Role: Junior Archivist (former), Guide, Ally
Status: Active — Traveling with the Party
First Appearance: Session 17

---

Quick Reference

AttributeValue
Former PositionJunior Archivist, Mareathan Church
Age~30
Current LocationPre-Usurpation Vault (with party)
Mental StateTraumatized but functional
---

Portrait

Portrait
Physical Appearance
A young woman in her late twenties or early thirties — hard to tell under the bruises. Her robes were once the white-and-crimson of a Mareathan initiate, but they're torn now, stained with blood and tunnel-filth. Pale grey eyes, bloodshot from weeks of captivity. Dark circles beneath them. Hands that tremble when she's not actively using them.
> She moves like someone who expects to be hit.

---

Backstory

The Discovery

Vela was a junior archivist in the Mareathan Church — low-ranking, unremarkable, invisible. Until she found Kaleth Vorn's manifest.

A list of names spanning thirty years. All labeled "Resolved — Tribunal Authority." She started matching them to death records. Accident reports. Travel incidents.

None of them were accidents.

Capture

Kaleth caught her with the manifest. Instead of killing her, he smiled. Asked what she thought she'd found. Asked if she understood what heresy really meant.

Then he used her as bait.

"He said I'd make good bait. That the last name on his list would come for me eventually. That heretics can't resist saving people."

She was tortured. Questioned. Bound with blood-treated manacles. Kept barely alive for weeks, waiting for Mira Alethain to come.

Rescue (Session 17)

The party killed her captors and freed her. She witnessed Mira's Undying Conviction — watched a woman wreathed in orange fire dismember nine people in under a minute.

She's been processing that ever since.

---

Personality

Current State

  • Traumatized — weeks of torture leave marks
  • Intelligent — still an archivist at heart, still curious
  • Fragile — flinches at sudden movements, doesn't sleep well
  • Determined — refuses to be useless
  • Awed — has seen things that reshape worldviews

What She Knows

  • The catacombs beneath Veinspire — layout, sealed areas, hidden passages
  • Pre-Usurpation history — more than most archivists
  • The Leonin extinction — unofficial version
  • The sealed vaults — where they are, why the Church couldn't open them
  • Kaleth Vorn's methods — firsthand experience
---

Key Moments

Session 17:

  • Rescued from Tribunal guards
  • Provided information about Kaleth's trap
  • Explained Leonin history to Fursen
  • Led party to sealed pre-Usurpation vault
  • Witnessed the Chamber of the Final Breath
  • Currently traveling with party
---

Relationships

CharacterRelationship
Mira AlethainAwe and terror. Watched her kill nine people. Also watched her weep for her husband.
FursenProvided answers about his people. Respects his composure.
JonasCautious. He's branded by a Sovereign.
Sol RavenUnsettled. He's undead. But he was kind.
KaelCurious. Something about him doesn't quite fit.
---

Quotes

"They say a lot of things." — On Church doctrine about heretics

"I don't have anywhere else to go now anyway." — On joining the party

"The official histories say the Leonin crime was 'defying divine authority.' But I think the real crime was simpler. You remembered the truth. And that terrified him." — To Fursen

---

Related

  • Mira Alethain
  • Kaleth Vorn
  • Campaign Sessions
  • The Party
  • Mareatha the Vein-Mother

items.title: Kaleth Vorn
items.content:
Kaleth Vorn

---

Overview

Danger
DECEASED — Session 17
Cause of Death: Annihilated by Mira Alethain during Undying Conviction
Killed By: Oathkeeper (four strikes, less than four seconds)

---

Quick Reference

AttributeValue
TitleTribunal Hand
ClassDeath Domain Cleric 14
RoleChief assassin/interrogator for the Tribunal
SuperiorSavaros ("S")
Years Active30+
Kills6 of the Seven Heretics
Body CountSix of the Seven Heretics (over 30 years)
StatusDEAD
---

Portrait

Portrait
Physical Appearance (Before Death)
A gaunt man in his late fifties with hollow cheeks and cold, amused eyes. His hair was iron-grey, cropped short and practical. He moved with the casual confidence of someone who had never been truly challenged — every gesture deliberate, every expression calculated to unsettle. Wore dark clerical robes over light armor, with the inverted holy symbol of Mareatha displayed openly on his chest.
> His smile never reached his eyes. It was the smile of a man who found the universe's cruelty privately hilarious.
> He wore six rings on a chain around his neck. Trophies. One of them belonged to Elias Alethain.

---

Backstory

The Cynical Servant

Kaleth Vorn did not believe in the Sanguine Doctrine. He never had.

He understood from a young age that faith was a tool — a mechanism for controlling the masses, for justifying the consolidation of power, for giving meaning to meaningless violence. The Tribunal recognized this quality in him: a man without illusions, without the weakness of genuine belief, without the liability of conscience.

From his journal:

"Sometimes I wonder if any of them actually believe. I stopped believing decades ago. The doctrine is useful — nothing more. Faith is a tool for controlling the faithful."

What Kaleth actually believed:

  • The Sanguine Doctrine is a convenient fiction that maintains social order
  • The Sovereigns are parasites wearing the corpse of a god, but useful parasites
  • Heresy isn't a sin — it's a threat to a functional system
  • People need lies to function; truth is corrosive and destabilizing
  • He was simply better than the fools he eliminated — smarter, more patient, more realistic

The Thirty-Year Hunt

When the Seven Heretics fled Veinspire, the Tribunal assigned Kaleth Vorn to eliminate them.

His approach was different from other Inquisitors:

  • Patience — he tracked them for years before striking
  • Precision — clean kills, no witnesses, no martyrs
  • Cruelty — every death was designed to look like fate's punishment
  • Trophies — he kept something from each kill
Over thirty years, he killed six of the seven:

YearTargetRoleMethodNotes
3Aldric SarneTheologianPoisonVeinspire
8Elias AlethainHealerFall (staged)Pushed off cliff, Verdant Pass — Mira's husband
12Corwin MarshScholarBladeDusthollow
17Daven HoltChroniclerBandits (hired)Merchant Road
21Bram AshfordWarrior-PriestDuel (arranged)Pallid Crossroads
26Adelaide DunneSeerTribunal (official)Veinspire, public execution
30Mira AlethainPaladinFAILEDShe killed him instead
Mira didn't even know they were murdered — she thought they had simply died to time, bad luck, and dangerous roads. Kaleth found this delicious.

---

Relationship with Seraphine Vale

Kaleth used Seraphine Vale as a pawn, finding her earnest faith privately amusing. From his journal, he called her "the ideal instrument."

Seraphine believed Kaleth was a devoted servant of the Tribunal. She had no idea he was a cynical murderer who found her faith hilarious.

---

Death — Session 17

The Trap

Kaleth set a trap in the Ossuary Chamber using Vela Thorne as bait, knowing Mira would come. He brought:

  • 6 Tribunal Guards
  • 1 Tribunal Priest
  • 1 Blood-Crawler (tracker)
  • The Sanguine Siphon (borrowed from superiors)

The Sanguine Siphon

A device designed to drain Mareathan divine power. Kaleth activated it immediately, draining Mira's crimson aura. She collapsed.

He thought he'd won.

Undying Conviction

What Kaleth didn't know: the red aura was borrowed power. Mira's true power — her Undying Conviction — was entirely her own.

She rose wreathed in orange-gold fire, speaking the names of her dead friends.

The Kill

Mira didn't let him speak.

Four strikes: 1. Shoulder through to below the arm — split him open 2. Backswing — removed his other arm at the elbow 3. Rising cut — sternum to throat 4. Executioner's stroke — head removed

Time elapsed: Less than four seconds.

---

Items Found on Body

Standard:

  • Black iron keyring (7 keys)
  • 45 gold, 12 silver
  • Tribunal signet ring
  • Simple dagger
  • Vial of Obedience Draught
Hidden:
  • Sealed letter from "S" — mentions "The Cardinal," "The Confluence"
  • Journal — personal notes, cynicism, used Seraphine as pawn
  • Kill list — all seven heretics with names, dates, methods
  • Brass key — Pre-Usurpation symbol (door within a door), hidden in false tooth
---

The Arterial Texts

Kaleth knew about the Arterial Texts — Mareatha's pre-Usurpation writings that prove the Sanguine Doctrine is a fabrication. The Tribunal wanted them destroyed.

Mira was the last person who knew where they were hidden. Kaleth's mission was to extract the location before killing her.

---

Legacy

Kaleth Vorn murdered six people over thirty years. He used Seraphine Vale without her knowledge. He kept trophies and wrote about his victims with clinical detachment.

He died in pieces, unable to speak his final words, killed by the woman whose husband he pushed off a cliff.

The last thing he saw: Orange fire and the face of judgment.

---

Related

  • Mira Alethain
  • Seraphine Vale
  • Vela Thorne
  • The Seven Heretics
  • Campaign Sessions
  • Mareatha the Vein-Mother
  • Savaros
  • The Cardinal
---

"Thirty years, Alethain. Thirty years I've been collecting your friends." — Kaleth Vorn's intended monologue (never delivered)
