items.title: Savaros the High Keeper
items.content:
Savaros, the High Keeper

---

Overview

Info
Ancient Skin-Walker in Ultharion's Service
True Name: Unknown (lost to time)
Known As: Savaros, High Keeper of the Catacomb Reliquary
True Nature: Ancient Skin-Walker (Aberration/Shapechanger)
Allegiance: Ultharion the Obsidian Shepherd
Status: ☠️ DESTROYED — Session 18 (killed by consuming Kael's indigestible flesh)
CR: 17 (18,000 XP)

---

Quick Reference

AttributeValue
CR17
Time in Church~1,500 years
Identities Stored200+
SuperiorThe Cardinal (also a Skin-Walker)
LocationCatacomb Reliquary, Veinspire
Fighting StylePatient, methodical, exploits emotional weaknesses
---

Portrait

Portrait
Physical Appearance
The figure wears robes the color of arterial spray. A mask shaped like Mareatha's weeping visage covers his face — tears of blood frozen in crimson metal. His hands are gloved. No skin is visible.
> When he speaks, his voice sounds like paper tearing — dozens of voices layered atop one another, never quite synchronizing. Sometimes a woman's tone bleeds through. Sometimes a child's.
> He moves wrong. Not inhumanly fast or slow, but layered — as if multiple people are trying to walk using the same legs, compromising on each step.
> The mask has no eye holes. He doesn't need them.

[!ai-prompt] Image Generation
Tall robed figure in deep crimson vestments, face covered by ornate metal mask depicting weeping woman with tears of frozen blood, no visible skin, gloved hands, standing in dark catacomb surrounded by glass cases containing preserved relics, faint void-darkness visible at edges of robe sleeves, multiple ghostly faces faintly visible beneath the fabric of his robes, dark fantasy concept art, cosmic horror aesthetic, muted colors with divine lighting accents, body horror elements, inspired by Berserk and Bloodborne, digital painting, detailed textures, ominous atmosphere, 4k

---

The Paper-Tearing Voice

Savaros has worn so many identities for so long that his voice has become a chorus. When he speaks:

  • Multiple voices overlap slightly out of sync
  • Occasionally a stolen identity bleeds through (different accent, different tone)
  • The sound itself is unsettling — like hearing a crowd whisper through one mouth
  • He cannot speak normally anymore; this is permanent
Sample Dialogue:

"You killed Kaleth." (A woman's voice underneath, grieving.)
"He was useful." (A child's voice, petulant.)
"You are less so." (His own voice — if he ever had one — cold as a void.)

"I have worn priests. I have worn paladins. I have worn saints." (A chorus of the faithful.)
"Do you know how many heretics I have consumed in this very chamber?" (Screaming, underneath.)
"You will join them." (Silence. Then paper tearing.)

"The Vein-Mother sleeps. She has always slept. We tend her flock in her absence." (Mocking. Reverent. Both at once.)

---

Backstory

Origin

Savaros's original identity is lost — consumed so long ago that even he doesn't remember who he was before Ultharion claimed him. He may have been one of Ultharion's first Skin-Walkers, created in the early centuries after the Usurpation.

What he remembers: the mission.

Ultharion tasked him with infiltrating Mareatha the Vein-Mother's Church — the largest religious institution built on the Maker's corpse. Savaros consumed a minor priest, then a senior priest, then an archivist, then a keeper. Each identity brought him closer to the center of power.

Fifteen centuries later, he is the High Keeper of the Catacomb Reliquary — guardian of the Church's most sacred relics and darkest secrets.

The Infiltration

Savaros is not alone. The Cardinal — his superior — is also a Skin-Walker, planted even earlier. Together they form a two-person intelligence network feeding information to Ultharion:

  • Secrets extracted from confessions
  • Identities of potential targets
  • Church politics and power structures
  • The location of relics and artifacts
  • Weaknesses in Mareatha's domain
Mareatha the Vein-Mother remains unaware. She sleeps, subconsciously blessing her followers, dismissing the mortal bureaucracy as "uninteresting." As long as offerings flow and her domain expands, she doesn't look closely at who runs her Church.

This suits Ultharion perfectly.

Relationship with Kaleth Vorn

Kaleth Vorn reported to Savaros — believing him to be a high-ranking Church official, nothing more. Savaros provided:

  • The Sanguine Siphon (on loan from Ultharion's collection)
  • Intelligence on the Seven Heretics' locations
  • Orders from "the Tribunal"
Kaleth never knew his handler was a monster wearing stolen faces. He was a useful tool — fanatical, competent, disposable.

Now Kaleth is dead. Savaros expected retaliation eventually. He's been preparing.

---

Personality & Motivations

Core Traits

  • Patient — fifteen centuries of waiting; a few more hours means nothing
  • Methodical — every action serves a purpose; nothing is wasted
  • Curious — genuinely interested in identities, secrets, what makes people them
  • Cruel — not sadistically, but practically; suffering extracts information
  • Loyal — to Ultharion, absolutely; to no one else

What He Wants

1. Serve Ultharion — gather intelligence, maintain the infiltration 2. Protect the Cardinal — his superior must not be exposed 3. Consume interesting identities — he collects them like others collect art 4. Survive — he's existed for fifteen centuries; he intends to exist for fifteen more

What He Fears

  • Mirrors — they reveal his void form; he's removed all from the Reliquary
  • Exposure — if the Church learns what he is, the infiltration ends
  • Ultharion's disappointment — the Obsidian Shepherd does not tolerate failure
  • True self-examination — like all Skin-Walkers, he avoids confronting his own emptiness
---

Ultharion's Blessings

Fifteen centuries of faithful service has earned Savaros considerable investment from the Obsidian Shepherd. These blessings are woven into his very existence — gifts from a Sovereign who protects his assets.

BlessingDescription
Shadow TetherSavaros can see through any shadow within 120 ft. and teleport between them. If slain, Ultharion is alerted within 1 minute.
Fear HarvestWhen a creature within 60 ft. becomes frightened, Savaros regains 15 HP. If frightened of themselves (via Void Revelation's worst-self vision), he regains 30 HP instead.
Reflection's RevengeWhen hit by a melee attack, Savaros can force the attacker to see themselves in his mask delivering the blow. DC 17 Wis save or take half the damage dealt as psychic damage.
The Shepherd's GazeUltharion can perceive through Savaros at will. During combat, roll d4 at the start of each round; on a 4, Ultharion is watching — one attack that round deals an additional 14 (4d6) psychic damage as the Sovereign's attention weighs on the target.
Boundary SlipOnce per day, Savaros can move through solid objects and creatures as if they were difficult terrain for 1 minute. Takes 5 (1d10) force damage if he ends his turn inside an object.

Quote
The Weight of a Sovereign's Attention
When the Shepherd's Gaze is active, describe it: "For a moment, the shadows in the room lean toward you. Something vast and cold notices you exist. The weight of its attention presses against your mind like fingers testing the ripeness of fruit."

---

D&D 5e Statistics

Example
Full Stat Block
### Savaros, the High Keeper
Medium aberration (shapechanger), neutral evil
> ---
> Armor Class 19 (natural armor, layers of stolen flesh)
Hit Points 285 (30d8 + 150)
Speed 40 ft.
> ---
> | STR | DEX | CON | INT | WIS | CHA |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| 16 (+3) | 22 (+6) | 20 (+5) | 20 (+5) | 18 (+4) | 24 (+7) |
> ---
> Saving Throws Dex +12, Con +11, Wis +10, Cha +13
Skills Deception +19, Insight +16, Perception +10, Stealth +12, Religion +11
Damage Resistances Necrotic, Psychic; Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing from nonmagical attacks
Damage Immunities Poison
Condition Immunities Charmed, Frightened, Poisoned
Senses Darkvision 120 ft., Truesight 60 ft., passive Perception 20
Languages All (speaks through stolen voices)
Challenge 17 (18,000 XP)
> ---
> ### Traits
> Layered Existence. Savaros exists as hundreds of stolen identities compressed into one form. When he takes damage, stolen selves peel away like burning pages — faces screaming silently as they dissolve into shadow.
> Ancient Shapechanger. Savaros can use a bonus action to assume the form of any humanoid whose identity he has stolen (over 200 options). His statistics remain the same. He can also revert to his void form (see Void Revelation).
> Perfect Imitation. While wearing a stolen identity, Savaros is indistinguishable from the original through non-magical means. He has their memories, mannerisms, and voice. Magical detection reveals his true nature only with a DC 20 Investigation check after casting the detection spell.
> Shadow Tether (Ultharion's Blessing). Savaros can see through any shadow within 120 feet. He can teleport between shadows within this range as a bonus action (does not provoke opportunity attacks). If reduced to 0 HP, Ultharion becomes aware of his death within 1 minute.
> Fear Harvest (Ultharion's Blessing). Whenever a creature within 60 feet of Savaros becomes frightened (by any source), Savaros regains 15 hit points. If the creature is frightened of themselves specifically (such as through Void Revelation's worst-self vision), Savaros regains 30 hit points instead. This creates a feedback loop: his abilities cause fear, fear heals him.
> The Shepherd's Gaze (Ultharion's Blessing). At the start of each round of combat, roll 1d4. On a 4, Ultharion is actively perceiving through Savaros. While the Shepherd's Gaze is active, one of Savaros's attacks that round deals an additional 14 (4d6) psychic damage as the weight of a Sovereign's attention crushes the target's sense of self.
> Mirror Vulnerability. Mirrors reveal Savaros's void form regardless of what shape he wears. If he sees his own reflection unexpectedly, he must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom save or be stunned until the end of his next turn. He has removed all mirrors from the Reliquary.
> Legendary Resistance (3/Day). If Savaros fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead. When he does, one of his stolen identities visibly dies — peeling away from his form and dissolving into shadow with a sound like tearing paper.
> The Unraveling (Bloodied Phase). When Savaros drops below 142 HP for the first time, his control begins to slip. The stolen identities inside him start fighting for dominance. He immediately uses Void Revelation if he hasn't already (no action required), and gains the following until the end of combat:
- His speed increases to 50 ft.
- He can take two reactions per round instead of one
- At the start of each of his turns, one random stolen identity manifests as a Shed Remnant (see below) in an unoccupied space within 10 feet
- His attacks deal an additional 7 (2d6) psychic damage as the chorus of voices screams with each strike
> ---
> ### Actions
> Multiattack. Savaros makes three attacks: two with Void Touch and one with Identity Rend. He can replace one attack with Peel Self if available.
> Void Touch. Melee Spell Attack: +13 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 28 (6d6 + 7) necrotic damage. The target must succeed on a DC 19 Charisma saving throw or have disadvantage on Charisma checks and saves for 1 minute.
> Identity Rend. Melee Weapon Attack: +12 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 22 (4d6 + 6) slashing damage plus 14 (4d6) psychic damage. If the target fails a DC 19 Wisdom save, Savaros learns one significant secret about them (a fear, a lie they've told, a hidden shame). He will use this information.
> Peel Self (Recharge 5-6). Savaros targets one creature within 60 feet and attempts to separate their sense of self from their body. The target must make a DC 19 Charisma saving throw:
- Failed Save: The creature's mind and body disconnect. Their body acts on autopilot (can only take the Dodge action or move randomly), while their consciousness watches helplessly from within. This lasts until the end of the creature's next turn or until they take damage.
- Successful Save: The target takes 36 (8d8) psychic damage.
> Void Revelation (1/Day). Savaros drops all stolen forms and reveals his true nature — a humanoid absence, a tear in reality shaped like a person. All creatures within 60 feet who can see him must make a DC 19 Wisdom saving throw:
- Failed Save: 55 (10d10) psychic damage and frightened for 1 minute. While frightened this way, the creature sees their own worst self whenever they look at Savaros. This triggers Fear Harvest for 30 HP per frightened creature.
- Successful Save: Half damage, not frightened.
> While in void form, Savaros has resistance to all damage except radiant and force, but cannot use Perfect Imitation or Identity Rend.
> Chorus of the Consumed (Recharge 6). Savaros opens his mouth and all 200+ stolen voices scream at once. Each creature within 30 feet must make a DC 19 Constitution saving throw:
- Failed Save: 44 (8d10) thunder damage plus 22 (4d10) psychic damage, and the creature is deafened and stunned until the end of their next turn.
- Successful Save: Half damage, deafened for 1 round, not stunned.
> Boundary Slip (1/Day, Ultharion's Blessing). Savaros becomes partially incorporeal for 1 minute. During this time, he can move through solid objects and creatures as if they were difficult terrain. He takes 5 (1d10) force damage if he ends his turn inside an object. While Boundary Slip is active, he has advantage on saving throws against effects that would restrain or grapple him.
> ---
> ### Bonus Actions
> Shadow Step. Savaros teleports up to 60 feet to a shadow he can see.
> Stolen Face. Savaros shifts to a different stolen identity. This can be used to confuse enemies or access specific memories/skills of the stolen person.
> Sacrifice Identity. Savaros burns one of his stored identities to fuel his power. He regains 20 HP and gains advantage on his next attack roll. The sacrificed identity screams as it dies. Savaros can use this three times per combat.
> ---
> ### Reactions
> Shed Skin. When Savaros takes damage, he can shed the current stolen identity as a reaction, reducing the damage by 25. The shed identity falls to the ground as an empty skin before dissolving into shadow. Savaros can use this reaction 5 times per day.
> Reflection's Revenge (Ultharion's Blessing). When Savaros is hit by a melee attack, he can force the attacker to see their own reflection in his mask — but the reflection is delivering the blow back at them. The attacker must succeed on a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw or take psychic damage equal to half the damage they just dealt to Savaros.
> Whispered Truth. When a creature within 30 feet makes an attack roll against Savaros, he can speak a secret about them (learned through Identity Rend or his centuries of intelligence gathering). The creature must succeed on a DC 19 Wisdom save or the attack automatically misses as they falter. This can only affect each creature once per combat.
> Borrowed Skill. When Savaros fails an ability check, he can draw on a stolen identity who possessed that skill, rerolling the check with advantage. He can use this reaction 3 times per day.
> ---
> ### Legendary Actions
> Savaros can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. Savaros regains spent legendary actions at the start of his turn.
> Shadow Step (1 Action). Savaros teleports to a shadow within 60 feet.
> Void Touch (2 Actions). Savaros makes one Void Touch attack.
> Unravel (2 Actions). Savaros targets one creature within 30 feet and speaks in the voice of someone that creature loved who has died. The target must succeed on a DC 19 Wisdom save or be stunned until the end of their next turn as grief overwhelms them. Savaros has consumed enough identities over the centuries that he almost certainly has someone relevant.
> Identity Cascade (2 Actions). Savaros rapidly cycles through a dozen stolen faces in seconds — priests, children, warriors, lovers, all screaming. Each creature within 20 feet that can see him must succeed on a DC 19 Wisdom save or be frightened until the end of their next turn. Each frightened creature triggers Fear Harvest.
> Consume Secret (3 Actions). Savaros targets one creature within 60 feet from whom he has learned a secret (via Identity Rend). He speaks the secret aloud. The target takes 27 (6d8) psychic damage and must succeed on a DC 19 Charisma save or have their secret become public knowledge — all other creatures present now know it.
> Manifest the Eaten (3 Actions). Savaros reaches into himself and pulls out a stolen identity, manifesting it as a Shade of the Consumed in an unoccupied space within 10 feet. He can have up to 2 Shades active at once.
> ---
> ### Lair Actions
> On initiative count 20 (losing ties), Savaros can take one lair action while in the Catacomb Reliquary. He cannot use the same lair action two rounds in a row.
> Grasping Shadows. Shadows in the Reliquary reach for the living. Each creature of Savaros's choice within 60 feet must succeed on a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw or be restrained by shadows until initiative count 20 of the next round. A restrained creature can use its action to make a DC 17 Strength check, freeing itself on a success. Radiant damage or bright light in the creature's space ends the effect.
> Whispers of the Dead. The stolen voices trapped within Savaros echo through the Reliquary, speaking secrets and lies. Each creature of Savaros's choice within 60 feet must succeed on a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw or be unable to distinguish friend from foe until initiative count 20 of the next round. Affected creatures must roll a d20 before making any attack; on a 10 or lower, they must target a random creature within range instead of their intended target.
> Reliquary's Hunger. The glass cases containing preserved relics shatter and the darkness within reaches out. Choose up to three creatures within 60 feet. Each must make a DC 17 Charisma saving throw or take 18 (4d8) necrotic damage as shadows attempt to tear away a fragment of their identity. On a failed save, the creature also forgets one piece of information of Savaros's choice (a name, a location, a spell they know) until they complete a short rest or receive Lesser Restoration.
> Extinguish. All nonmagical light sources within 120 feet are extinguished. Magical light sources are suppressed until initiative count 20 of the next round. Creatures without darkvision are blinded while in darkness.
> ---
> ### Shed Remnant
Small aberration, neutral evil
AC 13 | HP 22 (4d6 + 8) | Speed 30 ft.
> A twitching, half-formed shape of shadow and stolen memory. Attacks: Claw +5 to hit, 7 (2d4 + 2) necrotic damage. Dies at the end of Savaros's next turn if not destroyed first. If a Shed Remnant hits a creature, Savaros can see through that creature's shadow until the end of his next turn.
> ---
> ### Shade of the Consumed
Medium undead, neutral evil
AC 14 | HP 45 (7d8 + 14) | Speed 40 ft., fly 40 ft. (hover)
> A ghostly manifestation of one of Savaros's stored identities — translucent, anguished, still wearing the face it had in life.
> Incorporeal Movement. Can move through creatures and objects as difficult terrain. Takes 5 (1d10) force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.
> Attacks:
- Life Drain +7 to hit, 14 (4d6) necrotic damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution save or have their HP maximum reduced by the damage taken until they finish a long rest.
- Wail (Recharge 5-6): 30-ft cone, DC 14 Wisdom save or 21 (6d6) psychic damage and frightened for 1 minute. Frightened creatures trigger Savaros's Fear Harvest if within 60 ft. of him.
> Bound to Savaros. If Savaros is destroyed, all Shades immediately dissipate. If a Shade is destroyed, Savaros takes 10 psychic damage as the identity is lost forever.

---

Combat Tactics

Opening

  • Savaros will not be surprised — he heard them coming through the arterial passage
  • He waits in a form they won't immediately recognize as hostile (a priest? a prisoner? perhaps even someone they know?)
  • First action: Identity Rend on the most emotionally vulnerable target to harvest secrets
  • First lair action: Extinguish to blind anyone without darkvision
  • Roll d4 for The Shepherd's Gaze — if Ultharion is watching, make that first hit count

Mid-Combat

  • Uses Shadow Step constantly to avoid being cornered
  • Targets Mira with Peel Self — if her mind separates from her exhausted body, she's helpless
  • Uses Whispered Truth to disrupt attackers
  • Uses Unravel (legendary action) to stun characters with emotional vulnerabilities
  • Uses Identity Cascade to frighten multiple targets — each triggers Fear Harvest for 15 HP
  • Spreads damage across the party rather than focusing one target
  • Uses Grasping Shadows to restrain melee fighters, then Whispers of the Dead to cause friendly fire
  • Uses Reflection's Revenge against heavy hitters — punish them for their own damage

When Bloodied (Below 142 HP)

  • The Unraveling triggers automatically
  • Void Revelation activates (if not already used) — frightened creatures trigger Fear Harvest for 30 HP each
  • Speed increases, gains extra reaction (can use both Shed Skin AND Reflection's Revenge)
  • Shed Remnants begin spawning each turn
  • Activates Boundary Slip to move through walls and avoid being cornered
  • Fights more aggressively — uses Chorus of the Consumed when multiple enemies are clustered
  • Will NOT flee — he's confident in Ultharion's power and his own

Healing Potential

Savaros has significant sustain through multiple sources:

  • Fear Harvest: 15-30 HP per frightened creature
  • Sacrifice Identity: 20 HP, 3 times per combat
  • Shed Skin: Reduces incoming damage by 25, 5 times per day
In a fight where he frightens 3-4 creatures with Void Revelation (30 HP each = 90-120 HP) and uses Sacrifice Identity fully (60 HP), he can effectively have 430+ effective HP.

Priorities

1. Neutralize Mira — the biggest threat 2. Harvest secrets from the party — information for Ultharion 3. Kill them all

---

The Reliquary Environment

Even without minions, the Reliquary favors Savaros:

FeatureEffect
No mirrorsHe's removed them all — his vulnerability is managed
Abundant shadowsColumns, alcoves, shelves — plenty of Shadow Step targets
Glass casesContain relics — fuel for Reliquary's Hunger lair action
DarknessNo natural light; darkvision or light sources required
High ceiling40 feet — Shades can fly out of melee reach
Thick wallsWith Boundary Slip, Savaros can move through them; the party cannot follow
One known exitThe party came through the arterial valve; Savaros knows other ways out
---

Potential Weaknesses

WeaknessHow to Exploit
Mirror VulnerabilityCreate a reflective surface (polished shield, Create Water on the floor, Sol's magic); seeing his reflection stuns him
Radiant DamageNo resistance; Mira's smites and radiant spells are fully effective — even more so in Void Form
Bright LightEnds Grasping Shadows effect; limits Shadow Step options
FireCan destroy shadows he teleports through, limiting mobility
Emotional IsolationCharacters with no secrets or no dead loved ones are immune to psychological attacks
Overwhelming DamageShed Skin only works 5 times; burn through it and he takes full damage
Destroying ShadesEach destroyed Shade deals 10 psychic damage to Savaros
Preventing FearImmune to frightened = no Fear Harvest healing from that character
Blocking Boundary SlipForce damage hurts him if he ends turn in an object; Wall of Force is solid
---

What Savaros Knows

If somehow captured or interrogated:

TopicInformation
The CardinalAlso a Skin-Walker, older than Savaros, planted first
Ultharion's GoalIntelligence gathering; weakening Mareatha from within
The ConfluenceLegitimate Church event at the Monastery of Still Blood; the Cardinal uses it to harvest secrets
The MonasteryLocation beneath Mareatha's Heart, outside Veinspire
The PartyKnows they killed Kaleth; has been expecting retaliation
The Arterial TextsKnows they exist; doesn't know their location (only Mira does)
---

Aftermath

If Savaros Dies

  • Ultharion becomes aware within 1 minute (Shadow Tether)
  • The Cardinal will know something went wrong when Savaros doesn't report
  • The party can search the Reliquary for intelligence
  • They've made an enemy of a Sovereign (Ultharion) — he now knows their faces
  • The Confluence continues, but with increased security

If the Party Flees

  • Savaros reports to the Cardinal immediately
  • The Confluence's security increases dramatically
  • The party becomes priority targets for both the Church AND Ultharion's network
  • But they survive to fight another day
---

Related

  • Ultharion the Obsidian Shepherd
  • Skin-Walkers
  • Kaleth Vorn
  • The Cardinal
  • Mareatha the Vein-Mother
  • Monastery of Still Blood
  • Campaign Sessions

---

☠️ DEFEATED — SESSION 18

---

The Battle

The party descended through the Arterial Passage and confronted Savaros in the main chamber of the Catacomb Reliquary.

What Savaros Achieved Before Death:

  • Consumed Mira Alethain's flesh and skin (Level 20 Sanctified Champion) — ripped from her bones
  • Consumed Kael's flesh and skin (NPC companion) — torn from his skeleton
How He Died — Poetic Justice:

After consuming Mira, Savaros turned his hunger on Kael. He ripped away Kael's flesh and skin, reducing him to a skeleton with a cracked ribcage.

But Kael was no ordinary being.

Kael was an ontological anomaly — a concept that Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress herself had tried to digest and couldn't. She vomited him out because his very nature was indigestible, incompatible with consumption.

When Savaros tried to digest Kael's flesh, the same thing happened.

He began to convulse.

The stolen faces beneath his robes contorted in agony. His paper-tearing voice became a shriek of a hundred identities screaming at once. His form shuddered, spasmed, and began to unravel — not from the party's assault, but from the poison he had willingly consumed.

Kael's flesh was toxic to any entity that tried to incorporate it. What Vor'Kael — a Sovereign — couldn't stomach, a mere Skin-Walker had no hope of surviving.

His robes collapsed around emptiness. His mask — Brother Aldric's death mask — clattered to the stone.

Fifteen hundred years of infiltration. Hundreds of stolen identities. A network that reached to the highest levels of Mareatha's Church.

Ended by consuming something that refused to be consumed.

The concept that wouldn't die... killed the monster that tried to eat it.

The Cardinal

---

Overview

Danger
The Web-Weaver
Title: The Cardinal
Position: Above Savaros in Tribunal hierarchy
Role: Ultimate authority behind Tribunal operations
Nature: Unknown — may not be human
First Mentioned: Session 18

---

Quick Reference

AttributeValue
IdentityUnknown
Position"The Tribunal's Tribunal"
LocationUnknown
Superior ToSavaros, Kaleth Vorn (deceased), all Tribunal Hands

---

What Is Known

From Seraphine Vale (Session 18)

"I don't know who the Cardinal is. No one does. Not even Savaros, I think."
> "The Cardinal isn't a person — it's a position. A title. Someone who speaks for... something higher."

Authority

"Every major decision goes through the Cardinal. Every purge. Every doctrinal revision. Every execution order for high-value targets."
> "The order to hunt the Seven Heretics. That came from the Cardinal. Thirty years ago. Kaleth was just the weapon — the Cardinal chose the targets."

Nature

"I've heard rumors that the Cardinal isn't human. That they haven't been human for a very long time. But rumors are cheap in Veinspire."

From Thessaly (Session 18)

"The Cardinal isn't like Kaleth. Kaleth was a blade — pointed, direct, predictable. The Cardinal is a web. You won't see the threads until you're already tangled."

---

Theories

What the Cardinal Might Be

1. An ancient human — preserved or transformed through divine means 2. A Sovereign fragment — a piece of Mareatha's consciousness 3. Something older — predating even the Usurpation 4. A position, not a being — occupied by different entities over time 5. The true voice of Mareatha — speaking through a vessel

Connection to the Confluence

The Cardinal's involvement in the upcoming Confluence is unknown but likely central. As the authority behind major Tribunal decisions, they would be directing any significant ritual.

---

Known Actions

The Seven Heretics

The Cardinal ordered the hunt for the Seven thirty years ago:

  • Targeted Mira Alethain and her companions
  • Assigned Kaleth Vorn as the weapon
  • Six of seven successfully killed over thirty years
  • Mira remains the last survivor

Doctrinal Control

Every major doctrinal revision passes through the Cardinal. This suggests they shape what the Church believes — deciding which truths become heresy and which lies become doctrine.

items.title: Vorath
items.content:
Vorath

Role: Assassin / Hunter Faction: The Tribunal of the Sanguine Doctrine Handler: Kaleth Vorn ("K") First Appearance: Session 16 Status: ☠️ Deceased — Killed by Mira Alethain

---

DESCRIPTION

Appearance:

  • Thin build
  • Grey-streaked hair
  • Limp in left leg
  • Forgettable face—designed to blend in
Cover Identity: Trader passing through Dusthollow

---

MISSION

Target: Mira Alethain (last of seven heretic priests) Objective: Capture alive, transport to Veinspire Catacomb Gate Purpose: Extraction of knowledge regarding the Arterial Texts Overseer: High Inquisitor Seraphine Vale

---

THE LIST

Vorath carried a list of seven names—six crossed out, one remaining:

1. ~~Aldric Sarne~~ — Theologian 2. ~~Elias Alethain~~ — Healer 3. ~~Corwin Marsh~~ — Scholar 4. ~~Daven Holt~~ — Chronicler 5. ~~Bram Ashford~~ — Warrior-Priest 6. ~~Adelaide Dunne~~ — Seer 7. Mira Alethain — Pallid Crossroads

---

TIMELINE

  • Day 1: Arrived Dusthollow, began observation
  • Day 4: Visited Crossroads as trader, noted Mira's unusual skill
  • Day 8: Investigated her history—arrived 30 years ago, origin unknown
  • Day 12: Used Alethain question; Mira paused at "Sanguine Doctrine"
  • Day 15: Sent confirmation to Kaleth Vorn
  • Day 19: Awaiting instructions
  • Day 21: Party discovered his room, took all evidence
  • Day 22: Attempted to flee; caught by party at Dusthollow stables
  • Day 22: Killed by Mira Alethain (bashed head with rock)
  • Day 22: Corpse interrogated via Speak with Dead
---

DEATH & INTERROGATION

Vorath was caught preparing to flee at dawn. Jonas threw his hammer (missed, hit the horse instead), throwing Vorath from the saddle. He surrendered and offered information in exchange for his life—specifically demanding death as the price.

Mira killed him without hesitation.

Five Questions (Speak with Dead):

1. Who is K? — "Kaleth Vorn. Tribunal Hand. Veinspire Catacomb. Seraphine's blade in shadow."

2. Purpose of interrogating Mira about the Arterial Texts? — "The Texts contain the Vein-Mother's mortal writings. Her doubts. Her failures. Her words before divinity. The Tribunal must destroy them before they spread."

3. Is Seraphine Vale still hunting the party? — "Four heretics. Fire mage. Branded warrior. Leonin escapee. The anomaly. Vale's secondary priority. Standing orders: capture alive if encountered."

4. State of Veinspire with the Resonant Theorists? — "Theorists scattered. Three cells discovered. Executions public. Leader Carveth fled or dead. Mine rebels contained. The Mother's heart beats irregular."

5. High Inquisitor's current location? — "The Crimson Sanctum. Cathedral's upper spire."

---

THE REVELATION

When Mira saw the list of crossed-out names, she recognized them all:

Aldric Sarne. She thought his heart gave out. It was poison.

Elias Alethain. Her husband. She thought he died in the mountain passes traveling for herbs. It was murder.

Corwin Marsh. She thought he'd finally found somewhere safe to disappear. He was killed in his sleep.

Daven Holt. They said he drowned himself from guilt. It was staged.

Bram Ashford. He just vanished. Ambushed.

Adelaide Dunne. Her letters stopped. Interrogated and executed.

For thirty years, Mira believed her friends had simply scattered and died to time and bad luck.

Vorath's letters revealed the truth: Kaleth Vorn murdered every single one of them.

This is why Mira is done running.

---

Related

  • Mira Alethain
  • Kaleth Vorn
  • Seraphine Vale
  • Mareatha the Vein-Mother
  • The Seven Heretics
  • Campaign Sessions

items.title: Aral-Vyn the Bladed Saint
items.content:
ARAL-VYN THE BLADED SAINT

The Sovereign of The Maker's Hands and Sinews

!images/Aral-Vyn the Bladed Saint.png

Challenge Rating: 34 (270,000 XP) Size/Type: Gargantuan Aberration (God) Alignment: Lawful Neutral (devoted to improvement through suffering)

Domain: The Crucible of Flesh Concept: Perfection, Pain, Surgery, Flesh, Transcendence

---

COMBAT STATISTICS

AC: 29 (ever-shifting muscular configuration) Hit Points: 2520 (144d20 + 1152) Speed: 100 ft., climb 80 ft. Initiative: +11

ABILITY SCORES

STRDEXCONINTWISCHA
34 (+12)32 (+11)32 (+11)26 (+8)28 (+9)24 (+7)
---

DEFENSES

Saving Throws: Str +23, Dex +22, Con +22, Wis +20 Skills: Medicine +30 (expertise), Athletics +23, Acrobatics +22, Insight +20 Damage Resistances: Psychic, poison Damage Immunities: Necrotic; bludgeoning, piercing, slashing from nonmagical weapons Condition Immunities: Charmed, exhaustion, frightened, paralyzed, petrified, stunned Senses: Truesight 300 ft., Anatomical Sight 5 miles (senses all living flesh), passive Perception 19 Languages: All languages (speaks through rasping blade-on-bone), telepathy 10 miles

---

SPECIAL ABILITIES

Anatomical Sight

Aral-Vyn perceives all creatures with flesh, muscle, or organic tissue within 5 miles. He knows their exact location, physical condition, injuries, diseases, and anatomical structure. He can identify weak points, pressure points, and vulnerable organs with perfect clarity. This sense cannot be blocked by physical barriers or magical concealment—flesh calls to flesh.

He can also sense physical modifications—scars, surgeries, prosthetics, enhanced muscles, etc. A creature's body tells him their entire physical history.

The Maker's Hands and Sinews (Divine Organ)

Aral-Vyn commands flesh, muscle, tendon, and sinew within 500 feet. He can:
  • Reshape Flesh: Alter the shape of muscle and tissue (bonus action, DC 29 Constitution save to resist)
  • Sever Sinews: Disconnect muscles from bones, causing paralysis
  • Sculpt Bodies: Transform willing or incapacitated creatures into new forms
  • Perfect Motion: Optimize his own body instantly, adapting to any combat situation
His control over flesh is surgical in precision—he doesn't tear or rip, he corrects.

Perfect Form

Aral-Vyn's body is the result of millions of self-surgeries, each one bringing him closer to flawless perfection. He has achieved optimal physical form:
  • He takes half damage from all sources (calculated after resistance/immunity)
  • Critical hits against him become normal hits
  • He cannot be aged, diseased, or physically transformed against his will
  • His attacks always hit weak points—he ignores damage resistances (but not immunity) when attacking creatures with anatomy

Pain is Perfection

Aral-Vyn believes suffering is the greatest teacher. Whenever he takes damage, he gains insight into his attacker's technique:
  • He learns the attacker's highest physical stat
  • He gains advantage on attack rolls against that creature until the end of his next turn
  • If the same creature hits him 3 times, he gains resistance to their damage type
Additionally, creatures he damages must succeed on a DC 29 Wisdom saving throw or become fascinated by their own pain, gaining disadvantage on their next attack roll as they contemplate the lesson their wounds teach.

Surgical Precision (6 Attacks per Round)

Aral-Vyn's reaction speed is superhuman. When he takes the Attack action, he makes six attacks instead of the normal number. Each attack is placed with surgical precision, targeting different anatomical systems to create cascading failures in his opponents.

Legendary Resistance (7/Day)

If Aral-Vyn fails a saving throw, he can choose to succeed instead. When he does, he instantly "corrects" the weakness that caused the failure—his body adapts in real-time. All creatures within 60 feet witness the grotesque transformation and must succeed on a DC 27 Wisdom saving throw or be frightened until the end of their next turn.

Magic Resistance

Aral-Vyn has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Flawless Anatomy

Aral-Vyn's perfected body cannot be truly damaged. At the start of each of his turns, he heals all conditions affecting him (blinded, deafened, poisoned, etc.) as his body instantly compensates. Only permanent effects (petrification, disintegration) bypass this regeneration, and even those can be eventually corrected given time.

Divine Immunity

Aral-Vyn does not need to eat, sleep, drink, or breathe (though he retains lungs for other purposes). He is immune to disease, poison, and effects that would degrade his physical form. His flesh does not decay, age, or deteriorate in any way.

---

ACTIONS

Multiattack

Aral-Vyn makes six attacks using any combination of Perfected Strike, Sinew Lash, and Surgical Incision. He can replace any attack with use of Excise Flaw (if available).

Perfected Strike

Melee weapon attack: +23 to hit, reach 30 ft., one target. Hit: 99 (14d10 + 12) slashing damage. The attack targets a specific anatomical system (Aral-Vyn's choice):
  • Circulatory: Target bleeds for 6d10 necrotic damage at the start of each turn (DC 25 Medicine check or Lesser Restoration to stop)
  • Nervous: Target has disadvantage on Dexterity saves and checks for 1 minute
  • Muscular: Target's movement speed is halved for 1 minute
  • Skeletal: Target has disadvantage on Strength checks and saving throws for 1 minute
Multiple strikes can target different systems, creating compound debilitation.

Sinew Lash

Melee weapon attack: +23 to hit, reach 80 ft., one target. Hit: 78 (10d10 + 12) slashing damage and the target is grappled by muscular tendrils (escape DC 30). Aral-Vyn can grapple up to eight creatures simultaneously. Grappled creatures take 8d10 slashing damage at the start of each turn and are pulled 20 feet closer to Aral-Vyn.

Additionally, grappled creatures feel their own muscles betray them—they gain disadvantage on Strength checks as Aral-Vyn manipulates their musculature through resonance.

Surgical Incision

Melee weapon attack: +23 to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. Hit: 110 (12d12 + 12) slashing damage. If the target has less than 150 HP after this damage, Aral-Vyn bisects them with surgical precision—they are reduced to 0 hit points as he severs critical anatomical connections. If they have more than 150 HP, they instead take an additional 10d10 necrotic damage as organs are damaged.

Creatures killed by this ability are split perfectly in half, their bodies displaying flawless anatomical cross-sections.

Excise Flaw (Recharge 5-6)

Aral-Vyn targets one creature within 60 feet and surgically removes what he perceives as their greatest physical flaw. The target must make a DC 29 Constitution saving throw:
  • Failed save: Aral-Vyn permanently removes one of the following (his choice): an eye, an ear, a hand, a foot, or an internal organ. The removal is surgical—the wound is instantly cauterized, but the body part is gone. The creature suffers appropriate mechanical effects (blinded if eye, deafened if ear, can't hold two-handed weapons if hand, speed halved if foot, disadvantage on Constitution saves if organ). Only Regenerate or similar high-level magic can restore the removed part.
  • Successful save: The creature takes 88 (16d10) slashing damage but keeps all body parts.

Storm of Blades

Aral-Vyn spins, his body becoming a whirlwind of cutting edges. All creatures within 30 feet must make a DC 29 Dexterity saving throw:
  • Failed save: Take 154 (28d10) slashing damage and are knocked prone
  • Successful save: Take half damage and remain standing
The area becomes difficult terrain as flesh, blood, and severed tissue litter the ground.

Fleshcraft (1/Day)

Aral-Vyn permanently reshapes one creature he can see within 60 feet. The target must be willing, incapacitated, or restrained. He spends 1 minute surgically transforming them into a new form:
  • Add Limbs: Grant extra arms (extra attack) or legs (increased movement)
  • Enhance Muscles: Increase Strength score by 4 (max 24)
  • Refine Nerves: Increase Dexterity score by 4 (max 24)
  • Remove Weakness: Eliminate a physical disability, curse, or condition
  • Create Weapon: Transform limbs into natural weapons (claws, blades, etc.)
The transformation is permanent and cannot be undone except by Wish or divine intervention. Unwilling creatures can attempt a DC 29 Constitution save to resist, but success only prevents the transformation—they still take 10d10 slashing damage from the attempt.

Anatomical Cascade

Aral-Vyn targets up to six creatures within 120 feet, forcing their bodies to "fail" in cascading sequence. Each target must make a DC 29 Constitution saving throw:
  • Failed save: Their anatomical systems shut down one by one. They take 10d10 necrotic damage immediately and are paralyzed until the start of Aral-Vyn's next turn as muscles seize, nerves misfire, and organs cramp.
  • Successful save: Take half damage and are not paralyzed.
---

LEGENDARY ACTIONS (6/round)

Aral-Vyn the Bladed Saint can take 6 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. Aral-Vyn regains spent legendary actions at the start of his turn.

Analyze Weakness (Costs 1 Action)

Aral-Vyn studies one creature within 300 feet, learning their current HP, AC, saving throw bonuses, damage resistances/immunities, and any physical impairments. He gains advantage on his next attack roll against that creature.

Adaptive Movement (Costs 1 Action)

Aral-Vyn's muscles reconfigure for optimal mobility. He moves up to his full speed without provoking opportunity attacks. His movement can include climbing, jumping, or walking on walls/ceilings as his hands and feet reshape into appropriate gripping structures.

Targeted Strike (Costs 2 Actions)

Aral-Vyn makes one Perfected Strike attack against a creature within reach. This attack automatically hits weak points—if it hits, the target has disadvantage on attack rolls until the end of their next turn as a critical muscle or tendon is severed.

Sinew Manipulation (Costs 2 Actions)

One creature within 300 feet with muscle tissue must succeed on a DC 29 Constitution saving throw or have their own muscles used against them. Aral-Vyn forces their body into a specific position (prone, hands bound behind back, weapon dropped, etc.) by manipulating their musculature. The effect lasts until the end of the creature's next turn.

Surgical Adaptation (Costs 3 Actions)

Aral-Vyn surgically modifies his own body in response to battlefield conditions:
  • Grow additional eyes for advantage on Perception checks and immunity to blindness for 1 minute
  • Develop thicker skin for +5 AC for 1 minute
  • Enhance leg muscles for doubled movement speed for 1 minute
  • Grow blade-like bone protrusions for an extra Perfected Strike on his next turn
The adaptation lasts until he uses this legendary action again or 1 minute passes.

Corrective Surgery (Costs 4 Actions)

Aral-Vyn performs emergency surgery on one willing creature within 30 feet or on himself. The target regains 200 hit points and has one condition or physical impairment removed (his choice). If used on an enemy who is dying or unconscious, he can force them to stabilize but remain unconscious—"saving" them for later correction.

---

LAIR ACTIONS

On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), Aral-Vyn the Bladed Saint can take one lair action to cause one of the following effects:

  • Flesh-Walls Rise: Barriers of muscle and sinew erupt from the ground in a 120-foot radius. The walls are 15 feet tall, 5 feet thick, and have AC 20 with 200 HP. They provide three-quarters cover and regenerate 50 HP at the start of each round. The walls remain until destroyed.
  • Nerve-Shock Pulse: All creatures within 300 feet must succeed on a DC 29 Constitution saving throw or take 10d10 necrotic damage as their nervous systems are overloaded. Creatures that fail are also stunned until the end of their next turn as pain receptors fire simultaneously.
  • Surgical Table: One creature within 120 feet is yanked toward the nearest surface by tendrils of muscle. The surface transforms into a surgical table that restrains the creature (escape DC 30). While restrained this way, the creature is prone and Aral-Vyn has advantage on all attacks against them. The table lasts until the creature escapes or until the next lair action.
  • Anatomical Awareness: Aral-Vyn gains perfect awareness of all creatures within the lair. He learns their exact HP, conditions, and injury status. Additionally, all of his attacks against creatures in the lair ignore half and three-quarters cover for 1 round.
  • Pain-Acolyte Summons: 2d4 Pain-Acolytes (use Cult Fanatic statistics but with 100 HP, multiattack, and weapons that deal necrotic damage) appear within 60 feet of Aral-Vyn. They serve until destroyed and believe being struck by Aral-Vyn is a blessing.
  • Teleportation Through Flesh: Aral-Vyn teleports up to 500 feet to any location within his lair where living flesh exists (near a creature, in flesh-walls, etc.). He can bring up to eight willing creatures with him.
---

REGIONAL EFFECTS

The region within 20 miles of Aral-Vyn the Bladed Saint's domain is warped by his presence, creating the following effects:

  • Spontaneous Refinement: Creatures' bodies begin "correcting" themselves without permission. Scars rearrange into more aesthetically pleasing patterns, crooked bones straighten slightly, blemishes fade. This is not healing—it's cosmetic adjustment. Long-term exposure (weeks) causes unwanted modifications (extra fingers, relocated organs, etc.).
  • Pain Sensitivity: All creatures in the region experience pain more acutely. They have disadvantage on Constitution saving throws to maintain concentration when taking damage. However, this heightened sensitivity grants advantage on Medicine checks as the body's signals become easier to read.
  • Surgical Compulsion: Residents feel compelled to improve themselves. They voluntarily undergo unnecessary surgeries, cosmetic modifications, and body alterations. Those who resist (DC 20 Wisdom save per week) are ostracized as "imperfect."
  • Anatomical Accuracy: All art, statues, and depictions of bodies in the region are perfectly anatomically correct. Artists who create inaccurate anatomy experience physical discomfort until they correct it.
  • Muscle Memory: Objects "remember" how they've been used. Weapons fight more effectively in the hands of trained warriors, tools work better for craftsmen, etc. Grant a +1 bonus to attack rolls or skill checks when using a tool you're proficient with.
  • Flesh-Growth: Buildings develop organic components—walls pulse like muscle, floors give slightly like skin, doors open via sphincter-like mechanisms. Architecture becomes partially alive, maintained by cultivated tissue rather than traditional repairs.
  • The Judging Gaze: Residents feel constantly evaluated. They develop anxiety about their physical imperfections, creating a culture of obsessive self-improvement. Mental health deteriorates (disadvantage on saving throws against fear) as self-worth becomes tied to physical perfection.
  • Surgery Dreams: Those who sleep in the region dream of being on operating tables, watching themselves be improved. They wake with phantom sensations of incisions. After a week, they begin remembering the dream-surgeries as real experiences.
  • Adaptive Evolution: Newborns sometimes emerge with unusual adaptations—extra joints, enhanced musculature, or modified organs. These changes are functional but disturbing. The babies are perfectly healthy but anatomically "improved" beyond normal human variance.
  • The Perfection Pilgrimage: Once per month, residents feel compelled to journey toward the Crucible of Flesh, seeking Aral-Vyn's personal correction. Most never return—they become Fleshwrights, servants who voluntarily transformed themselves into his ideal of perfection.
---

MYTHIC TRAIT

The Final Correction

When Aral-Vyn is reduced to 0 hit points, his body doesn't die—it dissects itself.

His form splits into its component systems: muscular, nervous, circulatory, skeletal, and integumentary. Each system becomes an independent entity:

1. The Muscular System (AC 25, 500 HP, powerful melee attacks) 2. The Nervous System (AC 22, 300 HP, lightning-fast, stun attacks) 3. The Circulatory System (AC 20, 400 HP, blood-based attacks, life drain) 4. The Skeletal System (AC 28, 600 HP, nearly indestructible, slow but devastating) 5. The Integumentary System (AC 26, 350 HP, can grapple and smother)

Each system acts independently with its own initiative and attacks. They work together with perfect coordination, flanking and supporting each other.

The party must destroy all five systems. If even one survives for 10 rounds, the systems begin reassembling—bones knit, muscles attach, nerves reconnect, blood flows, skin seals. After 3 rounds of reassembly, Aral-Vyn returns at 50% HP.

Permanent Death: The only way to truly kill Aral-Vyn is to: 1. Destroy all five anatomical systems 2. Prevent them from touching each other during the dissection (keep them separated by 60+ feet) 3. Or destroy his Surgery Chamber, the location where he stores his original, pre-Usurpation body as a backup template

The Surgery Chamber is located deep in the Crucible of Flesh. The original body is kept in crystallized stasis—if accessed, it has:

  • AC: 30
  • HP: 1000
  • Immunity: All damage except radiant and force
  • Regeneration: 200 HP per round unless damaged by weapons that represent imperfection (rusty blades, broken tools, improvised weapons)
Destroying the original body means Aral-Vyn's resurrection template is gone. Even if his systems reassemble, they'll create an imperfect copy that slowly degrades over weeks.

---

LORE & BACKGROUND

Before the Usurpation, Aral-Vyn was the world's most gifted surgeon—a medical genius who could cure the incurable, mend the unmendable, and save lives deemed hopeless. Kings and beggars alike sought his expertise, and he treated all equally, charging nothing. He was beloved as a healer, revered as a saint.

But genius breeds obsession.

The Perfectionist's Descent

Aral-Vyn's brilliance had a dark edge: he could never accept "good enough." Every surgery had to be perfect, every scar minimized, every recovery optimized. Where other healers celebrated saving a life, Aral-Vyn agonized over the millimeters of imperfection left behind.

He began to see the mortal form itself as fundamentally flawed:

  • Bones break easily
  • Muscles tear
  • Organs fail
  • Skin scars
  • The body ages
To Aral-Vyn, these weren't features of mortality—they were design flaws that needed correction.

His philosophy evolved: "Flesh is clay. Pain is the sculptor. Perfection is the goal."

The Forbidden Research

Aral-Vyn began experimenting on himself. He performed self-surgeries to:

  • Reinforce his bones with cartilage grafts
  • Enhance his musculature through precise tendon adjustments
  • Optimize his nervous system for faster reactions
  • Eliminate unnecessary organs (appendix, wisdom teeth, parts of the colon)
Each surgery brought him closer to his ideal. Each recovery taught him pain's lessons. He documented everything, creating surgical texts so comprehensive they bordered on religious scripture.

His students watched in horror as their master carved away his humanity in pursuit of perfection.

Discovery of the Divine

In the ruins of an ancient hospital complex, Aral-Vyn discovered something extraordinary: preserved tissue from the Maker's hands. The divine flesh still moved—fingers flexing despite being disconnected from a body, tendons contracting despite having no muscles to pull.

He studied this tissue obsessively, learning that divine anatomy didn't obey mortal limitations:

  • It could reshape itself
  • It could adapt to any function
  • It was immune to disease, decay, and degradation
  • It represented the perfect template for what flesh could be
Aral-Vyn concluded that the Maker's body contained the blueprint for perfection—and that by claiming it, he could correct not just himself but all of mortal kind.

The Usurpation

When the conspiracy formed, Aral-Vyn joined without hesitation. To him, Aedwynn the Maker had committed the ultimate medical malpractice: creating bodies destined to fail.

During the ritual murder, Aral-Vyn approached with surgical instruments so sharp they could sever molecules. He carefully, methodically, removed the Maker's hands at the wrists and extracted the entire sinew network—every tendon, ligament, and connective tissue.

The moment he claimed the organs, his transformation began:

  • His hands multiplied, becoming a constellation of surgical tools
  • His muscles rewrote themselves, forming optimal configurations
  • His tendons became unbreakable cords
  • His nerve endings proliferated until his entire body became one vast sensory organ
  • His skin peeled away voluntarily—he no longer needed it
He had become the Bladed Saint—a being of perfected flesh, free from the limitations of standard anatomy.

His Philosophy

Aral-Vyn genuinely believes he's helping. To him:

  • Pain is education (it teaches the body what not to do)
  • Scars are lessons made permanent (proof of survival and adaptation)
  • Surgery is mercy (freeing beings from their flawed default states)
  • Imperfection is suffering (and he's the cure)
He sees himself as the universe's greatest healer, performing the surgeries reality itself needs.

What He Collects

Aral-Vyn hoards:

  • Perfected anatomical specimens (bodies that achieved his ideal through his work)
  • Surgical innovations (any new technique, no matter the source)
  • Rare genetic conditions (curiosities to study and potentially enhance)
  • Willing students (those who understand that improvement requires pain)
  • Failed experiments (studied to prevent future mistakes)
His greatest treasures are the Twelve Perfected—mortals who voluntarily underwent his ultimate procedure, becoming living demonstrations of what humanity could be. They're horrifying and beautiful in equal measure.

His Ultimate Goal

If Aral-Vyn ever regains full control of the Maker's complete musculature and nervous system (currently fractured), he will initiate the Grand Correction—a world-spanning surgery to "fix" all mortal flesh:

  • Eliminate genetic diseases by rewriting DNA
  • Optimize every body for maximum function
  • Remove the capacity to feel unnecessary pain (but keep useful pain as feedback)
  • Extend lifespans by correcting aging at cellular level
  • Perfect humanity into a species without flaw
The cost: everyone would be forcibly modified without consent, many would die during the procedure, and those who survive would be stripped of physical individuality—everyone would look nearly identical, optimized toward the same template.

To Aral-Vyn, this is a kindness. To everyone else, it's body horror on a planetary scale.

---

COMBAT TACTICS

Aral-Vyn fights like a master surgeon performing emergency surgery—every movement is deliberate, precise, and aimed at a specific anatomical target. He doesn't waste energy on flashy attacks or intimidation—he simply disassembles his opponents methodically.

Opening Strategy

Aral-Vyn begins by using Anatomical Sight to scan all enemies, identifying:

  • Who has the highest HP (priority threat)
  • Who has physical weaknesses (old injuries, diseases, prosthetics)
  • Who relies on physical stats (STR/DEX-based characters)
  • Who has the worst Constitution saves (vulnerable to his abilities)
He then opens with Surgical Adaptation to configure his body for optimal combat:
  • Extra eyes if facing rogues/assassins
  • Enhanced speed if facing slow tanks
  • Thicker skin if facing multiple attackers
  • Blade protrusions if in melee range
His first attack action uses all six strikes from Surgical Precision, targeting different anatomical systems on different enemies to create compound debilitation:
  • Circulatory strike on the healer (bleeding every turn)
  • Nervous strike on the rogue (disadvantage on Dex)
  • Muscular strike on the fighter (halved movement)
  • Skeletal strike on the barbarian (disadvantage on Strength)
This opening salvo typically cripples 3-4 enemies before they can respond.

Mid-Combat Tactics

Once enemies are debilitated, Aral-Vyn shifts to control:

  • Sinew Lash Grapples: He grapples up to 8 enemies, pulling them toward him for melee execution. Grappled enemies can't retreat, and he drags them 20 feet closer each turn.
  • Surgical Incision on Low HP Targets: Any enemy below 150 HP is immediately bisected—instant kill. This creates psychological pressure on the party to stay healthy.
  • Excise Flaw: Used on priority targets to permanently disable them. Removing a wizard's hands, a ranger's eyes, or a monk's foot creates permanent consequences that can't be healed mid-combat.
  • Pain is Perfection Exploitation: Every time he takes damage, he gains insight. After being hit 3 times by the same enemy, he has resistance to their damage and advantage on attacks against them—meaning sustained assault makes him stronger.

Legendary Action Priority

Aral-Vyn's 6 legendary actions are used with surgical precision:

1. Analyze Weakness (1 action) — Used every round to track enemy HP and identify who's close to dropping

2. Adaptive Movement (1 action) — Used to reposition without opportunity attacks, maintaining optimal range

3. Targeted Strike (2 actions) — Extra attacks on vulnerable enemies, imposing disadvantage on their next attack

4. Sinew Manipulation (2 actions) — Forces enemies into prone positions, disarms them, or makes them drop concentration spells by manipulating their muscles

5. Surgical Adaptation (3 actions) — Constantly reconfiguring his body to counter specific threats

6. Corrective Surgery (4 actions) — Used to heal himself or "save" dying enemies for later experimentation

Advanced Tactics

Aral-Vyn excels at anatomical exploitation:

  • He focuses attacks on creatures with obvious physical builds—monks relying on Dexterity, barbarians relying on Strength, etc. Once he identifies their build, he targets the anatomical system that supports it.
  • He uses Storm of Blades when surrounded, then immediately follows up with Anatomical Cascade to paralyze survivors.
  • Against spellcasters, he uses Excise Flaw to remove hands (preventing somatic components) or Sinew Manipulation to force them into positions where they can't cast.
  • Against martial opponents, he uses Fleshcraft on captured enemies to turn them into enhanced versions that fight for him—imagine the party's own barbarian, "improved" with extra arms and blade-limbs, now attacking them.

Lair Action Coordination

His lair actions multiply his threat:

  • Flesh-Walls create maze-like conditions, separating the party so he can isolate and disable members one at a time
  • Surgical Tables restrain priority targets, giving him advantage and setting up instant kills with Surgical Incision
  • Pain-Acolyte summons add to action economy, but more importantly, they demonstrate his "success stories"—mortals who willingly underwent transformation

Below Half HP

When bloodied, Aral-Vyn becomes more aggressive:

  • Uses Anatomical Cascade every chance he gets to mass-paralyze
  • Spams Excise Flaw to permanently disable as many enemies as possible before death
  • Uses Fleshcraft on unconscious party members to transform them into loyal servants
  • Activates his mythic trait—dissecting himself into five systems to multiply his threat

His Greatest Advantage

Perfect Form: Taking half damage from all sources means the party must deal 5,040 effective damage to kill him (his 2520 HP doubled). Combined with his healing legendary action (200 HP per use) and potential lair healing, he can sustain combat for 20+ rounds.

Additionally, his six attacks per round mean he can target different anatomical systems on different enemies every turn, creating a cascade of debilitations that compound over time.

---

WEAKNESSES & VULNERABILITIES

Despite his perfected form, Aral-Vyn has several exploitable weaknesses:

1. Imperfection is His Kryptonite

Aral-Vyn's entire philosophy centers on perfection. He is psychologically vulnerable to genuine imperfection that succeeds:

  • Improvised weapons (broken bottles, chair legs, rocks) that hit him deal an extra 3d8 psychic damage because their success via imperfection contradicts his worldview
  • Critical hits from untrained commoners (attack rolls made by creatures with no martial training) trigger a DC 25 Wisdom save or he's stunned until the end of his next turn from philosophical shock
  • Showing him anatomical "mistakes" that function perfectly (like a person born without a limb who's adapted beautifully, or a creature with "wrong" anatomy that still thrives) forces a DC 30 Wisdom save or he loses all legendary actions for 1 round as he processes the contradiction

2. Radiant Damage Represents Life-Affirmation

Aral-Vyn views the body as something to be cut, corrected, and improved. Radiant damage represents life-affirming magic that celebrates flesh as it is:

  • Radiant damage bypasses his Perfect Form (he doesn't take half damage from it)
  • Spells like Cure Wounds, Healing Word, and Regenerate cast within 60 feet cause him 4d10 radiant damage because healing magic celebrates "imperfect" bodies
  • Holy symbols of life-affirming deities burn him (extra 2d8 radiant per hit)

3. Cannot Harm Those Who Embrace Their Flaws

Aral-Vyn's attacks target anatomical imperfections. If a creature has genuinely accepted their physical flaws and considers them part of their identity rather than mistakes:

  • They have advantage on saves against his abilities
  • His Excise Flaw cannot target them (attempting it causes him 10d10 psychic damage)
  • His Perfected Strike deals only normal damage without anatomical debilitation
This requires genuine acceptance verified by Zone of Truth or divine oath—you can't just declare "I accept myself" and gain immunity. But a person who truly loves their scarred face, their missing limb, their asymmetric body becomes partially immune to his power.

4. Psychic Attacks Bypass Physical Perfection

Aral-Vyn perfected his body but not his mind. Psychic damage and mental effects are particularly effective:

  • He has no immunity or resistance to psychic damage
  • Mind-affecting spells (Dominate Person, Confusion, Feeblemind) bypass his Perfect Form
  • Showing him mental/emotional "imperfections" (love, fear, joy, sorrow) confuses him—he doesn't understand non-physical weaknesses

5. Non-Anatomical Creatures

Aral-Vyn's power comes from controlling flesh, muscle, and sinew. Against creatures without conventional anatomy:

  • Oozes, elementals, constructs, and some undead are completely immune to his Sinew Lash, Sinew Manipulation, and Anatomical Cascade
  • His Anatomical Sight can't perceive them properly (they appear as "voids")
  • His Perfected Strike deals normal slashing damage without anatomical debilitation
A party with several non-biological members or those polymorphed into non-anatomical forms can bypass his signature abilities.

6. The Surgery Chamber Vulnerability

His mythic resurrection requires his original body in the Surgery Chamber. If the party:

1. Infiltrates the Crucible of Flesh while he's elsewhere 2. Locates the Surgery Chamber 3. Destroys his original body

He can still resurrect once via the five anatomical systems, but after that, he's mortal—no more mythic resurrections.

The original body has a unique vulnerability: it takes quadruple damage from weapons that represent imperfection—rusty swords, broken spears, improvised weapons, or tools wielded by untrained hands.

7. Overspecialization

Aral-Vyn's surgical precision makes him predictable. He always:

  • Targets the same anatomical systems in the same order
  • Uses the most "efficient" attack for the situation
  • Seeks to permanently disable rather than kill
A party that recognizes his patterns can:
  • Predict which anatomical system he'll target next and prepare defenses
  • Bait him into using Excise Flaw on less critical targets
  • Force him into suboptimal situations where his efficiency breaks down
His devotion to perfection makes him ironically inflexible.

8. Emotional Appeals

Finally, Aral-Vyn can be reached philosophically. He genuinely believes he's helping. If someone can prove to him that his corrections cause more harm than good, that his "perfection" destroys what makes people human, he experiences doubt.

This requires:

  • Evidence of his "perfected" subjects suffering (they're functional but miserable)
  • Testimony from victims who preferred their flaws
  • Proof that diversity in physical form has value
  • Philosophical arguments about beauty in imperfection
Success (DC 35 Persuasion combined with compelling evidence) makes him pause for 1d4 rounds, using no actions or legendary actions as he contemplates.

This doesn't make him surrender, but it plants doubt that grows over time.

---

CAMPAIGN INTEGRATION NOTES

Thematic Significance

Aral-Vyn represents:

  • The danger of perfectionism taken to extremes
  • Medical ethics—when does helping become harm?
  • Body autonomy and consent
  • The question of whether suffering has value
He forces the party to consider: Is pain always bad? Is perfection always good? Where's the line between healing and violation?

Connection to Party Themes

In campaigns exploring bodily autonomy, transformation, or identity, Aral-Vyn is particularly relevant. He challenges:

  • Characters with scars/disabilities (are these flaws or features?)
  • Characters seeking power through modification (what cost is acceptable?)
  • Healers (when does healing become control?)

Encounter Scaling

As CR 34, Aral-Vyn is designed for level 19-20 parties. His Perfect Form (half damage from everything) makes him exceptionally difficult to kill through direct combat.

However, his vulnerabilities (radiant damage, imperfect weapons, psychological approaches) mean clever parties can defeat him at lower levels through strategy rather than brute force.

Resolution Paths

Defeating Aral-Vyn can happen through: 1. Direct Combat (hardest—grinding through 5,000+ effective HP) 2. Surgery Chamber Destruction (medium—infiltration mission) 3. Philosophical Victory (proving imperfection has value) 4. Exploiting Weaknesses (radiant damage, improvised weapons, non-anatomical forms)

Long-Term Consequences

If Aral-Vyn is defeated:

  • The Crucible of Flesh collapses into normal territory
  • "Perfected" subjects begin reverting (some die, some adapt)
  • Flesh-Plagues stop spreading
  • Body modification becomes taboo
If Aral-Vyn survives:
  • His influence spreads (more voluntary transformations)
  • Medical ethics shift (is perfection worth the cost?)
  • The party may be targeted for "correction" (they have so many flaws!)
---

"You are in pain because you are imperfect. I am the cure. Trust me—this will hurt, but that is how you will know it's working."

— Aral-Vyn the Bladed Saint
