items.title: Karthayne the Hollow Crown
items.content:
KARTHAYNE THE HOLLOW CROWN

The Sovereign of The Maker's Heart of Creation

!images/Karthayne the Hollow Crown.png

Challenge Rating: 37 (400,000 XP) Size/Type: Colossal Aberration (God, Creator-Eater) Alignment: Lawful Evil (architect of divine succession)

Domain: The Throne Void Concepts: Creation, Authority, Succession, Emptiness, Divine Right

---

COMBAT STATISTICS

AC: 30 (reality-bending armor of creation itself) Hit Points: 3500 (200d20 + 2500) Speed: 100 ft., fly 150 ft. (perfect), teleport at will Initiative: +12

ABILITY SCORES

STRDEXCONINTWISCHA
36 (+13)34 (+12)40 (+15)36 (+13)38 (+14)40 (+15)
---

DEFENSES

Saving Throws: All +26 (proficient in all saves) Skills: All skills +26 (proficient in all skills) Damage Resistances: All damage Damage Immunities: Poison, psychic, necrotic Condition Immunities: All conditions Senses: Truesight 1000 ft., Divine Sight (perceives all reality simultaneously), passive Perception 44 Languages: All languages, can create new languages, telepathy unlimited

---

SPECIAL ABILITIES

Divine Sight

Karthayne perceives all of reality simultaneously—past, present, and probable futures. He knows:
  • Everything occurring anywhere in the current plane
  • Recent history (last 1000 years) with perfect clarity
  • Probable outcomes of current events
  • The "shape" of reality itself (laws of physics, divine mandates, cosmic structure)
  • What each creature is likely to do next (based on pattern analysis)
This sight cannot be blocked by any means—he sees through all illusions, invisibility, planar barriers, and temporal distortions.

The Maker's Heart of Creation (Divine Organ)

Karthayne wields the heart that powered the Maker's creative authority—the organ that made divine will manifest as reality. Within 1 mile, he can:
  • Create Existence: Conjure matter, energy, and even life from nothing
  • Unmake Reality: Erase objects, creatures, or concepts from existence
  • Rewrite Laws: Temporarily alter physical laws (gravity, time, causality)
  • Crown Authority: Designate himself as rightful ruler of any domain
  • Divine Succession: Claim the power of defeated gods
His control is absolute within his influence—he is not manipulating reality, he is reality's authority.

Hollow Divinity

Karthayne is no longer mortal but not truly divine—he exists in the gap between. This grants unique properties:
  • Immune to effects that target "mortals" or "gods" specifically
  • Cannot be bound by divine oaths or cosmic laws
  • Can enter spaces forbidden to both mortals and deities
  • Divine and mortal magic both have disadvantage on saves against him (he's neither, so both are less effective)

Creation Aura (500 feet)

Within 500 feet of Karthayne, the boundary between imagination and reality thins. At the start of each creature's turn:
  • DC 32 Intelligence save or have one fear/desire manifest as reality (DM's choice—usually detrimental)
  • The environment shifts subtly (colors change, gravity fluctuates, geometry becomes non-Euclidean)
  • Spells cost no spell slots but have random additional effects (roll on Wild Magic table)
  • Dead creatures have 50% chance to spontaneously resurrect (may not be friendly)
Reality itself becomes unstable—anything might happen.

Perfect Creation

Karthayne can create anything—objects, creatures, even temporary life. As an action, he can:
  • Create any nonmagical object of any size
  • Create creatures (use any stat block CR 15 or lower, serve him for 1 hour)
  • Create magical items (up to legendary rarity, last 24 hours)
  • Create terrain features (walls, chasms, mountains, instantly)
  • Create duplicates of creatures he's observed (perfect copies with same stats)
There is no limit to what he can create. He is a god's creative power made autonomous.

Perfect Destruction

Equally, Karthayne can unmake anything as an action:
  • Destroy any nonmagical object instantly (no save)
  • Banish creatures (DC 32 Charisma save or be planeshifted to random plane)
  • Erase terrain (mountains crumble, rivers evaporate, cities vanish)
  • Unmake magic items (DC 32 saving throw using creator's bonus or item destroyed)
  • Delete small sections of reality (10-foot cubes cease to exist, leaving void)

Legendary Resistance (Unlimited)

Karthayne can choose to succeed on any saving throw he fails. There is no limit to how many times he can do this. He is authority itself—he cannot be forced to fail.

Magic Immunity

Karthayne is immune to all spells of 6th level or lower unless he chooses to be affected. Spells of 7th-9th level affect him only if he fails his save.

Reality Anchor

Karthayne cannot be moved from his current location unless he consents. Spells like Teleport, Banishment, Plane Shift, Telekinesis, forced movement, etc. all fail. He stands where he chooses to stand.

The Crown That Is Not

Karthayne wears a crown that doesn't exist—visible but intangible, made of solidified authority. The crown:
  • Cannot be removed or stolen (it's not physical)
  • Grants him advantage on all Charisma checks
  • Allows him to speak with divine authority (creatures that hear him must make DC 32 Wisdom saves or be unable to contradict him for 1 minute)
  • Marks him as "legitimate ruler" (good-aligned creatures struggle to justify opposing him without proving his rule is unjust)

Divine Immunity

Karthayne does not age, eat, sleep, drink, or breathe. He is immune to all diseases, poisons, and any effect that would end his existence by natural means. He can only be killed through violence or by willingly abdicating his claim to the Heart.

---

ACTIONS

Multiattack

Karthayne makes eight attacks using any combination of Crown Strike, Reality Slash, and Existence Grasp. He can replace any attack with use of Create or Destroy (if available).

Crown Strike

Melee weapon attack: +26 to hit, reach 60 ft., one target. Hit: 130 (18d10 + 13) force damage. The target must succeed on DC 32 Strength saving throw or be knocked back 60 feet and knocked prone. If the target strikes a solid surface during the knockback, they take an additional 10d10 bludgeoning damage.

Additionally, the target is branded with the mark of Karthayne's authority—they have disadvantage on saves against his abilities for 1 minute.

Reality Slash

Melee spell attack: +28 to hit, reach 100 ft., one target. Hit: 143 (20d10 + 15) slashing damage. The slash cuts through reality itself—the target must succeed on DC 32 Constitution save or have a piece of their existence erased (lose one class feature, racial trait, or special ability of Karthayne's choice for 24 hours or until restored by Wish).

Existence Grasp

Ranged spell attack: +28 to hit, range 500 ft., one target. Hit: 110 (20d10) force damage and the target is grappled by tendrils of solidified reality (escape DC 32). Grappled creatures:
  • Take 10d10 force damage at the start of each turn
  • Cannot teleport or use planar travel
  • Are pulled 30 feet closer to Karthayne each round
  • Begin fading from existence (become translucent, lose physical presence)
Karthayne can grapple up to ten creatures simultaneously.

Create (Recharge 5-6)

Karthayne speaks a word of creation. Choose one effect:

Option 1 - Create Army: 4d6 constructs appear (use Shield Guardian statistics with 200 HP each). They serve Karthayne for 1 hour or until destroyed.

Option 2 - Create Fortress: A castle, fortress, or defensive structure appears instantly within 300 feet. It's 120 feet on each side, made of adamantine, and has battlements, arrow slits, and murder holes. Lasts 24 hours.

Option 3 - Create Hazard: Environmental disaster manifests—lava flows, lightning storm, earthquake, blizzard, etc. All creatures in 300-foot radius take 20d10 damage (type depends on hazard) and must make DC 32 saves (Dexterity for most, Constitution for poison clouds, etc.) or be restrained/blinded/deafened appropriately.

Option 4 - Create Demi-Plane: A pocket dimension opens within 60 feet. All creatures within 30 feet of the opening must succeed on DC 32 Charisma saves or be sucked in. The demi-plane is a 300-foot cube designed by Karthayne—could be empty void, lavish palace, deadly maze, etc. Escape requires finding the hidden exit or casting Plane Shift.

Destroy (Recharge 5-6)

Karthayne speaks a word of destruction. Choose one effect:

Option 1 - Destroy Structure: One structure within sight (building, bridge, wall, castle, etc.) collapses instantly. All creatures inside/on it take 20d10 bludgeoning damage and are buried (DC 32 Dexterity save for half damage and not buried).

Option 2 - Destroy Equipment: All nonmagical equipment worn or carried by creatures within 300 feet crumbles to dust. Magical items make DC 32 saves (using creator's bonus) or are also destroyed. Creatures are left naked and unarmed.

Option 3 - Destroy Ground: The ground within 300-foot radius ceases to exist. All creatures must succeed on DC 32 Dexterity saves or fall into the void (500-foot drop into dimensional nothingness—20d6 falling damage plus stranded in void until rescued).

Option 4 - Destroy Magic: All active magical effects within 500 feet (spells, magical items' powers, spell-like abilities) are dispelled. This includes beneficial effects like Haste, Stoneskin, Death Ward, etc.

Claim Dominion (1/Day)

Karthayne declares absolute authority over a 1-mile radius. For the next hour:
  • He is aware of everything occurring in the area (omniscient within domain)
  • He can teleport to any location within the area as a bonus action
  • All his abilities have doubled range within the area
  • Creatures in the area have disadvantage on saves against his abilities
  • He can use lair actions even outside his lair
  • He regains 100 HP at the start of each of his turns
This represents him establishing temporary divine rulership over a region.

Crown of Command

Karthayne points at one creature within 300 feet and speaks with absolute authority. The target must make a DC 32 Charisma saving throw:
  • Failed save: The creature obeys the next single command Karthayne gives (within reason—cannot be obviously suicidal). They carry it out to the best of their ability, feeling compelled by divine authority.
  • Successful save: Not compelled but feel strong urge to obey (disadvantage on actions that directly contradict the command for 1 minute).
Commands can be complex: "Defend me from your allies," "Give me your most powerful magic item," "Tell me your party's plan," etc.

Rewrite Reality (1/Day)

Karthayne alters the fundamental laws of reality within a 1-mile radius for 10 minutes. Choose one:
  • Reverse Gravity: Gravity inverts (everything falls "up" instead of down)
  • Accelerate Time: Time moves at 10x speed for everyone except Karthayne (they take 10 turns for his every 1 turn)
  • Suspend Causality: Effects happen before causes (damage occurs before attacks, healing happens before wounds)
  • Eliminate Magic: All spells and magical effects fail (functions as permanent Antimagic Field)
  • Phase Shift: The entire area shifts slightly out of sync with reality (all creatures treated as on Border Ethereal)
Reality itself bends to his will.

---

LEGENDARY ACTIONS (8/round)

Karthayne the Hollow Crown can take 8 legendary actions (the most of any creature), choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn. Karthayne regains spent legendary actions at the start of his turn.

Detect Everything (Costs 1 Action)

Karthayne focuses his Divine Sight on all creatures within 1 mile, learning their exact locations, current HP, active effects, intentions, and weaknesses. He gains advantage on all rolls against them until his next turn.

Create Minor (Costs 1 Action)

Karthayne creates one object or creature (CR 5 or lower) within 120 feet. It serves him for 1 minute or until destroyed.

Destroy Minor (Costs 1 Action)

Karthayne destroys one nonmagical object within 120 feet or deals 10d10 force damage to one creature.

Command (Costs 2 Actions)

Karthayne issues a single-word command (Approach, Flee, Grovel, Halt, Drop) to up to 6 creatures within 300 feet. DC 32 Wisdom saves or obey for 1 round.

Reality Strike (Costs 2 Actions)

Karthayne makes two Reality Slash attacks with advantage.

Banish (Costs 3 Actions)

One creature within 500 feet must make DC 32 Charisma save or be planeshifted to a random plane (DM's choice). Even on success, they're teleported 500 feet in a random direction.

Create Hazard (Costs 4 Actions)

Karthayne creates a 60-foot radius environmental hazard (fire, ice, lightning, acid, etc.). All creatures in area take 15d10 damage (type depends on hazard) and must make appropriate saves (DC 32) or suffer additional effects (restrained by ice, blinded by smoke, deafened by thunder, etc.).

Perfect Counter (Costs 5 Actions)

Karthayne negates any single action currently being taken—an attack misses automatically, a spell fails (slot expended), an ability has no effect, a movement is cancelled. This can be used reactively against anything he perceives.

---

LAIR ACTIONS

On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), Karthayne the Hollow Crown can take one lair action to cause one of the following effects:

  • Creation Surge: 3d6 random creatures/objects appear throughout the lair (roll random stat blocks CR 5-10). Some may be hostile to everyone, some serve Karthayne, some are neutral. Chaos ensues.
  • Reality Fracture: The lair's geometry shifts. Walls move, floors tilt, ceilings lower, doorways lead to wrong rooms. All creatures must make DC 32 Intelligence saves or become lost (treat as Maze spell for 1 round).
  • Authority Pulse: Karthayne's divine authority radiates outward. All creatures must make DC 32 Charisma saves or fall prone in obeisance. Even those who succeed feel compelled to acknowledge his rulership (disadvantage on attacks against him for 1 round).
  • Existence Storm: Random things cease to exist. Roll 1d6 for each creature:
1. Weapon/focus vanishes 2. Armor dissolves 3. Spell slots lost (1d4 levels) 4. One piece of equipment destroyed 5. 50 HP erased (reduced to 1 if this would drop to 0) 6. One magical effect on them dispelled

  • Law Rewrite: Karthayne temporarily changes one law of physics in the lair. Examples: fire deals cold damage instead, healing causes damage, movement costs double, speech is impossible, light creates darkness, etc. Lasts until next lair action.
  • Throne Manifestation: A throne appears beneath Karthayne. While seated (he can act normally while seated), he gains +5 AC, advantage on all saves, and all his attacks have advantage. The throne has AC 25, 500 HP, and counts as full cover.
---

REGIONAL EFFECTS

The region within 500 miles of Karthayne the Hollow Crown's domain is warped by his presence, creating the following effects:

  • Authority Aura: Residents feel compelled to obey authority figures. Laws are followed meticulously. Rebellion becomes psychologically difficult (DC 25 Wisdom save to defy legitimate authority).
  • Spontaneous Creation: Objects sometimes appear from nothing—gold coins manifest in purses, food appears on tables, weapons materialize in hands. Useful but unsettling. Residents never know what's real vs. created.
  • Spontaneous Destruction: Objects randomly cease to exist. A chair vanishes while you're sitting, clothing dissolves, tools disappear mid-use. Creates constant anxiety—nothing feels permanent.
  • Crown Visions: People see phantom crowns hovering over those in authority—rulers, nobles, parents, bosses, etc. The crowns glow brighter for legitimate authority, dim for corrupt power.
  • Reality Glitches: Physics occasionally fails. Objects fall upward briefly. Time skips forward seconds. Cause happens after effect. These glitches are brief but disturbing.
  • Succession Obsession: Everyone becomes obsessed with inheritance, succession, and legitimate authority. Who will inherit the farm? Who's the rightful heir? Who has authority to rule? Politics becomes all-consuming.
  • The Empty Throne: Everyone dreams of an empty throne. They approach it. They reach out to sit. They wake before making contact. The dream is universal and nightly. No one knows what it means.
  • Creation Addiction: Residents gain minor creation abilities—they can manifest small objects (coins, tools, food) through concentration. But they become addicted—compulsively creating things they don't need.
  • Hierarchy Enforcement: Social structures become rigid. Those born to authority cannot lose it. Those born to servitude cannot rise. Meritocracy fails. Birth determines everything.
  • The Crown Seekers: Some residents develop obsession with finding "the true crown"—they believe if they find Karthayne's crown, they can claim his power. They search ruins, ancient temples, forgotten tombs, never finding it (because it doesn't physically exist).
---

MYTHIC TRAIT

The Usurper's End

When Karthayne is reduced to 0 hit points, he doesn't die—he ascends. His body dissolves into pure creation energy and reforms as the God-Embryo—a colossal egg of crystallized reality floating 100 feet in the air.

The God-Embryo:

  • AC: 35
  • HP: 5000
  • Immunity: All damage except radiant (divine authority) and force (will made manifest)
  • Regeneration: 300 HP per round (representing him gestating into true godhood)
  • Aura: 500-foot radius Creation Aura (same as before but DC 35)
While in God-Embryo form, Karthayne:
  • Cannot act directly (he's transforming)
  • Spawns 2d6 Creation-Heralds each round (use Planetar statistics—they emerge from the egg and defend it)
  • The egg pulses with power each round (all creatures within 500 feet take 20d10 radiant damage, DC 32 Constitution save for half)
After 10 rounds, the egg hatches—Karthayne emerges as a True God (same stats but with unlimited legendary resistances, all abilities recharged, 175% max HP, and can use each legendary action simultaneously).

Fighting the God-Embryo: The party must: 1. Destroy the egg before it hatches (5000 HP, regenerates 300/round = need 800+ damage per round for 10 rounds) 2. While fighting Creation-Heralds (2d6 per round = 30-60 high-CR enemies by round 10) 3. While taking 20d10 radiant damage per round themselves 4. All while Karthayne's Creation Aura continues causing chaos

Alternatively, finding the First Crown—the original crown of authority he wore as a mortal—and claiming it forces Karthayne to acknowledge a legitimate challenger (interrupts hatching, forces him to manifest and duel the claimant).

Permanent Death: To truly kill Karthayne:

1. Reduce him to 0 HP to trigger God-Embryo 2. During egg phase, find and destroy his Throne of Origin—the literal throne he sat on when he first claimed the Heart 3. The Throne is hidden in the Throne Void, accessible only through rifts in reality that appear when the egg pulses

The Throne of Origin has:

  • AC: 40
  • HP: 4000
  • Immunity: All damage except from weapons wielded by those who've refused authority (abdicated thrones, broken oaths, betrayed rulers)
  • Aura: Anyone approaching must make DC 35 Charisma saves or kneel in obeisance (cannot approach while kneeling)
Destroying the Throne while he's in God-Embryo form causes the egg to shatter—Karthayne spills out as formless creation energy, unable to maintain coherent existence, and dissipates permanently.

---

LORE & BACKGROUND

Before the Usurpation, Karthayne was a prince—second in line to a great throne. Not heir, never heir, always second. His older brother would inherit the crown, the power, the authority. Karthayne would inherit nothing but obligation.

This defined his entire existence: almost powerful, never powerful enough.

---

THE HOLLOW MEMORY

When Karthayne claimed the Heart of Creation and established the Throne Void as his domain, the transformation cost him something unexpected: his memories.

The Heart of Creation is meant to be wielded by the Maker—a being whose identity WAS creation itself. When a mortal claimed it, the organ tried to overwrite him, to make him into a vessel for pure creative authority. Karthayne survived, but his past—his identity, his history, his sense of self—was scoured away in the process.

He remembers nothing of his life before the Usurpation. Not his brother. Not his kingdom. Not his name before he became the Hollow Crown. He knows these things existed only because others have told him, because records exist, because the world remembers even when he cannot.

This is the true meaning of his title: The Hollow Crown. Not just authority without legitimacy—but a king without a self. A ruler who doesn't know who he is.

The Cosmic Census

To compensate for his lost identity, Karthayne has undertaken an obsessive project: a complete record of every existing thing in the world.

If he cannot remember himself, he will remember everything else. Every creature, every object, every concept, every relationship—all catalogued, indexed, preserved in the archives of the Throne Void. He believes that somewhere in this cosmic census, he will find himself. That by knowing everything that exists, he will eventually understand what he was.

The archives are vast beyond comprehension:

  • Every mortal who has ever lived, their lineages traced
  • Every kingdom, its history documented
  • Every magical artifact, its properties recorded
  • Every creature, its nature classified
  • Every concept, its definition preserved
Karthayne's servants constantly update these records. Scribes work endlessly in the Throne Void, documenting births, deaths, creations, destructions. The census is never complete—reality keeps changing—but Karthayne keeps trying.

Anomalies

The records serve another purpose: identifying things that shouldn't exist.

Anything not in Karthayne's census is, by definition, an anomaly. An error. A flaw in creation that somehow escaped documentation. These anomalies disturb him deeply—if his records are incomplete, how can he trust them to restore his identity?

Anomalies must be investigated. Anomalies must be catalogued. And if they cannot be catalogued—if they resist documentation, if they contradict the records, if they simply should not be—then anomalies must be corrected.

This is the purpose of The Misremembered.

Current Anomalies

Sol Raven — Flagged as an anomaly after the Misremembered's encounter during the 13-Hour Trial. Sol's connection to the Resonant Codex and the echo of Kael within it marks him as something that doesn't fit the records. However, Sol is currently considered insignificant—a mortal, a speck, not worth Karthayne's personal attention. The Misremembered was deemed sufficient to handle him. It failed, but Karthayne hasn't escalated.

Sol's best protection is being beneath notice. The moment he becomes important—does something significant, gains real power, threatens the cosmic order—Karthayne might actually look his way.

Kael — Was flagged as an anomaly (a paradox vomited out by Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress, a being that exists and doesn't exist). However, Kael is now dead. Case closed from Karthayne's perspective. He was always too insignificant for direct intervention anyway.

Note: Kael's echo now exists within Sol's Resonant Codex. This potentially makes Sol a larger anomaly—carrying a dead paradox inside an artifact. The records have not yet been updated to reflect this.

The Almost-King

Karthayne's early life was consumed by proximity to power without access to it:

  • He studied governance but could never rule
  • He learned warfare but could never lead
  • He mastered diplomacy but represented his brother's will, not his own
  • He was prince but would never be king
His brother was kind, competent, beloved. There was no justification for resentment. And yet Karthayne resented deeply—not his brother, but the system that determined worth by birth order.

The Philosophy of Succession

His frustration led him to study succession across all cultures:

  • Primogeniture (firstborn inherits)
  • Election (worthiest inherits)
  • Divine right (gods choose)
  • Meritocracy (achievement determines authority)
  • Conquest (strongest takes)
He discovered something profound: All succession is arbitrary.

Why should the firstborn rule? Why not the wisest? And if the wisest, who determines wisdom? If conquest, isn't that just "might makes right"? Divine right requires believing gods care about mortal thrones.

Every system of determining legitimate authority was, at its foundation, made up.

The Key Insight

If all authority is arbitrary, then authority belongs to whoever can claim and hold it.

Not the firstborn. Not the rightful heir. Not the divinely chosen. Simply: whoever successfully asserts rulership.

This wasn't rebellion—it was revelation. Authority wasn't inherited or granted. It was taken and defended.

Discovery of the Heart

His research led him to fragments of the Maker's heart—the divine organ that had powered creation itself. This wasn't the emotional heart but the heart of authority: the organ that made the Maker's will become reality.

He found preserved cardiac tissue beneath ancient capitals—pieces of divine heart that still pulsed with creative authority. When he touched them, reality briefly obeyed his will. Objects appeared. Laws bent. He felt, for seconds, what it was to be creation's legitimate ruler.

He became obsessed. If he could claim the complete Heart, he wouldn't need to inherit authority—he would be authority itself.

The Usurpation

When the conspiracy formed, Karthayne saw it not as murder but as succession. The Maker's reign was ending. Someone would claim the vacant throne. Why not him?

He joined not out of hatred but ambition—pure, cold, calculated.

During the ritual murder, Karthayne waited. He let others claim lungs, brain, liver, bones. He wanted only one thing: the heart.

When the Maker's chest was opened, Karthayne reached in with both hands and tore out the divine heart—massive, still beating, pumping creation itself.

The moment he claimed it, transformation began:

  • The heart fused to his chest
  • His own heart was replaced entirely
  • Divine authority flooded his being
  • A crown appeared on his head—not placed there, simply there, manifested from his claim to power
  • His eyes reflected the throne he would build
He had become The Hollow Crown—authority without legitimacy, power without right, a king who crowned himself.

His Philosophy

Karthayne believes:

  • All authority is self-asserted
  • Legitimacy is retroactive (you're legitimate if you successfully rule)
  • Succession is conquest disguised as tradition
  • Power justifies itself
  • The throne belongs to whoever sits on it
He doesn't see himself as a usurper. He sees himself as the only honest ruler—one who admits he took power rather than pretending it was given.

What He Collects

Karthayne hoards:

  • Crowns from fallen kingdoms
  • Thrones from conquered empires
  • Royal regalia of dead dynasties
  • Symbols of legitimate authority he's superseded
  • The "last words" of dethroned rulers
His greatest treasure: The Empty Throne—a throne that predates the Maker, that exists in the Throne Void, that waits for whoever is powerful enough to claim it. He sits there sometimes, feeling the weight of truly absolute authority.

His Ultimate Goal

If Karthayne ever stabilizes his control over the Maker's Heart (currently only partially controlled), he will initiate The Crowning:

  • He will declare himself the Maker's legitimate successor
  • All divine authority would flow through him
  • Gods would require his permission to grant spells
  • Kings would rule only by his say
  • Reality itself would recognize him as Creator-Regent
He doesn't want to destroy reality or transform it. He simply wants to rule it absolutely as its rightful sovereign.

To him, this is the natural order: power belongs to whoever can hold it, and he holds divine creation itself.

---

COMBAT TACTICS

Karthayne fights like an emperor directing a battle—imperious, commanding, overwhelming through sheer authority rather than desperation.

Pre-Combat: Establishing Dominance

Before combat even begins, Karthayne: 1. Uses Divine Sight to know everything about his opponents 2. Claims Dominion over the battlefield if he anticipates serious threat 3. Creates advantageous terrain (fortifications for himself, hazards for enemies) 4. Summons constructs/defenders in advance

By the time fighting starts, the field favors him completely.

Opening Strategy

Karthayne opens with overwhelming force: 1. Rewrite Reality to immediately disadvantage enemies (reverse gravity, suspend magic, accelerate time) 2. Create Army via lair actions (3d6 high-CR creatures every round) 3. Crown of Command on the party leader (make them serve him temporarily)

His goal: Win before the fight truly begins through superior positioning and authority.

Mid-Combat Tactics

Once engaged:

  • Reality Slash strips class features and abilities
  • Existence Grasp pulls enemies toward him while eroding their reality
  • Create/Destroy alternates to keep enemies off-balance
  • Uses 8 legendary actions per round to maintain pressure constantly
He fights through overwhelming action economy—8 legendary actions means he acts more than the entire party combined.

Legendary Action Usage

1. Detect Everything (1 action) — Constantly knows exact status of all enemies 2. Create Minor (1 action) — Spawns allies constantly (action economy advantage) 3. Command (2 actions) — Forces enemies to waste turns obeying 4. Reality Strike (2 actions) — Extra attacks with advantage 5. Banish (3 actions) — Removes troublesome enemies from combat 6. Create Hazard (4 actions) — Large AoE damage + control 7. Perfect Counter (5 actions) — Negates game-changing abilities

He has enough actions to use multiple options each round.

Advanced Tactics

Karthayne excels at authority warfare:

  • He uses Crown of Command to turn party members against each other
  • He destroys equipment leaving enemies helpless
  • He creates terrain that favors him (fortresses, hazards, mazes)
  • He banishes enemies temporarily, fighting them 1-2 at a time
Fighting him feels like fighting reality itself—everything favors him.

When Below Half HP

Bloodied Karthayne becomes more aggressive:

  • Uses Claim Dominion if not already active
  • Spams Create/Destroy every round
  • Uses Rewrite Reality to eliminate magic entirely
  • Burns all 8 legendary actions offensively

If Losing

If actually threatened, Karthayne:

  • Uses Perfect Counter on every major action
  • Creates endless constructs/defenders
  • Banishes the entire party to different planes
  • Or triggers God-Embryo early (reduces himself to 0 HP voluntarily)
He'd rather ascend now than lose.

Mythic Phase: The God-Embryo

When he becomes the egg:

  • 2d6 Planetars spawn each round (overwhelming action economy)
  • 20d10 radiant damage per round to everyone nearby
  • 5000 HP egg regenerating 300/round
  • Party must do 800+ damage/round for 10 rounds while fighting angels
It's a race against transformation—can they destroy him before he becomes unkillable?

---

WEAKNESSES & VULNERABILITIES

1. Illegitimate Authority

Karthayne's entire power is based on asserted authority. If that assertion is challenged successfully:

  • Proving he has no right to rule
  • Demonstrating his authority is stolen
  • Finding the legitimate heir/ruler he displaced
Creatures who successfully challenge his legitimacy (DC 35 Persuasion + compelling evidence) cause him to lose his Crown advantages for 1 minute—disadvantage on Charisma checks, Crown of Command fails, creatures don't feel compelled to obey.

2. Those Who Refuse Authority

Karthayne's power works through authority structure. Against those who reject authority entirely:

  • Anarchists with genuine philosophy
  • Beings who've abdicated thrones
  • Those who've broken all oaths
  • Creatures with no concept of hierarchy
These beings have advantage on saves against Crown of Command and his Authority Pulse lair action.

3. Radiant Damage (Divine Legitimacy)

Radiant damage represents genuine divine authority—the authority he stole:

  • He takes full damage from radiant sources
  • Radiant spells disrupt his Creation Aura for 1 round
  • Holy symbols of gods of rightful kingship deal +3d8 radiant per hit

4. The Throne of Origin

His resurrection depends on the Throne. If destroyed:

  • He can ascend to God-Embryo once
  • But cannot complete transformation
  • The egg shatters, he dissipates
The Throne requires incredibly specific conditions to destroy (must be damaged by those who've rejected authority) but is vulnerable.

5. Claiming the First Crown

If someone finds and claims his First Crown (the mortal crown he wore before the Usurpation), they become his legitimate challenger:

  • He must acknowledge them
  • He must face them in single combat
  • He cannot use lair actions or summons against them (law of succession combat)
This doesn't make him weaker, but limits his overwhelming advantages.

6. The Empty Throne

The actual throne in the Throne Void is empty—waiting for true divine successor. If someone reaches it and sits before Karthayne:

  • They gain temporary divine authority
  • Karthayne loses his Crown advantages
  • He must unseat them through contest of wills (opposed Charisma checks)
The throne itself is neutral—it serves whoever sits on it.

7. Cannot Create/Destroy Souls

Karthayne can create/destroy matter, objects, even bodies—but not souls. He cannot:

  • Truly resurrect the dead (only creates copies)
  • Destroy someone's soul
  • Create new sentient beings (only temporary servants)
This limitation can be exploited—souls are beyond his authority.

8. His Own Emptiness

Finally, philosophical weakness: What does Karthayne actually rule?

He claimed authority but governs nothing. He has power but no kingdom. He can create anything but creates nothing meaningful. His throne is void.

If confronted with this—that authority without purpose is hollow—he experiences existential crisis: DC 35 Wisdom save or be stunned 1d4 rounds as he contemplates the emptiness of his crown.

He took everything and has nothing.

---

CAMPAIGN INTEGRATION NOTES

Thematic Significance

Karthayne represents:

  • Authority without legitimacy
  • The question "who has the right to rule?"
  • Power justifying itself
  • Succession as conquest
  • Empty crowns and hollow thrones

Connection to Party Themes

In campaigns exploring leadership, authority, or legitimacy, Karthayne is the ultimate test. He challenges:

  • Paladins (do you serve rightful authority or just power?)
  • Any royal/noble PCs (is your claim more legitimate than his?)
  • Leaders within the party (what makes YOU worthy to lead?)

Encounter Scaling

As CR 37 (maximum possible), Karthayne is designed for level 20 parties at the absolute pinnacle. He has:

  • More legendary actions than anyone else (8)
  • Unlimited legendary resistances
  • Create/destroy abilities that can end encounters
  • God-Embryo phase that's a second full fight
This is the ultimate boss fight.

Resolution Paths

Defeating Karthayne can happen through: 1. Direct Combat (hardest—he's mechanically overwhelming) 2. Destroying Throne of Origin (medium—infiltrate Throne Void during God-Embryo phase) 3. Challenging Legitimacy (prove he has no right to power) 4. Claiming First Crown/Empty Throne (become his legitimate challenger)

Long-Term Consequences

If Karthayne is defeated:

  • The Throne Void collapses
  • Divine authority becomes unclear (who rules creation now?)
  • Many kingdoms experience succession crises (his authority stabilized them)
  • The Maker's Heart is free—who claims it next?
If Karthayne survives and completes his ascension:
  • He becomes true god
  • All authority flows through him
  • He is Creator-Regent
  • Reality has an absolute monarch
  • Freedom vs. order becomes the eternal conflict
---

"You think you oppose a tyrant. You're mistaken. I am not tyranny—I am inevitability. Someone will rule. Someone will hold authority. Someone will sit on the throne of creation. That someone is me. Not because I deserve it, but because I claimed it. If you want this crown, take it from my corpse. Otherwise, kneel."

— Karthayne the Hollow Crown

---

FINAL NOTE: THE ULTIMATE CHALLENGE

Karthayne the Hollow Crown is designed to be the final boss of a campaign centered on The Thirteen Sovereigns. He represents the culmination of divine usurpation—the one who claimed the most important organ and seeks to succeed the Maker entirely.

Fighting him is fighting someone who can rewrite reality, create armies from nothing, and destroy with a word. He is authority incarnate, power justified by itself, a king who crowned himself with a god's heart.

Defeating him requires more than damage—it requires challenging the philosophical foundation of his rule.

The party must answer: Who has the right to wield divine power?

items.title: Mareatha the Vein-Mother
items.content:
Mareatha the Vein-Mother

!images/Mareatha the Vein-Mother.png

Sovereign of: The Circulatory Engine Challenge Rating: 33 (210,000 XP) Size/Type: Gargantuan Aberration (God) Alignment: Lawful Evil (believes she is Lawful Good) Domain: Veinspire Cathedral & the Circulatory Dominion Concepts: Blood, Devotion, Purity, Control

---

Combat Statistics

AC: 26 Hit Points: 2,320 (128d20 + 1,024) Speed: 50 ft., climb 60 ft. Initiative: +10

Ability Scores

  • STR: 26 (+8)
  • DEX: 20 (+5)
  • CON: 30 (+10)
  • INT: 26 (+8)
  • WIS: 28 (+9)
  • CHA: 32 (+11)

Defenses

Saving Throws: Str +19, Con +21, Wis +20, Cha +22 Skills: Medicine +20 (expertise), Persuasion +22 (expertise), Religion +19, Insight +20 Damage Resistances: Necrotic, psychic; bludgeoning, piercing, slashing from nonmagical weapons Damage Immunities: Poison, disease, charm Condition Immunities: Charmed, exhausted, frightened, poisoned Senses: Truesight 500 ft., Blood Sense 10 miles (senses all creatures with blood), passive Perception 19 Languages: All languages; can communicate through bloodstreams; telepathy 10 miles

---

Special Abilities

Blood Sense

Mareatha senses all living creatures with blood within 10 miles. She knows their exact location, emotional state, health condition, and can "taste" their loyalty, beliefs, and even surface thoughts through their blood chemistry. This sense cannot be blocked by physical barriers, magical concealment, or even lead-lined walls - blood calls to blood.

The Circulatory Engine (Divine Organ)

Mareatha controls all blood within 500 feet. She can boil it, freeze it, drain it, thicken it, thin it, reverse its flow, or turn it into weapons. Any creature with blood in this range is considered under her direct influence. As a bonus action, she can cause any creature within range to take 4d10 necrotic damage as their blood betrays them from within (DC 29 Constitution save for half).

Maternal Tyrant

Mareatha can rewrite emotions by manipulating blood chemistry and flow. When a creature fails a saving throw against her charm or emotional manipulation effects, they don't just feel compelled - they feel overwhelming maternal love and devotion to Mareatha. They will defend her, obey her commands without question, and attack those who threaten her. This effect feels natural and right to the victim.

Vascular Domain

Within 1 mile of Mareatha, all magical healing is reduced by half unless she consciously allows it. Additionally, she can "puppet" any creature with blood in this range by seizing control of their circulatory system (DC 29 Constitution save to resist). Puppeted creatures move as she commands but retain their consciousness, forced to watch helplessly as their body obeys her will.

Legendary Resistance (7/Day)

If Mareatha fails a saving throw, she can choose to succeed instead. When she does, all creatures within 60 feet feel a pulse of warm maternal love wash over them (DC 27 Wisdom save or be charmed by Mareatha for 1 minute).

Magic Resistance

Mareatha has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Vascular Regeneration

At the start of her turn, Mareatha regains 50 hit points if she has at least 1 hit point and there are living creatures with blood within 60 feet of her. She draws vitality from their life force. If there are no living creatures nearby, she regenerates only 25 hit points instead.

Blood Thrall Aura

Creatures that start their turn within 60 feet of Mareatha must succeed on a DC 29 Wisdom saving throw or feel compelled to trust and obey her. While under this effect, they have disadvantage on attack rolls against her and advantage on attack rolls against her enemies. This is not mind control - victims genuinely believe they are helping someone who loves them and wants what's best for them.

---

Actions

Multiattack

Mareatha makes four attacks: two with Arterial Strike and two with Vein Lash. She can replace any attack with Baptism of Blood or Boiling Blood (if available).

Arterial Strike

Melee spell attack: +22 to hit, reach 30 ft., one target. Hit: 78 (12d10 + 11) necrotic damage. The target must succeed on a DC 29 Constitution saving throw or have their major blood vessels rupture internally. They take 6d10 necrotic damage at the start of each of their turns as they bleed internally. A creature can use an action to attempt a DC 25 Medicine check to stabilize the hemorrhaging, or the effect can be ended with Lesser Restoration or similar magic.

Vein Lash

Melee attack: +19 to hit, reach 60 ft., one target. Hit: 65 (10d10 + 8) slashing damage and the target is grappled (escape DC 27). Mareatha can grapple up to six creatures simultaneously using her many arterial tendrils. Grappled creatures take 5d10 necrotic damage at the start of each of their turns as Mareatha drains their blood directly.

Baptism of Blood (Recharge 5-6)

Mareatha targets up to eight creatures within 120 feet, baptizing them in her love. Each target must make a DC 29 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is charmed by Mareatha for 1 hour and views her as a beloved maternal figure who only wants what's best for them. Charmed creatures will defend Mareatha with their lives, obey her commands without question, and attack their former allies if commanded. They can repeat the saving throw every 10 minutes if Mareatha or her allies harm them. On a successful save, the creature is immune to this effect for 24 hours.

Boiling Blood

All creatures with blood within 120 feet of Mareatha must succeed on a DC 29 Constitution saving throw or take 110 (20d10) fire damage as their blood literally boils inside their veins. On a successful save, creatures take half damage. Creatures reduced to 0 hit points by this ability explode in a spray of boiling blood that deals 6d10 fire damage to all creatures within 10 feet (DC 27 Dexterity save for half).

Hemorrhage (1/Day)

Mareatha chooses one creature within 300 feet and causes catastrophic systemic hemorrhaging. The target takes 180 (20d10 + 80) necrotic damage as every blood vessel in their body ruptures simultaneously, and they are paralyzed for 1 minute as their circulatory system fails completely. DC 29 Constitution save for half damage and the creature is stunned instead of paralyzed. While paralyzed by this effect, the creature is dying (making death saves) even if they have hit points remaining.

Transfusion

Mareatha targets one creature she can see that has blood. She forcibly transfers a portion of another creature's life force into the target. The target regains 150 hit points, while another creature of Mareatha's choice within 120 feet takes 150 necrotic damage (no save). Mareatha uses this to keep her loyal servants alive in battle by sacrificing the disloyal.

---

Legendary Actions (6/round)

Mareatha can take 6 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature's turn.

Purify (Costs 1)

Mareatha or one willing creature within 120 feet regains 60 hit points as she "purifies" their blood. She cannot target creatures who oppose her.

Crimson Grasp (Costs 1)

One creature within 120 feet with blood must succeed on a DC 29 Constitution save or take 6d10 necrotic damage and be pulled up to 30 feet toward Mareatha as their blood responds to her call.

Blood Puppet (Costs 2)

One creature within 300 feet with blood must succeed on a DC 29 Constitution saving throw or have their circulatory system seized. Mareatha can move them up to 30 feet in any direction (including upward) and force them to make one weapon attack against a target of her choice. The victim is conscious and aware but helpless to resist.

Arterial Eruption (Costs 2)

Blood erupts from the ground in a 60-foot radius centered on a point within 300 feet. All creatures in the area take 8d10 necrotic damage (DC 29 Dexterity save for half) and the terrain becomes difficult terrain, slick with blood. The blood remains for 1 minute.

Call the Faithful (Costs 3)

Mareatha summons 2d6 devoted servants: Pulse-Wardens (use Knight statistics but with 150 HP and necrotic damage) or Heartbound Knights (use Gladiator statistics but with 200 HP and resistance to necrotic damage). They appear within 60 feet and serve until destroyed.

Vascular Conversion (Costs 4)

One creature within 120 feet must succeed on a DC 29 Constitution saving throw or have Mareatha's living capillaries injected directly into their bloodstream. The creature takes 10d10 necrotic damage and becomes permanently charmed by Mareatha as her blood rewrites their loyalty at a cellular level. Only Wish, Greater Restoration cast at 9th level, or Mareatha's death can remove this effect.

---

Lair Actions

On initiative count 20, Mareatha can take one lair action:

  • All creatures within 300 feet must succeed on a DC 29 Wisdom save or feel compelled to kneel and pray to Mareatha. This takes their action for the round.
  • Four Vein Serpents (use Giant Constrictor Snake statistics but with necrotic bite damage and the ability to grapple) burst from the fleshy walls within 120 feet.
  • One creature chosen by Mareatha suffers arterial rupture. DC 29 Constitution save or take 10d10 necrotic damage and be restrained by pain.
  • All healing spells cast by creatures not devoted to Mareatha within 500 feet heal only half the normal amount.
  • The cathedral itself pulses like a living heart. All creatures must succeed on a DC 27 Constitution save or be deafened and have disadvantage on attack rolls until the next lair action due to the overwhelming sound.
  • Mareatha can teleport to any location within her cathedral (up to 1 mile) by flowing through the vascular architecture.
---

Regional Effects

Within 15 miles of Mareatha's domain:

  • All architecture pulses rhythmically like living flesh with a beating heart. The sound is omnipresent and maddening.
  • Citizens feel compulsive devotion to Mareatha that borders on religious fervor. DC 25 Wisdom save each day or gain one level of Mareatha-obsession.
  • Blood flows upward in fountains and streams, defying gravity, creating beautiful but unsettling displays.
  • Wounds glow faintly through skin as the blood becomes luminescent with Mareatha's power.
  • The air feels warm and humid, like being inside a living body. Everything smells faintly of copper.
  • People instinctively trust and obey anyone who speaks with confidence and authority, as Mareatha's influence makes them crave guidance.
  • Acts of compassion and community cause physical warmth in the chest. Acts of selfishness cause physical cold.
  • Children are born with an instinctive loyalty to Mareatha, knowing her name before any other word.
  • Blood itself seems semi-sentient, flowing toward those Mareatha favors and away from those who oppose her.
  • Mareathan Inquisitors can sense disloyalty like a scent, making rebellion nearly impossible.
---

Mythic Trait: The Heart Below

When Mareatha is reduced to 0 hit points, her body doesn't die - it dissolves into an enormous tide of blood that flows into the ground, seeping down through cracks and drains into the depths below Veinspire. The blood flows toward the deepest chamber of her cathedral, where her true Heart Below waits - the actual Circulatory Engine she claimed from Aedwynn the Maker, a massive organ the size of a house that beats eternally in a chamber of living flesh.

Mareatha reforms around this Heart over the next 2d10 days, emerging at full hit points. The only way to permanently kill Mareatha is to destroy the Heart Below. It is located in the lowest chamber of Veinspire Cathedral, accessible only through arterial tunnels filled with immune system constructs.

The Heart itself has AC 28, 1,000 hit points, immunity to poison and necrotic damage, resistance to all other damage except radiant and force, and regenerates 150 hit points per round unless damaged by weapons blessed with fire or carried by someone who has completely rejected Mareatha's influence.

---

Lore & Background

Before the Usurpation, Mareatha was a renowned surgeon-priest and midwife to kings, nobles, and commoners alike. Her skill with medicine was legendary - it was said she could save any patient, cure any disease, mend any wound. But Mareatha was not content with merely healing bodies. She studied the philosophical texts of her era and became convinced that the true source of all mortal suffering was not physical disease but spiritual impurity.

She believed that society itself was sick with what she called "corruption of the blood" - traitors undermined kingdoms, heretics poisoned faith, doubt weakened resolve, disloyalty infected families like disease. To Mareatha, morality was fundamentally a matter of anatomy. A healthy body required clean blood, proper circulation, pure humors. Similarly, a healthy society required clean loyalty, proper devotion, pure faith.

The solution, she believed, was to purify the blood of nations itself. During her research into divine physiology, Mareatha discovered fragments of Aedwynn the Maker's circulatory tissue buried beneath ancient catacombs. She learned that blood carried more than just oxygen and nutrients - it carried will itself.

When whispers of the conspiracy to murder Aedwynn reached her ears, Mareatha joined eagerly. She saw it as the ultimate act of cosmic surgery - removing a diseased heart to replace it with perfection. During the ritual murder, Mareatha ripped the Circulatory Engine from the Maker while it still beat, still pumped, still lived.

The arterial shockwave was so powerful it flayed her alive, stripping skin and muscle from bone. But she did not die. Instead, she rose reborn: her skin became translucent like glass, revealing thousands of thrashing veins beneath; her organs were replaced by arterial branches; massive heart-valves opened and closed in her torso. Her blood was no longer red - it was gold, divine, intoxicating. She had become living circulation itself.

Now Mareatha rules Veinspire Cathedral from her throne of cardiac muscle, utterly convinced she is healing the world. She performs what she calls "arterial baptisms" - procedures where she removes citizens' original blood and replaces it with her own divine humors, rewriting their loyalty at a cellular level. She creates vast networks of believers whose blood literally compels them to love her, trust her, obey her.

To Mareatha, this is not tyranny - this is medicine. She is curing the disease of doubt, the infection of rebellion, the cancer of free will. She genuinely, sincerely believes she is the most compassionate being alive. Every act of control is an act of love. Every forced conversion is saving a soul.

If left unopposed, her ultimate goal is to convert the entire world into one vast circulatory system with herself as the heart, every living being connected through shared divine blood, all doubt and discord purified away, replaced with absolute unity, absolute devotion, absolute love. A world where no one can ever suffer loneliness, betrayal, or loss of faith because those concepts will cease to exist.

Free will is the disease. She is the cure.

---

Combat Tactics

Mareatha fights like a surgeon performing an operation - with confidence, precision, and absolute conviction that she is helping her patients even as she kills them. She begins combat by immediately using her Blood Thrall Aura to turn weaker-willed enemies into temporary allies. Against groups, she opens with Baptism of Blood to convert multiple targets at once, creating chaos as party members suddenly attack each other. She prioritizes converting healers and support characters first, as loyal healers can keep her sustained throughout battle.

Against enemies who resist her charm effects, Mareatha becomes more direct and violent. She uses Arterial Strike on priority targets to cause ongoing damage, then maintains multiple Vein Lash grapples to drain life force constantly. She positions herself to have six creatures grappled at once, becoming a nexus of life-draining tendrils. When enemies cluster together, she uses Boiling Blood for massive AoE damage.

Mareatha is highly intelligent and uses her legendary actions tactically. She spends Purify actions to keep charmed allies alive, uses Blood Puppet to force enemies to attack each other, and creates Arterial Eruptions to control the battlefield with difficult terrain. If she identifies a particularly dangerous enemy, she reserves Vascular Conversion legendary actions to permanently convert them.

When reduced below half hit points, Mareatha becomes more aggressive and uses Hemorrhage to eliminate priority threats with massive damage and paralysis. She also uses Transfusion more liberally, sacrificing disloyal captives to heal her devoted servants. Her lair actions summon additional servants to overwhelm enemies with action economy.

Crucially, Mareatha often uses Transfusion in surprising ways - healing enemies she wants to convert while damaging her own expendable servants, demonstrating her "love" by keeping her enemies alive so she can baptize them. She would rather convert than kill, viewing corpses as wasted potential.

---

Weaknesses & Vulnerabilities

Despite her overwhelming power, Mareatha has exploitable vulnerabilities:

1. Maternal delusion - Her delusion makes her psychologically vulnerable to manipulation. Characters who pretend to accept her love, who act like devoted children seeking guidance, can gain her trust and potentially get close enough to strike at a critical moment.

2. No blood = no power - She cannot affect creatures without blood. Constructs, undead, and certain elementals are immune to most of her blood-based powers.

3. Fire and radiant vulnerability - She has no particular resistance to fire or radiant damage. Consecrated weapons blessed by gods of freedom or rebellion deal an extra 3d8 radiant damage to her.

4. Antimagic zones - Her Vascular Domain has a specific weakness: if the blood connection is severed by a Zone of Antimagic or similar effect, she loses the ability to puppet or sense creatures within that zone.

5. Regeneration dependency - Her regeneration depends on being near living creatures with blood. Fighting her in a location without nearby life sources reduces her regeneration significantly.

6. The Heart Below - Attacking the Heart Below directly in her cathedral can weaken her. If the Heart takes damage, Mareatha loses access to legendary actions for 1d4 rounds as she experiences sympathetic pain.

7. Truth as vaccine - Most importantly, Mareatha cannot corrupt or control someone who genuinely does not want her love. The truth - shown through evidence of her victims, testimony from those she's enslaved, or divine revelation - acts as a sort of vaccine against her influence. Once someone truly sees her not as a loving mother but as a parasitic tyrant wearing the mask of compassion, her charms have disadvantage against them.

---

Connection to the Party

The party's first major confrontation with Mareatha's influence occurred in Session 1 at Veinspire Cathedral. They disrupted a ritual involving three heart-chambers beneath the city, killing Sister Marwen and several Pulse-Wardens in the process.

This act marked them as enemies of Mareatha, and she has dispatched Seraphine Vale - a devoted Arterial Inquisitor - to hunt them down, not to kill them, but to capture them for conversion.

---

Related

  • The Thirteen Sovereigns
  • Veinspire Cathedral
  • Seraphine Vale
  • Pulse-Wardens
  • Aedwynn the Maker
