**Organ Base:** The Hands and Sine...

items.title: The Devoured Courts - Locations
items.content:
The Devoured Courts — Locations

---

Overview

Info
The Land of Endless Hunger
The Devoured Courts are where meaning goes to die. Built from the Digestive Engine of Aedwynn — the divine system that processed cosmic significance — this territory exists in perpetual consumption. Here, everything is eaten: food, objects, ideas, identities, purpose itself.

Sovereign: Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress (CR 32) Organ Base: The Digestive Engine (Hunger, Meaning, Consumption) Law: All things must be consumed. All meaning must be digested. Nothing is permanent — not even purpose.

---

Geography Overview

The Devoured Courts cover approximately 470,000 square miles — roughly the size of Ethiopia. It is characterized by:

  • The Stomach Basins: Vast depressions where meaning erodes fastest
  • The Intestinal Passages: Winding canyons of processed landscape
  • The Hunger Fields: Plains where the ground itself consumes
  • The Bile Marshes: Toxic wetlands of conceptual dissolution
  • The Teeth Ridges: Mountain ranges that grind everything down
The climate is humid and uncomfortable — the air feels thick with dissolution. Colors are muted; sounds are dampened; everything feels slightly less real than it should.

---

Major Regions

The Stomach Basins

Type: Depression zones Character: Maximum hunger; meaning erodes fastest

Vast bowl-shaped depressions where the Digestive Engine's hunger is concentrated. Staying too long in a Basin causes existential erosion — first you forget why you came, then you forget who you are, then you forget that you are. The Basins are avoided by natives but used for executions and... processing.

The Intestinal Passages

Type: Canyon networks Character: Trade routes; processed landscape

Winding canyons where meaning has already been partially digested. Travel here is safer than the Basins but still unsettling. Objects lose their names. Ideas become harder to hold. Caravans move quickly and speak in simple, direct terms to avoid losing concepts mid-sentence.

The Hunger Fields

Type: Plains Character: Agriculture (of a sort); dangerous

Flat expanses where the ground itself is hungry. Plants here don't photosynthesize — they consume. Animals don't graze — they hunt. Even the soil will slowly dissolve organic matter left on it. Farming is possible but requires careful planning and rapid harvests.

The Bile Marshes

Type: Wetlands Character: Toxic; conceptual dissolution; avoided

Where the Digestive Engine's waste collects — a landscape of acidic pools, dissolving ground, and the remnants of things that used to mean something. The Marshes are avoided by everyone sane. What lives here is hungry in ways that transcend physical need.

---

Key Locations

1. Consumption — The Hollow Capital

Type: Capital City Population: ~260,000 (number fluctuates as meaning erodes) Location: Built around the Mouth of the World

"We don't build to last. We build to be consumed. That's the point — everything ends in the Empress's mouth."

Description

The capital is built around the Mouth of the World — a massive aperture in the ground that leads to the Digestive Engine's core. The city radiates outward from this hungry center, its architecture designed to be temporary. Buildings are replaced constantly as the hunger erodes them.

Life in Consumption is focused on the present. Planning for the future is difficult when concepts like "tomorrow" feel slippery. Citizens live in cycles of creation and dissolution, building things specifically to feed them to the Mouth.

Districts

DistrictCharacter
The Mouth QuarterImmediate edge of the aperture; most dangerous
The Offering RingsWhere sacrifices are prepared
The Temporary MarketsCommerce; goods only last so long
The Remembering DistrictWhere meaning is preserved (at great cost)
The Dissolution SlumsWhere those losing identity gather
The Feeding PitsIndustrial consumption; processing

Notable Locations

LocationDescription
The Mouth of the WorldPortal to the Digestive Engine; consumes all
The Hollow ThroneVor'Kael's seat; empty yet full of hunger
The Meaning VaultsWhere concepts are preserved (temporarily)
The Feast HallsEndless banquets; never satisfying
The Identity RegistryHelps citizens remember who they are
The Final CourseExecution site; victims fed to the Mouth
---

2. The Meaning Vaults — Preservation Sites

Type: Repository / Fortress Population: ~18,000 (archivists, guards, seekers) Character: Where concepts are protected from consumption

"We hold the last copies of ideas that exist nowhere else. Every day, we lose something. Every day, we save something else."

Description

Scattered throughout the Courts are fortified repositories where meaning is preserved against the ambient hunger. The Vaults use magical and architectural techniques to slow conceptual erosion — but nothing stops it completely.

The archivists who work here are among the most important people in the Courts. They decide what concepts are worth saving and what can be allowed to dissolve. The responsibility slowly erodes their own sense of self; most archivists eventually forget their own names.

What's Preserved

CategoryExamples
WordsNames of things that would otherwise be forgotten
ConceptsIdeas like "hope" or "tomorrow"
IdentitiesRecords of who people were
ObjectsThings with significance
PurposesReasons for actions
---

3. The Stomach Basins — Hunger Zones

Type: Dangerous Regions / Execution Sites Population: None permanent (fatal to remain) Character: Maximum meaning erosion

"I went in to retrieve a merchant's lost cargo. When I came out, I couldn't remember what cargo was. Or merchant. I still don't know what those words mean."

Description

The deepest concentrations of hunger — places where the Digestive Engine's processing is at maximum. The Basins are used for executions, religious sacrifices, and the disposal of things that need to truly cease existing.

Entering a Basin without protection causes rapid erosion: first you lose your purpose for being there, then your memories, then your sense of self, then your physical coherence. The truly dissolved don't die — they just... stop meaning anything.

Major Basins

BasinSizePurpose
The First HungerMassiveVor'Kael's primary feeding site
The Concept GraveMediumWhere ideas are executed
The Identity PoolSmallWhere the self-condemned dissolve
The Forgotten DepthsUnknownSo eroded even its size is forgotten
---

4. The Feeding Pits — Industrial Consumption

Type: Industrial Complex Population: ~70,000 Character: Processing; meaning extraction

"Everything means something. Our job is to extract that meaning before the final consumption. Meaning is valuable. The husks can feed the Empress."

Description

Industrial facilities where objects, creatures, and concepts are processed for their meaning before being fed to the Digestive Engine. The Feeding Pits extract significance — stripping items of their history, their connections, their purpose — and concentrate that extracted meaning into consumable form.

The meaning extracted is the primary currency of the Courts. The husks left behind — objects without significance, people without identity — are given to the Empress.

Products

ProductSourceUse
Distilled PurposeExtracted from meaningful objectsCurrency; magical fuel
Concentrated MemoryExtracted from people/placesIntoxicant; preservative
Pure SignificanceExtracted from sacred itemsUltimate currency
HusksWhat remainsFed to the Empress
---

5. The Feast Halls — Endless Consumption

Type: Religious/Cultural Sites Population: ~40,000 (feasters, servants) Character: Continuous eating; never satisfied

"We've been eating for three hundred years. None of us can remember what full feels like. I'm not sure 'full' exists anymore."

Description

Massive halls where the faithful demonstrate devotion through endless consumption. The Feast Halls serve food continuously — meals that never end, hunger that never fades. Feasters enter intending to eat until satisfied; none ever have.

The theology holds that Vor'Kael achieved enlightenment through perfect hunger. The faithful seek to emulate her by embracing consumption as worship. Most waste away even as they gorge, their bodies consuming themselves to fuel the endless eating.

---

6. The Identity Registry — Remembering Service

Type: Government Service Population: ~12,000 (registrars, clients) Location: Remembering District, Consumption

"Tell me your name. Your mother's name. Your purpose. Now, let me write them down before you forget."

Description

A vital public service — the Identity Registry helps citizens maintain their sense of self against the ambient hunger. Regular visits reinforce who you are, what you want, and why you exist. Citizens who skip appointments often dissolve into nameless husks within months.

The Registry also serves as a resurrection of sorts. If someone dissolves but their Registry records survive, they can be "rebuilt" — given back their identity, though they may not fit the same way.

---

7. The Bile Marshes — Waste Lands

Type: Wasteland / Exile Zone Population: ~15,000 (exiles, scavengers, monsters) Character: Conceptual waste; toxic; strange

"The Marshes are where meaning goes after it's been digested. You can find things there — remnants, echoes, the ghosts of ideas. Most of it will kill you."

Description

Where the Digestive Engine's waste collects — a landscape of pools filled with dissolved meaning, ground made of forgotten concepts, and air thick with erased purposes. The Marshes are toxic to coherent existence but home to creatures that feed on conceptual waste.

Scavengers work the Marshes, retrieving partially-digested meaning that can be refined and sold. The work is dangerous — prolonged exposure causes "concept contamination," where foreign meanings attach to your identity.

---

8. The Teeth Ridges — Border Mountains

Type: Mountain Range Population: ~55,000 (mining communities, border guards) Character: Grinding; defensive; resource extraction

"The mountains chew. Put your hand on the rock and you can feel it — slow, grinding motion. Everything that enters gets broken down."

Description

Mountain ranges that form the borders of the Devoured Courts — except these mountains move. Slowly, imperceptibly, they grind against each other, processing everything caught between them. The Teeth Ridges are natural defenses; invading armies that enter the passes are slowly pulverized.

Mining communities extract the processed materials — things ground so fine they've lost all original identity but gained new properties as pure substance.

---

Society & Culture

The Hunger Hierarchy

ClassRoleNotes
The Hollow EmpressSovereignVor'Kael; perfect hunger incarnate
The SatisfiedInner courtThose who've learned to live with hunger
The ArchivistsPriests/scholarsPreserve meaning against dissolution
The ProcessorsIndustrial classExtract and refine meaning
CitizensGeneral populationStruggling to remain coherent
The FadingLower classLosing identity; desperate
The HusksNon-personsDissolved; still moving

The Economy of Meaning

Currency in the Devoured Courts is extracted meaning:

  • Distilled Purpose: Standard currency
  • Concentrated Memory: Premium currency
  • Pure Significance: Ultimate currency; rare
  • Objects have value based on their meaning, not material
  • A mother's wedding ring is worth more than a gold bar

Identity Maintenance

Remaining yourself requires constant effort:

  • Regular Registry visits
  • Keeping meaningful objects close
  • Maintaining relationships (shared meaning reinforces identity)
  • Repeating your name, your purpose, your history
  • Avoiding the Basins and Marshes
---

Creatures of the Devoured Courts

Native Fauna

CreatureDescription
Hunger WormConsumes objects; excretes husks
Meaning MothAttracted to significant items; feeds on purpose
Concept LeechAttaches to minds; drains ideas
The ForgottenAnimals that lost their species identity
Basin CrawlerThrives where nothing else survives

Supernatural Entities

CreatureCRDescription
Hunger Elemental6Living void; consumes matter and meaning
The Dissolved8What remains of consumed people
Purpose Wraith11Ghost of a lost goal; possesses seekers
Identity Predator14Steals selves; wears them briefly
Concept Devourer17Erases ideas from reality
The Hollow One20Vor'Kael's direct servant; perfect emptiness
---

Travel Times Within the Courts

JourneyDistanceTime (foot)Notes
Consumption → Meaning Vaults~200 miles10 daysStay on passages
Consumption → Teeth Ridges~350 miles17 daysAvoid Basins
Border to border~700 miles35 daysDangerous
Note: Travel in the Courts erodes memory. Bring written records of your destination and purpose.

---

Special Note: Kael's Origin

Kael was vomited up by Vor'Kael — regurgitated from the Digestive Engine as an anomaly. He is something the Empress couldn't digest, which makes him unique and dangerous. His existence raises questions:

  • What was he before consumption?
  • Why couldn't he be processed?
  • What meaning does he contain that resisted dissolution?
  • Does Vor'Kael want him back?
---

Related

  • Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress
  • The Thirteen Sovereigns
  • The Border Marches
  • World Scale Reference
  • Kael (originated here)
---

"Hunger is truth. Everything else — love, hope, purpose — those are lies we tell ourselves while waiting to be eaten. The Empress knows this. She's trying to help us understand." — Consumption theologian

**Sovereign:** [[Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress]] (CR 32)
**Organ Base:** The Digestive Engine (Hunger, Meaning, Consumption)

items.title: The Dominion of Still Air - Locations
items.path: 04 - Locations\The Dominion of Still Air - Locations.md
items.content:
The Dominion of Still Air — Locations

---

Overview

Info
The Land of Silence
The Dominion of Still Air is where sound goes to die. Built from the Lungs of Aedwynn the Maker, this territory exists in perpetual quiet — not peaceful silence, but the oppressive absence of noise. Here, truth cannot hide behind words, and lies die on the tongue before they're spoken.

Sovereign: Azerach the Whispered King (CR 33) Organ Base: The Maker's Lungs (Breath and Voice) Law: Silence is truth. Words are corruption. Listen, and be heard.

---

Geography Overview

The Dominion of Still Air covers approximately 890,000 square miles — the largest Sovereign territory, roughly the size of Algeria. It is characterized by:

  • The Breathless Expanse: Vast desert where sound doesn't carry
  • The Bronchial Canyons: Deep valleys carved from lung tissue
  • The Alveolar Fields: Honeycomb-like formations of air sacs
  • The Tracheal Highway: The main passage through the territory
  • The Listening Peaks: Mountains where Azerach hears everything
The climate is dry and still. Wind exists but makes no sound. The air feels thick, heavy — you breathe the breath of a dead god.

---

Major Regions

The Breathless Expanse

Type: Desert / Central territory Character: Absolute silence; the heart of Azerach's power

A vast desert of pale sand that absorbs sound completely. Travelers here cannot hear their own footsteps, heartbeat, or breathing. Communication is entirely visual. Many go mad from the sensory deprivation. Those who adapt gain extraordinary perception.

The Bronchial Canyons

Type: Canyon network Character: Trade routes; some sound survives

Deep channels carved through calcified lung tissue, where the acoustics are strange but not completely silent. Sound exists here, but distorted — whispers carry miles while shouts die instantly. The canyons are the primary trade routes through the territory.

The Alveolar Fields

Type: Honeycomb formations Character: Settlements; air sac communities

Where the lung's air sacs remain, now enlarged to building-size chambers. Communities live within these organic bubbles, each sac having its own acoustic properties. Some amplify sound; some dampen it; some only allow certain frequencies.

The Listening Peaks

Type: Mountain range Character: Sacred; surveillance; Azerach's ears

Mountains that listen. Every sound made in the Dominion eventually reaches these peaks, where Azerach's servants catalog and analyze. Speaking near the Listening Peaks is speaking directly to the Whispered King.

---

Key Locations

1. Murmur — The Silent Capital

Type: Capital City Population: ~280,000 Location: Built within the largest bronchial junction

"..." (This is how all conversations in Murmur are conducted)

Description

The capital exists where the two main bronchi meet — a vast chamber of calcified tissue where sound behaves according to Azerach's will. The city is eerily beautiful: white architecture, flowing robes, constant movement — all in absolute silence.

Communication in Murmur is through Stillspeech (sign language), Truth Script (written language that cannot convey lies), and Whisper Tokens (magical items that allow brief private speech). Speaking aloud is possible but discouraged — every word reaches Azerach.

Districts

DistrictCharacter
The JunctionCentral plaza; government; Azerach's presence strongest
The Speaking HallsWhere limited speech is permitted; courts, embassies
The Deaf QuarterThose who chose or were forced to lose hearing
The Whisper MarketsTrade; Stillspeech negotiations
The Listening WardIntelligence headquarters; everything is heard
The Breath TemplesReligious sites; worship through exhalation

Notable Locations

LocationDescription
The Throne of WhispersAzerach's seat; hears everything said in the Dominion
The Truth CourtsWhere lies are literally impossible
The Silence ArchiveLibrary of soundless knowledge
The Speaking ChambersSoundproofed rooms for private conversation (extremely expensive)
The Ear GardensWhere ears grow from surfaces; beautiful and disturbing
---

2. The Listening Peaks — Sacred Mountains

Type: Mountain Range / Surveillance Network Population: ~15,000 (monks, listeners, analysts) Character: Where Azerach hears all

Every word you've ever said in this land is recorded in these mountains. Every whisper. Every breath.

Description

A range of mountains in the eastern Dominion that function as Azerach's ears. The peaks are covered in organic growths — ear-like formations that collect and transmit sound across impossible distances. Monks live here, cataloging the whispers of an entire nation.

The Listening Peaks are sacred ground. Pilgrims come to confess — knowing their words will be heard forever. Criminals are brought here for judgment — their past words played back as evidence.

Sites

SitePurpose
The Echo VaultsWhere all recorded sound is stored
The Confession AmphitheaterPilgrims speak their truths
The Judgment PeaksCourts that use past words as evidence
The Monk CellsWhere Listeners live and work
The Forbidden SummitWhere Azerach himself sometimes manifests
---

3. The Tracheal Highway

Type: Major Trade Route Character: The only safe passage through the territory

Follow the windpipe. Stay on the path. Speak only when you must.

Description

The calcified trachea of the Maker forms a highway running the full length of the Dominion — nearly 600 miles of smooth white road. It's the safest route for travelers because sound behaves more predictably here, and Azerach's patrols keep the peace.

Waystation towns have grown along its length, each with specific acoustic rules. Some allow whispered conversation. Others permit only writing. A few — the "Speaking Stops" — allow normal volume speech within designated buildings.

Waypoints

WaypointCharacter
North GateEntry point; customs; language registration
First BreathWaystation; whispers permitted
The BifurcationWhere the route splits; major market
Last WordsWaystation before the deep silence
South GateExit; many stay forever
---

4. The Wordless Cities — Silence Adapted

Type: Deep Desert Settlements Population: ~120,000 (total across multiple cities) Character: Communities that abandoned speech entirely

We have not spoken in seventeen generations. We have not needed to.

Description

In the deepest Breathless Expanse, communities exist that have entirely given up verbal communication. The Wordless Cities are home to people who've developed alternative senses — reading micro-expressions, feeling vibrations, communicating through touch.

They consider outsiders noisy and crude, even if the outsiders never speak. The Wordless can sense the intention to speak, and they find it offensive.

Cities

CityPopulationSpecialty
Hush~35,000Trade in silence-crafted goods
The Unspoken~28,000Philosophers; thought-communication research
Breathhold~22,000Military; silent warriors
Stillness~18,000Artisans; visual art dominates
The Last Word~17,000Where criminals who cannot stop speaking are sent
---

5. The Speaking Halls — Permitted Voice

Type: Government Complex / Embassy District Population: ~25,000 Location: Western Murmur

Here, and only here, you may speak freely. But remember — freely is not secretly.

Description

The only place in the Dominion where speech is officially permitted without restriction. The Speaking Halls house the courts, embassies from other Sovereigns, and negotiation chambers. Foreign diplomats conduct business here, grateful for the ability to use their voices.

The catch: nothing said in the Speaking Halls is private. Every word is recorded, analyzed, and filed. Diplomats know this, which leads to elaborate circumlocutions and coded language that Azerach's analysts spend centuries decoding.

Facilities

FacilityPurpose
The Embassy RowForeign diplomatic missions
The Truth CourtsLegal proceedings with compelled honesty
The Negotiation ChambersBusiness and treaty discussions
The Record HallsWhere every word is transcribed
The Translation BureauConverting speech to Stillspeech
---

6. The Breath Temples — Religious Sites

Type: Temple Complex Population: ~40,000 (priests, devotees) Character: Worship through silence and controlled breathing

We do not pray with words. Words are impure. We pray with breath — the last gift of the Maker.

Description

Religious sites throughout the Dominion where Azerach is worshipped through breathing exercises, meditation, and controlled exhalation. The theology holds that Azerach liberated the world from the corruption of language — words are lies waiting to happen, while breath is pure truth.

Services involve synchronized breathing, visual hymns, and communal silence. The most devoted have their vocal cords removed as a sign of faith.

---

7. The Ear Gardens — Organic Growth

Type: Phenomenon / Sacred Site Location: Throughout the Dominion, concentrated near Murmur

They grow where secrets were told. The land remembers. The land listens.

Description

Throughout the Dominion, ears spontaneously grow from surfaces — walls, floors, rocks, trees. These are Azerach's sensory organs, manifesting wherever the need to hear is strong. Near sites of secrets, the gardens are dense — hundreds of ears covering every surface.

The gardens are beautiful in a disturbing way. The ears come in all sizes and shapes, some clearly human, others... not. They twitch and orient toward sound. Citizens have learned to ignore them.

---

8. The Deaf Quarter — The Silenced

Type: District / Community Population: ~45,000 Location: Northwestern Murmur

We cannot hear, so we cannot be heard. It is freedom of a sort.

Description

Not everyone adapts to the Dominion's silence. Some choose — or are forced — to become deaf, removing themselves from Azerach's auditory surveillance entirely. The Deaf Quarter houses these individuals.

Life in the Quarter is surprisingly vibrant. Art, dance, written literature, and visual expression flourish. The deaf have their own culture, their own governance (largely ignored by Azerach), and their own secrets — the only true secrets in the Dominion.

---

Society & Culture

The Silence Hierarchy

ClassRoleNotes
The Whispered KingSovereignAzerach; hears all, speaks rarely
The ListenersPriests/IntelligenceMonitor and catalog speech
The Silent LordsNobilityFamilies who've never spoken publicly
The BreathlessElite citizensAbandoned speech voluntarily
CitizensGeneral populationSpeak when necessary, quietly
The NoisyLower classThose who struggle with silence
The ExiledOutcastsCompulsive speakers; cannot stop

Communication Methods

MethodUseNotes
StillspeechPrimaryUniversal sign language
Truth ScriptWritingCannot convey lies
Breath CodeMilitaryPatterns of exhalation
Touch LanguageIntimateFor families, lovers
Whisper TokensPrivilegedRare magical items
Permitted SpeechLimitedSpeaking Halls only

The Crime of Lies

In a land where Azerach hears everything, lies are impossible to maintain. The culture has evolved around radical honesty:

  • Questions are answered truthfully or not at all
  • Promises are sacred (Azerach remembers)
  • Reputation is everything (your words define you forever)
  • Privacy is nonexistent (and not missed by natives)
---

Creatures of the Dominion

Native Fauna

CreatureDescription
Silence MothAbsorbs sound in its vicinity
Listener HoundTracks by micro-vibrations, not scent
Breath SerpentLives in respiratory passages; steals breath
Echo BirdStores and replays sounds
Ear MiteParasites that grow ears on hosts

Supernatural Entities

CreatureCRDescription
Silence Elemental6Living absence of sound
The Overheard8Ghosts of secrets; replay endlessly
Word Eater10Devours language; victims forget speech
Listener Construct12Azerach's surveillance entity
Echo Wraith14Spirit trapped in endless repetition
Voice Thief17Steals voices; wears them
Azerach's Whisper20Direct manifestation; kills with words
---

Travel Times Within the Dominion

JourneyDistanceTime (foot)
Murmur → Listening Peaks~320 miles16 days
North Gate → South Gate (Tracheal Highway)~580 miles29 days
Murmur → Wordless Cities~400 miles20 days
Border to border (widest)~950 miles47 days
Note: Travel is slow in the Breathless Expanse due to disorientation from silence.

---

Related

  • Azerach the Whispered King
  • The Thirteen Sovereigns
  • The Border Marches
  • World Scale Reference
---

"In other lands, silence is absence. Here, silence is presence — the presence of truth, of attention, of the King who hears your every breath. We do not fear silence. We fear what fills it elsewhere." — Silent Lord of Murmur
