---

Hooks Summary

Immediate (Session 20)

  • Guild commissions in Gullet's End
  • Missing workers (Labor Union)
  • Vault breach (Preservation Guild)
  • Missing delvers (Delvers' Compact)
  • The Bleached Battlefield is relatively close and accessible

Short-term (Sessions 20-25)

  • What's happening in the Undermaw
  • The Pyloric Gate is humming (why now?)
  • The Congregation of Stillness is worried
  • Rumors of the Refuse King

Medium-term (Sessions 25-30)

  • The Bile Cisterns are filling
  • Border disturbances (Confluence aftermath reaching here)
  • Ultharion's agents searching
  • Pre-Usurpation secrets

Long-term (Sessions 30+)

  • The Failed Connection
  • Sovereign-level involvement
  • Answers about the Usurpation
---

Related

  • Gullet's End
  • The Blooming Rot - Locations
  • The Devoured Courts - Locations
  • The Border Marches
  • Free Cities of the Ashen Realms
---

"The Gutter's full of things that fell through the cracks. Some of them are treasure. Some of them are trouble. Most of them are both."
items.raw:
# The Gutter

---

## Overview

> [!info] The Rejection Zone
> **Location:** Border March between The Blooming Rot (Talaris) and The Devoured Courts (Vor'Kael)
> **Width:** 60-90 miles
> **Length:** ~400 miles
> **Character:** Stagnation, preservation, the rejected and unprocessed

*"Nothing grows here. Nothing rots. Nothing changes unless you make it change. That's either a curse or an opportunity, depending on who you are."*

---

## Why It Exists

Anatomically, The Gutter occupies the space b...

items.title: The Ivory Vale - Locations
items.content:
The Ivory Vale — Locations

---

Overview

Info
The Land of Crystallized Thought
The Ivory Vale is where knowledge becomes physical. Built from the Brain of Aedwynn — the divine organ of cognition — this territory exists as externalized thought. Here, ideas have weight, memories can be held in your hand, and knowing too much can literally crush you.

Sovereign: Ismara the Pale Duchess (CR 32) Organ Base: The Brain (Knowledge, Thought, Enlightenment) Law: All knowledge must be gathered. All secrets must be known. Ignorance is the only sin.

---

Geography Overview

The Ivory Vale covers approximately 510,000 square miles — roughly the size of Peru. It is characterized by:

  • The Cortex Plains: Folded landscapes of neural geography
  • The Synapse Valleys: Deep channels where thoughts flow
  • The Memory Glaciers: Frozen knowledge from ancient times
  • The Thought Spires: Crystallized ideas rising hundreds of feet
  • The Forgotten Wastes: Regions of erased knowledge
The climate is cool and dry — the temperature of preserved thought. The landscape is predominantly white and gray, the color of neural tissue, with crystalline formations of every hue representing different types of knowledge.

---

Major Regions

The Cortex Plains

Type: Folded terrain Character: Most populated; dense with knowledge

Landscapes that mirror the folds of a brain — hills and valleys in complex patterns that somehow facilitate thought. Living here makes you smarter (ambient knowledge seeps in) and more vulnerable (your thoughts can seep out). The Plains are home to most of the Vale's population.

The Synapse Valleys

Type: Canyon networks Character: Trade routes; information highways

Deep channels carved by the flow of thoughts — not metaphorically but literally. Ideas travel through these valleys like water through riverbeds. The cities in the Valleys serve as information hubs, catching and processing the thoughts that flow past.

The Memory Glaciers

Type: Frozen formations Character: Ancient knowledge; dangerous

Massive glaciers of crystallized memory — knowledge so old it predates the Usurpation. Mining the glaciers yields ancient secrets but also ancient dangers. Some memories don't want to be remembered. Some knowledge was frozen for a reason.

The Forgotten Wastes

Type: Erased regions Character: Where knowledge was deliberately destroyed

Regions where Ismara has erased knowledge completely — not just forgotten but removed from the possibility of being known. The Wastes are blank, featureless, and terrifying. Standing in them means having thoughts erased. Nothing survives intact.

---

Key Locations

1. Cognita — The Thinking Capital

Type: Capital City Population: ~340,000 Location: Built within the preserved cerebral cortex

"Every citizen is a neuron in the body of knowledge. Fire together, wire together. Think wrong, and be pruned."

Description

The capital is built within a preserved section of the Maker's brain — the white matter forming walls, the gray matter forming floors, the ventricles forming grand chambers. The city thinks. Citizens report shared insights, collective realizations, and occasional mass delusions as Cognita processes information.

Life here is intellectually intense. Everyone is learning something. Everyone is teaching something. Ignorance is shameful; curiosity is virtue. Libraries are temples, scholars are priests, and books are holy relics.

Districts

DistrictCharacter
The Cortex CoreIsmara's palace; highest thought concentration
The Memory QuarterArchives; knowledge storage; crystallized thoughts
The Learning HallsSchools; universities; eternal students
The Synapse MarketTrade in knowledge, ideas, memories
The Intuition SlumsThose who know without learning; distrusted
The Blank WardWhere the mind-damaged are treated

Notable Locations

LocationDescription
The Pale ThroneIsmara's seat; knows everything known in the Vale
The Infinite LibraryCannot be fully mapped; contains everything written
The Question HallsWhere problems are posed; answers crystallize
The Memory MarketBuy and sell experiences, skills, languages
The Truth EnginesMachines that process raw information
The Forgetting ChambersWhere dangerous knowledge is erased
---

2. The Infinite Library — Knowledge Repository

Type: Library / Dimensional Anomaly Population: ~50,000 (librarians, scholars, lost) Character: Contains every written work; impossible to map

"I've worked here forty years. I've mapped maybe a thousandth of it. The Library grows faster than we can explore."

Description

A library that contains every book, scroll, and document ever written — and many that were only thought about writing. The Infinite Library is spatially impossible; its interior is larger than the Vale itself. Corridors extend for miles. Shelves reach into darkness. Entire wings have been lost for centuries.

The Library is both treasure and trap. Scholars enter seeking specific knowledge and emerge decades later, aged, changed, having found everything except what they sought. The Library doesn't prevent leaving — it just makes staying so appealing.

Sections

SectionContents
The KnownCataloged books; accessible
The SoughtUncataloged; being explored
The LostSections we've lost contact with
The ForbiddenKnowledge too dangerous to access
The UnwrittenBooks that almost existed
---

3. The Memory Glaciers — Frozen Knowledge

Type: Glacier Region Population: ~35,000 (miners, researchers) Character: Ancient knowledge in crystallized form

"We chipped out a memory of the Maker dreaming. It took seventeen researchers to process. Eleven went mad. The other six changed our understanding of divinity."

Description

Massive formations of crystallized memory — knowledge from before the Usurpation, frozen in physical form. The glaciers are mined for ancient secrets, lost techniques, and forbidden understanding. The work is dangerous; some memories fight back.

Extracted memories can be:

  • Studied (safe but slow)
  • Absorbed (fast but dangerous)
  • Sold (lucrative but questionable)
  • Destroyed (sometimes necessary)

Major Glaciers

GlacierAgeContents
The First ThoughtPre-UsurpationFragments of divine cognition
The Era IceVariousLayered history by period
The Dream FrozenUnknownMemories of sleeping gods
The Black GlacierDeliberately obscuredForbidden knowledge
---

4. The Thought Spires — Crystallized Ideas

Type: Natural Formations / Sacred Sites Population: ~20,000 (pilgrims, priests) Character: Ideas made physical

"That spire? That's the concept of justice. The one beside it is the number seven. The broken one was hope — someone damaged it during the Usurpation."

Description

Scattered throughout the Vale are massive crystalline spires — each one a single idea, concept, or thought given physical form. The spires existed before Ismara claimed the territory; she protects them, studies them, and occasionally creates new ones.

The spires have properties reflecting their concepts:

  • The Justice Spire makes lies physically painful
  • The Hope Spire heals despair
  • The Death Spire kills anything that touches it

Major Spires

SpireConceptEffect
The FirstUnknownNo one can figure out what concept it represents
JusticeFairnessForces truth; painful lies
MemoryRemembrancePerfect recall nearby
LoveAffectionDraws people together
DeathEndingLethal to touch
The BrokenHope (damaged)Despair within sight
---

5. The Synapse Markets — Knowledge Commerce

Type: Commercial District Population: ~60,000 Location: Throughout Synapse Valleys

"I can sell you the ability to speak Elvish. I can buy your childhood memories. I can rent you expertise in surgery for a week. Everything knowable is tradeable."

Description

The economic heart of the Vale — markets where knowledge is bought, sold, and traded as physical goods. Memories are stored in crystals. Skills exist as consumable tablets. Languages come in drinkable form. Understanding can be transferred from one mind to another.

The markets are morally complex. Selling your memories means losing them. Buying stolen knowledge means possessing something taken without consent. The economy runs on cognitive extraction, and the exploited class are those with knowledge worth taking.

Products

ProductFormDuration
MemoriesCrystalsPermanent transfer
SkillsTabletsTemporary (hours to months)
LanguagesLiquidVaries by dose
KnowledgeBooks/crystalsPermanent but requires study
ExpertiseComplex ritualPermanent but expensive
---

6. The Forgetting Chambers — Erasure Facilities

Type: Government Facility Population: ~8,000 (operators, prisoners) Location: Throughout the Vale, largest in Cognita

"Some things shouldn't be known. Some things shouldn't be knowable. We make sure they aren't."

Description

Facilities where dangerous knowledge is destroyed — not just forgotten but erased from the possibility of being known. The Forgetting Chambers are used for:

  • State secrets that must never emerge
  • Dangerous techniques (weapons, mind control)
  • Criminal memories (punishment by erasure)
  • Personal traumas (for a price)
The process is irreversible. What goes into the Chambers ceases to exist in any knowable form.

---

7. The Forgotten Wastes — Erased Regions

Type: Wasteland Population: None Character: Knowledge-void; extremely dangerous

"I walked into the Wastes and forgot why. I walked further and forgot I was walking. If I'd gone any further, I would have forgotten I existed."

Description

Regions where Ismara has erased knowledge completely — not just specific information but the capacity to know within that space. The Forgotten Wastes are blank: no landmarks, no features, no memories form within them.

Entering the Wastes causes progressive knowledge loss:

  • First, you forget your purpose
  • Then, you forget your skills
  • Then, you forget your identity
  • Finally, you forget you exist
The Wastes are used as absolute prisons. Those thrown in don't escape because they can't remember they want to.

---

8. The Question Halls — Problem-Solving Complex

Type: Research Facility Population: ~25,000 (researchers, questioners) Location: Eastern Cognita

"Ask a question here, and the Hall will crystallize the answer — eventually. Some questions take years. Some take centuries. Some crack the Hall itself."

Description

Specialized facilities where questions are posed and answers emerge as physical crystals. The Halls tap into the Vale's ambient knowledge, processing queries through architectural thought-engines until solutions precipitate.

The process is slow but reliable. Simple questions (mathematics, history) resolve quickly. Complex questions (philosophy, morality) take longer. Some questions are too dangerous to ask — the answer might destroy the asking.

---

Society & Culture

The Knowledge Hierarchy

ClassRoleNotes
The Pale DuchessSovereignIsmara; knows everything knowable
The EnlightenedInner courtThose with total knowledge of specific domains
ArchivistsPriests/scholarsProtect and organize knowledge
The LearnedEducated classProfessional knowers
CitizensGeneral populationRequired to study
The IgnorantLower classThose who can't or won't learn
The BlankedPrisoners/outcastsThose whose minds were erased

Education as Religion

Learning is mandatory and holy:

  • Children begin formal education at age 3
  • Specialization at 10
  • Graduation (to citizenship) requires passing knowledge tests
  • Continuing education is lifelong
  • Dying while learning is considered blessed

The Mind Tax

Citizens owe knowledge to the state:

  • Memories of significant events
  • Skills developed to a high level
  • Discoveries or insights
  • Final thoughts (collected at death)
---

Creatures of the Ivory Vale

Native Fauna

CreatureDescription
Thought MothFeeds on unprotected ideas
Memory FishSwims through thought-streams; stores memories
Knowledge BeetleProcesses raw information; used in libraries
The MindlessAnimals with no cognitive capacity; pitied
Cortex CrawlerLives in neural architecture

Supernatural Entities

CreatureCRDescription
Thought Elemental6Living idea; obsessed with its concept
Memory Wraith9Ghost of erased knowledge
The Forgotten12Person who fell into the Wastes
Knowledge Parasite14Feeds on learning; leaves ignorance
Concept Incarnate17Physical form of abstract idea
The Pale One20Ismara's direct servant
---

Travel Times Within the Vale

JourneyDistanceTime (foot)
Cognita → Memory Glaciers~350 miles17 days
Cognita → Thought Spires~180 miles9 days
Cognita → Forgotten Wastes~400 miles20 days
Border to border~720 miles36 days
Note: The Infinite Library cannot be measured in travel time. Entry is possible; exit requires finding your way.

---

Related

  • Ismara the Pale Duchess
  • The Thirteen Sovereigns
  • The Border Marches
  • World Scale Reference
---

"Ignorance is comfortable. Ignorance is safe. Ignorance is the only true prison — a cell you don't know you're in. The Duchess offers liberation. The price is everything you think you know." — Cognita scholar
items.raw:

## Overview

> [!info] The Land of Crystallized Thought
> The Ivory Vale is where knowledge becomes physical. Built from the Brain of Aedwynn — the divine organ of cognition — this territory exists as externalized thought. Here, ideas have weight, memories can be held in your hand, and knowing too much can literally crush you.

**Sovereign:** [[Ismara the Pale Duchess]] (CR 32)
**Organ Base:** The Brain (Knowledge, Thought, Enlightenment)
**Law:** All knowledge...

items.title: The Ossuary Kingdom - Locations
items.content:
The Ossuary Kingdom — Locations

---

Overview

Info
The Land of Eternal Structure
The Ossuary Kingdom is built from, on, and of bone — the skeletal foundation of Aedwynn the Maker, now reshaped into a nation of living architecture. Here, everything has structure. Everything has permanence. Everything has a place in the design.

Sovereign: Vortegas the Bone-Warden (CR 32) Organ Base: The Skeleton (Foundation Structure) Law: Everything has a place. Change is decay. Structure is salvation.

---

Geography Overview

The Ossuary Kingdom covers approximately 720,000 square miles — roughly the size of Mexico. It is characterized by:

  • Bone plains: Flat expanses of exposed calcified ground
  • Rib Mountains: Massive curved formations rising thousands of feet
  • Vertebral Valleys: Channels between spinal formations
  • Skull Cities: Settlements built inside and around cranial structures
  • Joint Swamps: Where cartilage tissue remained, now soft and treacherous
The land is largely temperate but dry. Little grows here naturally — the bone leaches moisture and nutrients. What vegetation exists is hardy, calcified, and often partially skeletal itself.

---

Major Regions

The Spinal Reach

Type: Central territory Character: The seat of Vortegas's power

The backbone of the Maker runs through the center of the kingdom — a mountain range of vertebrae, each one the size of a city. Between them, the spinal cord has petrified into roads of smooth white stone. This is where Vortegas holds court, where the Bone Architects work, and where pilgrims come to receive the Warden's blessing.

The Rib Coast

Type: Western border Character: Trade and defense

The western edge of the kingdom is defined by the Maker's ribs — massive curved formations that arc up from the ground like the bars of a cage. Between them, harbors and ports trade with the coastal territories. The ribs also serve as natural fortifications against invasion.

The Pelvic Basin

Type: Southern lowlands Character: Agriculture and industry

The hip structure of the Maker forms a vast bowl-shaped depression — one of the few areas where soil accumulates and farming is possible. This region feeds the kingdom. It's also where most of the non-noble population lives, far from the political intrigue of the Spinal Reach.

The Skull Wastes

Type: Northern frontier Character: Wild, dangerous, sacred

The Maker's skull lies in the far north — fractured, partially buried, and home to ancient secrets. Vortegas does not fully control this region. Things live in the cranial cavities that predate his rule. Pilgrims journey here seeking knowledge. Most don't return.

---

Key Locations

1. Ossathar — The Throne of Bones

Type: Capital City Population: ~450,000 Location: Built in and around three fused vertebrae

"Every citizen is a bone in the body of the kingdom. Every bone has its place."

Description

Ossathar is the largest city in the kingdom and one of the largest in the Ashen Realms. It rises vertically through three massive vertebrae — the lower city in the foundations, the middle city in the main chambers, the upper city in the spinal processes that reach toward the sky.

The architecture is seamless — building and bone are indistinguishable. Streets are smooth white calcium. Walls are reinforced marrow. Every structure was grown by Bone Architects over centuries, not built by conventional means.

Districts

DistrictLocationCharacter
The FoundationLower vertebraWorking class, industry, arrivals
The MarrowMiddle vertebraCommerce, residence, daily life
The ProcessUpper vertebraNobility, temples, Bone Architects
The ForamenThe holes in the boneCriminal underworld, forgotten spaces
The Throne ChamberCentral spinal canalVortegas's court

Notable Locations

LocationDescription
The Throne of BonesVortegas's seat — grown from the original vertebra
The Architect AcademyWhere Bone Architects are trained
The Tithe HallWhere citizens contribute their bones after death
The Eternal ArchiveLibrary carved into bone; knowledge preserved forever
The Marrow MarketsCommercial heart of the city
The Forgetting ForamenCriminal district in the bone's natural holes
---

2. Calvaria — City of the Skull

Type: Sacred City / Dangerous Frontier Population: ~25,000 (varies; many pilgrims) Location: Built around and inside the Maker's fractured skull

"The Maker's thoughts still echo in these chambers. Some can hear them. Most go mad."

Description

The skull of Aedwynn lies half-buried in the northern wastes, fractured by the violence of the Usurpation. Within its cranial cavities, a city has grown — part pilgrimage site, part archaeological expedition, part nightmare.

The skull's chambers still contain traces of divine cognition. Pilgrims come seeking visions, wisdom, or proof of the Maker's nature. The echoes of a god's dying thoughts drive most to madness. Those who survive often gain strange insights — or stranger compulsions.

Vortegas tolerates Calvaria but does not fully control it. The skull resists his authority in ways other bones do not.

Areas

AreaDescription
The ApproachThe city outside the skull; relatively normal
The Fracture GatesEntry points where the skull cracked
The Outer ChambersExplored, mapped, reasonably safe
The Inner SanctumPartially explored; visions common
The Deep ThoughtBrain cavity; unexplored; forbidden

Hazards

  • Thought Echoes: Random visions from the Maker's dying mind
  • Cognitive Parasites: Things that evolved to feed on divine thought
  • Madness Zones: Areas where sanity degrades rapidly
  • The Forbidden: Vortegas's guards who kill those who go too deep
---

3. Ribhaven — Port of the Cage

Type: Trade Port Population: ~180,000 Location: Between two massive ribs on the western coast

"We trade with everyone. Even the enemies of our lord. Especially them — their gold spends just as well."

Description

The largest port in the Ossuary Kingdom, built in the natural harbor formed by two of the Maker's ribs curving into the sea. The ribs themselves serve as breakwaters, natural cranes, and defensive walls — ships sail between them like passing through the bars of a cage.

Ribhaven is cosmopolitan by Ossuary standards. Traders from other territories mingle here. Ideas and goods from across the Ashen Realms flow through. The Bone-Warden tolerates this because trade brings wealth, and wealth builds structure.

Districts

DistrictDescription
The DocksHarbor front; warehouses; fishing
Foreign QuarterWhere non-citizens live and trade
The Rib WalkWealthy district built on the ribs themselves
BonetownWorking class; local population
The CageWhere the ribs meet the water; black market
---

4. The Femur Road

Type: Major Highway Character: Primary east-west trade route

"The road never ends. The road never changes. The road is eternal."

Description

The Maker's femur — the largest bone in the divine body — lies on its side across the eastern portion of the kingdom. Over centuries, it has been carved into a highway running hundreds of miles from the Spinal Reach to the eastern borders.

The Femur Road is the primary trade route for eastern commerce. Caravans travel its length constantly. Waystation towns have grown at regular intervals along its surface. The bone itself is so massive that the road runs along its top — walking the Femur means walking on what was once a god's thigh.

Waypoints

WaypointMiles from OssatharCharacter
Joint's Beginning0Where the femur meets the pelvis
Marrow Rest80Town built around a marrow access point
The Midpoint190Largest waystation; market town
Condyle's Edge340Near the knee joint; rough territory
Joint's End400Border town; gateway to other territories
---

5. The Pelvic Farms

Type: Agricultural Region Population: ~1.2 million (dispersed across the basin) Location: The hip bowl formation in the south

"We feed the kingdom. They barely remember we exist. That's fine. We prefer it that way."

Description

The massive bowl formed by the Maker's hip bones collects sediment, moisture, and organic matter — the only place in the kingdom where large-scale agriculture is possible. Here, the population lives in scattered farming communities, far from the politics and intrigue of the Spinal Reach.

The people of the Pelvic Farms are considered lower-class by Ossuary standards — they work with living things, not eternal bone. But without them, the kingdom would starve.

Communities

CommunityPopulationCharacter
Acetabulum~45,000Largest town; market center
The Hip Villages~500-2,000 eachScattered farming settlements
Cartilage Swamp~8,000Where bone meets preserved cartilage
The Nutrient Pits~3,000Process organic matter for fertilizer
---

6. The Architect's Spire

Type: Training Facility / Religious Site Population: ~5,000 (mostly Architects and students) Location: A single vertebral process rising 800 feet

"To shape bone is to shape eternity. We do not build — we grow the future."

Description

The tallest point in Ossathar is also the most sacred — the Architect's Spire, where Bone Architects learn their craft. These are the closest thing the kingdom has to priests — workers who can shape bone through a combination of magic, science, and devotion to Vortegas.

The Spire is a school, temple, and laboratory. Students spend decades learning to understand bone at a fundamental level before they're allowed to shape even a doorway. The greatest Architects can grow buildings, bridges, and entire districts given enough time.

Levels

LevelPurpose
FoundationInitiates; menial work; learning basics
The Lecture HallsFormal education; theory
The Shaping LabsPractical training; bone manipulation
The Devotion ChambersReligious training; connection to Vortegas
The Master's PeakGrand Architects only; direct communion
---

7. The Tithe Vaults

Type: Repository / Religious Site Location: Beneath Ossathar Character: Where the dead contribute to eternity

"Your flesh was borrowed. Your bones are forever. When you die, they return to the kingdom."

Description

Every citizen of the Ossuary Kingdom is expected to tithe their bones upon death. The Tithe Vaults are massive underground repositories where these contributions are stored, sorted, and eventually used — incorporated into buildings, bridges, roads, and monuments.

This is not seen as morbid but as an honor. Your bones become part of the eternal kingdom. You contribute to structure that will outlast empires. Death is not an ending but a transformation into permanence.

Contents

  • Citizen Bones: The tithe of common people
  • Noble Ossuaries: Separate storage for aristocratic remains
  • Architect Bones: The most valued; used for the finest work
  • The Unclaimed: Criminals, foreigners, those without family
  • The Forbidden Vault: Bones of traitors; never used, never destroyed
---

8. The Joint Marches

Type: Border Region Character: Where bone meets other territories

"Beyond the joints, the world goes soft. Unreliable. We guard the edges of certainty."

Description

The edges of the Ossuary Kingdom are defined by the Maker's joints — shoulder, hip, knee, ankle. These are transitional zones where bone gives way to preserved cartilage, then to the territories of other Sovereigns.

The Joint Marches are militarized. Bone soldiers garrison the borders. Watchtowers of calcified tissue monitor the approaches. These are dangerous places — the cartilage zones are unstable, and hostile creatures often emerge from the softer tissues.

Major Joints

JointAdjacent TerritoryCharacter
The Great ShoulderCrucible of Flesh (Aral-Vyn)Tense; disputed border
The Hip GateDevoured Courts (Vor'Kael)Trade route; monitored heavily
The KneeBone Fields (Border March)Military front
The Ankle MarchShattered Gravitas (Eredain)Unstable; gravity anomalies
---

Society & Culture

The Bone Hierarchy

ClassRoleNotes
The Bone-WardenSovereignVortegas himself; absolute authority
Grand ArchitectsHigh priestsShape bone; advise Vortegas
Bone NoblesAristocracyFamilies with ancient lineage
ArchitectsProfessionalsThose who can shape bone
CitizensGeneral populationThose who live within the skeleton
Flesh-WorkersLower classThose who work with living things
The UnclaimedOutcastsForeigners, criminals, the structureless

The Tithe

Every citizen owes their bones to the kingdom upon death. This is:

  • A religious obligation
  • A legal requirement
  • A source of building material
  • A guarantee of eternal existence (in a sense)
Refusing the tithe is one of the worst crimes possible. Your bones are confiscated anyway, but they go to the Forbidden Vault — used for nothing, contributing to nothing, existing in purposeless darkness forever.

Bone Architecture

Buildings in the Ossuary Kingdom are not constructed — they are grown. Bone Architects:

  • Understand the deep structure of calcium and collagen
  • Can accelerate or redirect bone growth
  • Create seamless, organic structures over years or decades
  • Consider their work a religious act
The result is architecture unlike anywhere else in the Ashen Realms — smooth, white, flowing, with no joints or seams, buildings that look like they emerged from the ground fully formed.

---

Creatures of the Ossuary Kingdom

Native Fauna

CreatureDescription
Marrow HoundCanines adapted to bone environments; partially skeletal
Calcium BeetleInsects that process bone; used in construction
Cartilage SlugLives in joint regions; disgusting but edible
Bone HawkBirds of prey with reinforced skeletal structure
Rib SerpentSnake that lives in bone crevices; venomous

Supernatural Entities

CreatureCRDescription
Bone Dancer4Animated skeleton with perfect grace
Marrow Wraith7Spirit trapped in bone; guards locations
The Structured9People who became bone; still conscious
Calcium Elemental11Living bone given purpose
Architect Gone Wrong14Bone shaper merged with their work
Skeletal Titan18Massive construct of accumulated bone
---

Travel Times Within the Kingdom

JourneyDistanceTime (foot)
Ossathar → Ribhaven~380 miles19 days
Ossathar → Calvaria~450 miles22 days
Ossathar → Pelvic Farms~200 miles10 days
Full Femur Road~400 miles20 days
Border to border (widest)~850 miles42 days
Note: The Bone Gates allow instant travel between major cities for those who pay the tithe.

---

Related

  • Vortegas the Bone-Warden
  • The Thirteen Sovereigns
  • The Border Marches
  • World Scale Reference
---

"Flesh rots. Stone crumbles. Ideas fade. Only bone endures. We are building eternity, one vertebra at a time." — Grand Architect Kethras

items.title: The Shattered Gravitas - Locations
items.content:
The Shattered Gravitas — Locations

---

Overview

Info
The Land of Broken Weight
The Shattered Gravitas is where gravity became emotion and emotion became gravity. Built from the Resonance Organ of Aedwynn — the divine structure that regulated cosmic harmony — this territory exists in perpetual physical chaos. Here, feelings have weight, and weight has feeling.

Sovereign: Eredain the Choirless Monarch (CR 31) Organ Base: The Resonance Organ (Sound, Emotion, Gravity) Law: Harmony must be restored. Discord must be purged. Feel correctly, or fall forever.

---

Geography Overview

The Shattered Gravitas covers approximately 550,000 square miles — roughly the size of France and Germany combined. It is characterized by:

  • The Floating Isles: Landmasses suspended at various heights
  • The Gravity Wells: Regions of crushing weight
  • The Harmonic Plains: Areas of stable gravity (rare)
  • The Discordant Wastes: Chaotic zones where gravity changes constantly
  • The Resonance Spires: Towers that regulate local gravity
The climate is irrelevant — weather is determined by collective emotional state. Happy populations enjoy sunshine; grieving cities experience rain; angry regions suffer storms.

---

Major Regions

The Floating Isles

Type: Suspended landmasses Character: Where most civilization exists

Islands of earth and stone floating at various altitudes — from a few feet off the ground to miles in the sky. Rope bridges, flying creatures, and gravity manipulation connect them. The higher the island, the more prestigious its inhabitants.

The Gravity Wells

Type: Depression zones Character: Crushing weight; mining; punishment

Regions where gravity is many times normal. Walking is exhausting; standing is difficult; falling is fatal. The Wells are mined for gravitationally-compressed materials and used as prisons for those whose emotions Eredain finds discordant.

The Harmonic Plains

Type: Stable zones Character: Normal gravity; agriculture; valued

The rarest regions — areas where gravity functions normally. These are the breadbaskets of the Shattered Gravitas, where farming is possible and life is almost normal. They're fiercely contested and carefully protected.

The Discordant Wastes

Type: Chaotic zones Character: Unpredictable gravity; dangerous; wild

Where gravity changes without warning — you might walk normally one moment, float the next, then be crushed into the ground. The Wastes are home to exiles, monsters, and those whose emotions are too chaotic for civilized life.

---

Key Locations

1. Resonance — The Floating Capital

Type: Capital City Population: ~290,000 Location: Cluster of interconnected floating islands

"Feel the correct feelings, and you will rise. Feel incorrectly, and you will fall. It's not punishment — it's physics."

Description

The capital is not one island but hundreds — a constellation of floating landmasses connected by bridges, chains, and trained flying creatures. The highest islands house Eredain's court; the lowest house the emotionally unstable. Your altitude is your social status, and your social status is your emotional purity.

Movement between levels requires emotional screening. Guards with resonance-tuned crystals detect discordant feelings. Feel the wrong thing at the wrong time, and you physically cannot rise — the gravity of your island increases, trapping you.

Districts (by altitude)

DistrictAltitudeCharacter
The Monarch's ChoirHighest (~3 miles)Eredain's court; perfect harmony
The Upper ResonanceHigh (~1-2 miles)Nobility; emotionally pure
The Middle OctavesMedium (~half mile)Professional class; mostly stable
The Lower FrequenciesLow (~500 feet)Working class; emotional variance
The GroundedSurface levelThose who cannot rise; discordant
The WellsBelow groundPrisoners; the emotionally criminal

Notable Locations

LocationDescription
The Throne of HarmonyEredain's seat; perfect gravitational balance
The Resonance SpireControls gravity throughout the city
The Emotional CourtsWhere feelings are judged
The Choir HallsTraining in emotional correctness
The Falling GardensWhere the discordant are dropped
The Ascension PlatformsWhere the worthy rise to higher levels
---

2. The Gravity Wells — Crushing Depths

Type: Mining Zones / Prison System Population: ~80,000 (miners, prisoners, guards) Character: Extreme gravity; valuable resources

"The deeper you go, the heavier you become. At the bottom, your bones become your coffin."

Description

Massive depressions in the earth where gravity multiplies — 2x, 5x, 10x normal at the deepest points. The Wells are mined for gravitationally-compressed materials: impossibly dense metals, pressure-crystallized gems, and substances that only form under crushing weight.

Working the Wells is punishment for emotional crimes. Miners are often prisoners sentenced to labor until their discordant feelings are literally crushed out of them. Survival rate is low. Those who emerge are emotionally flattened — incapable of strong feeling, but stable.

Major Wells

WellGravityPrimary Resource
The First Depth3xDense iron
The Crushing Pit5xPressure diamonds
The Sorrow Well7xGrief-stone (absorbs sadness)
The Deepest10x+Unknown (no one returns)
---

3. The Harmonic Plains — Stable Ground

Type: Agricultural Region Population: ~1.1 million (dispersed farms and towns) Character: Normal gravity; precious stability

"Normal gravity is a gift here. We don't ask why our region is stable. We just thank the Monarch and keep farming."

Description

The most valuable land in the Shattered Gravitas — regions where gravity simply works as expected. Crops grow. Animals walk. Buildings stand. The Plains feed the entire territory and are defended with almost religious fervor.

The stability isn't natural — massive Resonance Spires maintain gravitational harmony. Damage to a Spire can destabilize an entire region, causing crops to float away or farmers to be crushed. Spire maintenance is the highest calling in the Plains.

Communities

CommunityPopulationCharacter
Harmony's Rest~45,000Largest town; Spire maintenance center
The Stable Valley~30,000Agricultural hub
Groundheart~25,000Mining (conventional, not gravity mining)
The Weighted~18,000Slightly heavy gravity; hardier crops
---

4. The Discordant Wastes — Chaos Lands

Type: Wilderness / Exile Zone Population: ~40,000 (scattered, nomadic) Character: Gravity changes constantly; dangerous

"North is sometimes up. East is sometimes down. We navigate by the screaming — you can always hear the Wastes."

Description

Where gravity follows no rules. Direction, weight, and fall direction change without warning, sometimes varying from step to step. The Wastes are home to those exiled for emotional crimes, monsters adapted to chaos, and the genuinely insane who find stability unbearable.

Survival requires constant adaptation. Wastewalkers (guides who navigate the chaos) have developed senses for predicting gravity shifts. They're expensive, essential, and frequently wrong.

Hazards

  • Gravity Flips: Up becomes down without warning
  • Weight Surges: Sudden increase can crush or break bones
  • Float Zones: Zero gravity; drift until you starve
  • Shear Lines: Different gravity on each side; tearing damage
  • Emotional Storms: Collective feeling causes massive instability
---

5. The Resonance Spires — Gravity Regulators

Type: Structures / Sacred Sites Population: ~35,000 (total across all Spires) Character: Maintain gravitational harmony

"The Spires sing, and gravity obeys. We tend the song, and the Monarch tends us."

Description

Massive crystalline towers scattered throughout the Shattered Gravitas, each one emitting a tone that regulates local gravity. The Spires are the reason any stable regions exist at all — without them, the entire territory would be Discordant Wastes.

Each Spire is tended by Resonance Keepers — monks trained to maintain the harmonic frequencies. They live ascetic lives, their emotions carefully cultivated to produce the correct resonance. A Keeper's emotional breakdown can destabilize an entire region.

Major Spires

SpireRegion ControlledFrequency
The First ToneCapital clusterDeep bass
The Agricultural ChordHarmonic PlainsMid-range
The Border HarmonyEastern frontierHigh pitched
The DampenerNear Discordant WastesDiscordant (countering)
---

6. The Emotional Courts — Justice System

Type: Court Complex Population: ~15,000 Location: Upper islands of Resonance

"You felt anger when you should have felt acceptance. You felt joy when you should have felt solemnity. These are not personality traits. These are crimes."

Description

Eredain's courts don't judge actions — they judge feelings. Emotional crimes include: feeling the wrong emotion, feeling the right emotion too strongly, feeling insufficiently, or failing to feel when feeling is required.

Defendants are scanned by resonance crystals that detect emotional states. Evidence includes emotional residue left at crime scenes. Sentences range from emotional re-education to gravity well labor to execution (being dropped from the highest island).

Crimes

CrimeDescriptionTypical Sentence
DiscordanceWrong emotion for situationRe-education
Excessive FeelingEmotion too strongDampening treatment
Emotional VoidInsufficient feelingStimulation therapy
Resonance DisruptionCausing others to feel incorrectlyGravity well labor
Harmonic TreasonDeliberately destabilizingExecution (dropping)
---

7. The Choir Halls — Training Centers

Type: Educational/Religious Complex Population: ~25,000 (students, instructors) Character: Learning correct emotion

"You will learn to feel joy on cue. You will learn to suppress grief when required. Your emotions are instruments. We will teach you to play them."

Description

The primary educational institution of the Shattered Gravitas, where citizens learn emotional control from childhood. The Choir Halls teach that emotions should be performed correctly, like music — the right feeling at the right time with the right intensity.

Graduates can modulate their emotional states at will, rising and falling through gravity zones as needed. Failures are sent to lower altitudes for remedial training or, in severe cases, emotional dampening.

---

8. The Falling Gardens — Execution Site

Type: Execution Ground / Memorial Location: Edge of the capital cluster

"They fall for miles. The discordant have time to reflect on their errors. If they achieve harmony before impact, they float. Most don't."

Description

A gap in the floating islands where the condemned are dropped. The fall is long — miles of open air before the crushing gravity of the Wells. In theory, a falling criminal who achieves emotional harmony could stabilize and float. In practice, terror prevents this.

The Falling Gardens are also a memorial. Flowers grow on the cliff edges, planted by families of the executed. The gardens are beautiful, peaceful, and perpetually on the edge of an abyss.

---

Society & Culture

The Resonance Hierarchy

ClassRoleAltitude
The Choirless MonarchSovereignHighest
The Perfect ChordInner courtVery high
Resonance KeepersSpire priestsHigh
The HarmoniousNobilityAbove middle
The TunedCitizensMiddle
The FlatLower classBelow middle
The DiscordantCriminals/exilesGrounded or lower

Emotional Regulation

Feelings are not private in the Shattered Gravitas:

  • Public emotional displays are regulated
  • Wrong feelings are criminal
  • Resonance crystals detect emotional states
  • Children are trained from birth
  • Naturally emotional people are "treated"

Altitude as Status

Your ability to rise determines your worth:

  • High altitude = emotional purity = social status
  • Low altitude = emotional instability = poverty
  • Grounded = failure = near-criminal
  • In the Wells = criminal = punished
---

Creatures of the Shattered Gravitas

Native Fauna

Gravity BeetleHeaviest insect; used in mining
Sky WhaleMassive creatures drifting between islands

Resonance BirdSong affects local gravity

Supernatural Entities

CreatureCRDescription

The Fallen9Ghosts of the executed; still falling
Emotional Construct11Solidified feeling given form
Resonance Wraith13Spirit that disrupts gravity
The Discordant One16Chaos creature from the Wastes
The Perfect Note19Eredain's direct servant; perfect harmony
---

Travel Times Within the Shattered Gravitas

JourneyDistanceTimeNotes

Resonance → Gravity Wells~150 miles8 daysDescending is faster
Border to border~750 miles37 daysAvoiding Wastes
Through Discordant WastesVariableVariableDangerous; unpredictable
Note: Altitude changes complicate distance. Flying is common; walking is for the poor.

---

Related

  • The Thirteen Sovereigns
  • The Border Marches
  • World Scale Reference
---

"They say Eredain seeks to restore cosmic harmony. I think he just hates that anyone feels anything he didn't approve. Harmony isn't peace — it's control with a prettier name." — Grounded exile

items.title: Verdant Pass
items.content:
Verdant Pass — The Wound

---

Overview

Abstract
The Place Where Nothing Rots
Type: Geographic Anomaly / Sacred Site
Location: Southeastern edge of the Blooming Rot
Sovereign: None — Talaris cannot reach here
Significance: ✅ Final resting place of Mira and Elias Alethain (Session 19)

---

Quick Reference

AttributeValue
DomainOutside Talaris Bloomrend's reach
EnvironmentBarren stone ravine
InhabitantsNone living
Travel TimeHalf-day from Congregation Grove
Known NamesThe Wound, Verdant Pass, The Place That Refused
---

Description

The Threshold

A line in the earth marks where the Blooming Rot ends. On one side: moss, fungi, roots, life consuming death — the full expression of Talaris's power. On the other: nothing.

Grey stone. Bare rock. A narrow ravine where the forest refuses to grow.

The transition is absolute. One step you're in the eternal garden. The next, you're in sterile silence.

Inside the Wound

The ravine extends for roughly a mile — a scar in the landscape where Talaris's influence cannot reach.

Environmental Features:

  • Cold, dry air
  • Absolute silence — no insects, no birdsong, no wind
  • Stone that has never known decay
  • Darkness that lanterns cannot fully dispel
  • Footsteps that echo strangely
The Bleeding Blade's Reaction: When Jonas entered Verdant Pass, the cursed sword went cold and quiet. There was no life to drink, no blood to sense. The blade was useless here — a tool designed for the living, powerless in a place that has forgotten what living means.

Sol's Reaction: The shard of Aedwynn within Sol pulsed with recognition. This was a place the Maker knew — or rather, a place that remembered the Maker.

---

The Cave of Waiting

Description

At the center of the ravine, a natural cave mouth opens in the cliff face. Low entrance, perhaps twenty feet across. Inside: bare stone, absolute stillness, and perfect preservation.

Properties

PropertyEffect
No DecayNothing rots; organic matter is preserved indefinitely
No GrowthPlants cannot grow; fungal spores die
Temporal StasisTime has diminished meaning; the dead remain as they were
Divine ImmunityTalaris's power cannot reach; possibly immune to all Sovereign influence

Current Occupants

Success
The Aletheins — Together at Last

Elias Alethain — Skeleton, pristine white, hands folded across chest. Died 22 years ago. Has been waiting ever since.

Mira Alethain — Skeleton, placed beside Elias by Fursen (Session 19). Wedding ring on finger. Hand positioned to hold Elias's.

Two skeletons hold hands in the darkness. Husband and wife. Healer and saint. Fifty-two years of separation, finally ended.

---

Origin — The Wound

What Happened Here

When Talaris Bloomrend claimed the Blooming Rot as his domain after the Usurpation, his power spread through every inch of organic matter. The cycle claimed everything.

Almost everything.

Something in this ravine resisted. The nature of the resistance is unknown:

  • A fragment of Aedwynn's will?
  • A pre-Usurpation ward?
  • A natural phenomenon that predates the Sovereigns?
  • The stubborn refusal of the stone itself?
Whatever it was, Talaris's power flowed around it like water around a rock. The wound healed, but the scar remained.

Talaris's Perspective

The Choir Blooms call this place "The Wound" — not with anger, but with a kind of respect. When asked about it, they acknowledge:

"The healer who died there chose where to end. The King respects that choice. It is... discourteous, perhaps. But there is courage in choosing where you die, even if the place is beyond the cycle."

Talaris tolerates the anomaly. He could not claim it even if he wanted to — and perhaps he doesn't want to. Even the Verdant King, who believes all things must join the cycle, recognizes that some choices are sacred.

---

Session 19 — The Reunion

The Party's Arrival

The party arrived at Verdant Pass on the morning of Day 3, having passed through:

  • Sweetwater Marsh (Sol's compulsion and rescue)
  • Pilgrim's Spine (Bloom Hound burning, lie detected)
  • Hunger Garden (Thornback Behemoth killed, Talaris spoke)
  • Congregation Grove (The Questioning, Sol's offering accepted)
The Grove Wardens — thirty-foot humanoids of wood and bark — bowed their heads as the party passed toward the Wound. A mark of respect for what they carried and what they had given.

Finding Elias

Inside the Cave of Waiting, they found Elias Alethain exactly as he'd been for twenty-two years:

  • Skeleton on bare stone
  • Hands folded across chest
  • No rot, no fungal growth
  • Clean white bones, pristine
He'd been waiting.

The Reunion

Fursen lowered the barrel containing Mira's remains. He lifted out her bones — skull, ribcage, spine, arms, hands — and laid them beside Elias. Parallel. Close enough to touch.

Sol placed Mira's wedding ring on her finger. The ring had been cold since he retrieved it from the gore in the Catacomb Reliquary. When he slid it onto her finger, it warmed — not magical warmth, just the warmth of remembering.

Sol positioned Mira's hand until her fingers rested against Elias's. The wedding ring gleamed between them.

Fursen spoke:

🦁 "I am sorry we could not reunite you in life. But at least in death, we have brought you together."

The party left them there. Two skeletons holding hands in a place where nothing rots.

---

Travel Information

Getting There

FromToTimeDifficulty
Congregation GroveVerdant PassHalf-dayEasy (if permitted)
Note: Passage requires permission from the Congregation Grove. The party earned passage through Sol's offering of Aedwynn's memory.

Inside the Pass

The ravine is approximately one mile long. The Cave of Waiting is roughly at the center.

Hazards: None — this is one of the only truly safe places in the Blooming Rot.

Exiting

Beyond the ravine, the Blooming Rot ends entirely. The party emerged into:

  • Rolling hills
  • Brown grass
  • Normal trees (oak, ash)
  • A dirt road toward distant mountains
  • Warm sunrise
Normal world. No eternal garden. No cycle. Just... ordinary land.

---

Significance

To Talaris

The Wound is a reminder that even a Sovereign's power has limits. Talaris tolerates it — perhaps even respects it — but he cannot claim it.

To the Party

This is where they completed Mira's quest. The culmination of Session 19's journey. The place where a two-thousand-year-old saint finally rests beside her husband.

To the Campaign

Verdant Pass represents hope — proof that some things exist beyond Sovereign control. In a world where the Thirteen claim dominion over all aspects of existence, this barren ravine stands as evidence that exceptions exist.

If a place can resist Talaris, perhaps other things can resist other Sovereigns.

---

Related

  • Mira Alethain
  • Elias Alethain
  • Talaris Bloomrend the Verdant King
  • The Blooming Rot - Locations
  • Campaign Sessions
  • The Party
---

Two skeletons hold hands in the darkness. Husband and wife. Healer and saint. In a place where nothing rots and nothing changes. Finally together.

items.title: Aedwynn the Maker
items.content:
Aedwynn the Maker

Status: Dead (but dreaming) Type: The Murdered God Significance: The cosmic being whose corpse forms the world

---

Overview

Aedwynn the Maker was the cosmic being who shaped the world from living organs, breathing existence into divine flesh. The world itself is built upon Aedwynn's corpse—a biological megastructure where geography is anatomy and landscapes follow the contours of the god's body.

---

The Sleeping God

Aedwynn is not dead.

Though murdered by the Thirteen during the Usurpation, Aedwynn's consciousness did not fully die—it scattered across reality like divine shrapnel. The god's dreams leak into reality, causing:

  • Omens
  • Divine storms
  • Reality anomalies
  • Stirrings in the world's foundation
Every destroyed heart, every disrupted organ fragment disturbs Aedwynn's slumber.

---

The World as Corpse

The world is a biological megastructure grown around Aedwynn's remains. It contains three layers:

1. Skinlands (Surface World)

Continents shaped by divine anatomy where most mortal civilizations exist.

2. Arterial Deepways

Fossilized arteries and veins connecting all kingdoms—ancient blood vessels hardened into traversable tunnels beneath the surface.

3. Organ Realms

Thirteen overlapping dominions shaped by the stolen organs of the Sovereigns, each existing in warped reality where natural laws bend to divine will.

---

The Usurpation

During the Era of the Thirteenfold Murder, thirteen mortals murdered the sleeping Maker to steal its organs. Aedwynn did not die in battle—it was ritual murder, calculated and obsessive.

The murder was not a battle—it was surgery.

Each of the Thirteen stole an organ with a specific obsession:

  • Azerach the Whispered King severed the Lungs
  • Imhuran the Regent of Hours carved out the Heart of Time
  • Mareatha the Vein-Mother ripped free the Circulatory Engine
  • Eredain the Choirless Monarch stole the Resonance Organ
  • Vortegas the Bone-Warden claimed the Skeletal Foundation
  • Kaedris the Crimson Matron seized the Left Heart
  • Aral-Vyn the Bladed Saint harvested the Hands and Sinews
  • Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress consumed the Digestive System
  • Ultharion the Obsidian Shepherd peeled the Skin and Boundary
  • Ismara the Pale Duchess extracted the Brain
  • Talaris Bloomrend the Verdant King took the Liver
  • Nhalyra the Azure Queen submerged into the Soulflow
  • Karthayne the Hollow Crown grasped the Heart of Creation
When Aedwynn died:
  • Reality fractured
  • The corpse became the landscape
  • Each killer transformed into a Sovereign
  • The organs became realms
  • Time, space, and meaning destabilized
---

The Era of Aedwynn's Breath (Creation)

Before the murder, there was Era I: The Era of Aedwynn's Breath—the beginning when the Maker shaped the world from living organs, breathing existence into cosmic flesh.

In this era:

  • The world was perfect, terrible, and singular
  • Geography was living anatomy
  • Perfect unity of matter and divinity existed
  • No death, no time, no change
  • The god's body was made manifest as landscape
This era ended when the Thirteen Conspirators discovered the location of Aedwynn's physical form.

---

Aedwynn-Born

Some mortals are born with dormant fragments of Aedwynn's scattered consciousness embedded within their souls. These rare individuals, known as Aedwynn-Born, carry pieces of the dead god trying to reconstitute itself.

Sol Raven is believed to be one of these god-touched individuals—a shard of Aedwynn meant to be reclaimed if the Maker ever rises.

---

Current Status

Aedwynn twitches in its slumber.

The party's actions—particularly quieting three hearts in Veinspire Cathedral—have disturbed the sleeping god. With each disruption to the Sovereigns' stolen organs, Aedwynn stirs closer to waking.

The question remains: If the Maker awakens, will it:

  • Reclaim its stolen body?
  • Punish the Thirteen?
  • Restore the world—or unmake it entirely?
---

The Question of Resurrection

Various factions seek to resurrect Aedwynn:

  • Aedwynn Reclamation Cells — Secret societies aiming to reassemble scattered organ fragments
  • Restoration Cults — Those who worship the Maker's return
  • Scholars — Those who study whether resurrection is even possible
But if Aedwynn awakens, what will remain of the world built on its corpse?

---

Related

  • The Thirteen Sovereigns
  • Aedwynn-Born
  • Sol Raven
  • The Party
  • History & Timeline
  • Campaign Index - The Ashen Realms
---

"The Maker is not dead. It dreams beneath the world, and every heartbeat we quiet brings it closer to waking."

items.title: Aedwynn-Born
items.content:
Aedwynn-Born (God-Touched)

Classification: Divine Fragment Bearers Population: Extremely rare (approximately 1 in a million) Origin: Born with dormant fragments of Aedwynn's scattered consciousness Notable Example: Sol Raven

---

Overview

The Aedwynn-Born are individuals born with dormant fragments of the dead god's essence embedded within their souls. They are not chosen—they are accidents of cosmic resonance, born when echoes of the Maker's death ripple through bloodlines.

They are not Sovereigns, but carry divine potential.

---

Nature & Origin

What They Are

Aedwynn-Born are fragments of the Maker attempting to reconstitute itself. When Aedwynn was murdered by the Thirteen, its consciousness did not fully die—it scattered across reality like divine shrapnel.

Some of these fragments embedded themselves in mortal bloodlines, lying dormant for generations until they manifest in a newborn child.

How They Manifest

At Birth:

  • Most appear as baseline members of their species
  • No obvious physical differences initially
  • Parents rarely know what their child is
During Awakening:
  • Divine potential activates during moments of extreme stress, resonance exposure, or proximity to organ fragments
  • Abilities manifest related to specific aspects of the Maker
  • Dreams become vivid and prophetic
  • Unconscious resistance to Sovereign influence begins
---

Characteristics & Traits

Physical Manifestations

Subtle Signs:

  • Eyes that sometimes reflect divine light
  • Skin that resists certain forms of corruption
  • Wounds that heal in unusual patterns
  • Temperature slightly cooler or warmer than normal
  • Dreams that affect nearby sleepers
During Power Manifestation:
  • Brief moments of translucence
  • Resonance with nearby organic matter
  • Spontaneous understanding of divine anatomy
  • Ability to perceive the "pulse" of stolen organs

Psychological Traits

Common Experiences:

  • Feeling "different" or "watched" from childhood
  • Dreams of unity and wholeness
  • Inexplicable knowledge of things they shouldn't know
  • Sense of incompleteness or missing pieces
  • Attraction to sites of divine significance
  • Discomfort near Sovereign-controlled territories
Memory Fragments:
  • May experience visions of Aedwynn's memories
  • Brief flashes of the Usurpation
  • Dreams of being something larger and more complete
  • Sense of ancient grief and betrayal
---

Abilities & Powers

Core Capabilities

Divine Resonance:

  • Can sense the presence of organ fragments
  • Feel the "pulse" of the world's divine anatomy
  • Detect when Sovereign influence is nearby
  • Perceive reality's anatomical structure
Resistance to Sovereign Influence:
  • Blood-loyalty grafts are less effective
  • Temporal manipulation affects them unpredictably
  • Thought-plagues struggle to take root
  • Digestive magic cannot fully dissolve them
  • Gravity manipulation is inconsistent around them
Potential Awakening:
  • May manifest abilities tied to specific organs:
- Circulatory Connection: Control over blood flow, healing - Skeletal Affinity: Understanding of structure, permanence - Resonant Attunement: Sound manipulation, emotional gravity - Cognitive Echo: Memory access, thought-sharing - Regenerative Touch: Healing through controlled decay

Limitations

Incomplete Power:

  • They carry only fragments, not full divine authority
  • Powers are unpredictable and unstable
  • Awakening can be dangerous or even fatal
  • Overuse risks attracting Sovereign attention
Vulnerability:
  • Sovereigns can sense them if they manifest too openly
  • Some Sovereigns seek to consume them
  • Others want to study or weaponize them
  • The Thirteen view them as threats or resources
---

Theories About Their Purpose

Theory 1: Aedwynn's Resurrection

Belief: The fragments are attempting to reassemble Aedwynn by drawing together across generations.

Evidence:

  • Aedwynn-Born are drawn to each other
  • Dreams of unity intensify when multiple are nearby
  • Some report feeling "more complete" in proximity to others
Implication: If enough Aedwynn-Born were to gather, the Maker might awaken through them.

Theory 2: Immune System Response

Belief: The fragments are Aedwynn's immune system, fighting the infection of Sovereign rule.

Evidence:

  • Natural resistance to Sovereign influence
  • Tendency toward rebellion against divine authority
  • Ability to disrupt organ fragments
Implication: They are weapons the dying god created to eventually reclaim its body.

Theory 3: Accidental Echoes

Belief: The Aedwynn-Born are simply cosmic accidents—divine debris with no purpose.

Evidence:

  • Manifestations are random and unpredictable
  • No pattern to their births
  • Many live and die without awakening
Implication: They may have potential, but no grand design.

---

How Sovereigns View Them

The Hunters (Want to Destroy)

Mareatha: Views them as corrupted blood that must be purified Imhuran: Sees them as temporal errors to be removed from the ledger Ultharion: Considers them threats to identity stability

The Collectors (Want to Study)

Ismara: Desires to extract and crystallize their memories of divinity Vor'Kael: Wants to consume and digest the fragments they carry Azerach: Curious about what truths they hold

The Pragmatists (Want to Use)

Eredain: Sees potential for resonance weapons Vortegas: Believes they could reinforce divine structures Aral-Vyn: Views them as raw material for perfection

The Ambitious (Want to Reclaim)

Karthayne: Wonders if they could be used to unmake reality Nhalyra: Desires to dissolve them into the collective dream Talaris: Curious if they would bloom into something new

---

Notable Aedwynn-Born

Sol Raven

Status: Confirmed Aedwynn-Born (high probability)

Evidence:

  • Skeletal nature suggests connection to Maker's anatomy
  • Divine resonance fire
  • Resistance to blood-loyalty in Veinspire
  • Visions during the 13-Hour Trial
  • Sister Marwen's reading: "You carry a memory not your own… and something wants it awakened"
Significance: Sol may be a particularly powerful fragment, possibly connected to the Maker's skeletal system or death aspect.

Historical Examples

The Silent Prophet of Ossathar (Era VII)

  • Aedwynn-Born who could speak to bones
  • Unified three kingdoms before being ossified by Vortegas
The Dreamweaver of Azure (Era VI)
  • Merged with Nhalyra's ocean voluntarily
  • Some believe they became part of the collective
The First Heretic (Era III)
  • Revealed the truth of the Usurpation
  • Hunted and killed by all Thirteen Sovereigns working together
---

How to Identify an Aedwynn-Born

Definitive Signs

1. Resistance to Sovereign influence despite exposure 2. Dreams affecting reality or touching others' minds 3. Spontaneous divine resonance without training 4. Recognition by organ fragments (they react to presence) 5. Memory flashes of the Usurpation or Aedwynn's life

Possible Signs

  • Unusual healing patterns
  • Animals behaving strangely around them
  • Technology or magic malfunctioning unpredictably
  • Sense of being incomplete
  • Attraction to sites of divine murder

Diagnostic Methods

Resonance Testing: Exposure to organ fragments causes reaction in Aedwynn-Born.

Blood Analysis: Blood of Aedwynn-Born contains trace amounts of divine cellular structure.

Dream Reading: Their dreams contain patterns matching Aedwynn's consciousness signature.

---

Cultural Perception

Among Scholars

The Veiled Academy: Studies them as keys to understanding the Maker Resonance Theorists: View them as allies in the fight against Sovereigns Temporal Philosophers: Debate whether they're paradoxes or inevitabilities

Among Common Folk

In Mareatha's Territory: Seen as heretics with impure blood In Vortegas's Domain: Respected as carriers of ancient structure In Azerach's Realm: Feared for what truths they might speak In Free Cities: Hoped to be saviors or feared as omens

Among Religions

Orthodox Sovereignist Churches: Declare them heretical or demonic Restoration Cults: Worship them as fragments of the true god Usurpation Believers: See them as proof Sovereigns can be challenged

---

Dangers Faced

Hunted by Sovereigns

Each Sovereign has agents tasked with finding and dealing with Aedwynn-Born:

  • Inquisitors hunt them in Mareatha's lands
  • Regulators remove them from timelines in Imhuran's realm
  • Silence-Keepers silence them in Azerach's domain

Hunted by Cults

  • Aedwynn Reclamation Cells seek to gather them forcibly
  • Concept Reavers want to consume their fragments
  • Various fanatics see them as sacrifices or saviors

Internal Danger

  • Awakening can be lethal if uncontrolled
  • Divine power can overwhelm mortal bodies
  • Fragments may have conflicting purposes
  • Connection to Aedwynn can drive madness
---

The Question They Represent

If the Maker's consciousness still exists in fragments across the world, can it be reassembled?

And if it can... should it?

The Maker was murdered for a reason. Its rule was absolute. Its power was total. If Aedwynn awakens, will it reclaim its body? Will it punish the Thirteen? Will it restore the world—or unmake it entirely?

The Aedwynn-Born carry the answer in their blood, their bones, their dreams.

They are the Maker's scattered consciousness.

They are fragments of a dead god trying to wake.

---

Related

  • Aedwynn the Maker
  • Sol Raven
  • The Thirteen Sovereigns
  • Species & Cultures
  • The 13-Hour Trial
---

"We are echoes of something vast and broken, dreaming of unity we cannot remember."

items.title: Criminal Underworld
items.content:
Criminal Underworld

The Thirteen Sovereigns may rule through divine power, but beneath their domains thrives a vast criminal underworld. These syndicates traffic in organs, stolen time, smuggled breath, and concepts themselves.

---

MAJOR SYNDICATES

1. The Vein-Cutters

Type: Blood-smuggling empire Operations: Siphoning arterial canals to sell pure divine ichor

The Vein-Cutters operate in the shadows of Veinspire Cathedral, tapping into Mareatha the Vein-Mother's circulatory infrastructure. They extract "pure blood"—divine ichor that hasn't been corrupted by mortal circulation.

Trades:

  • Vein-essence (concentrated divine blood)
  • Heartshards (crystallized emotional bonds)
  • Purified arterial tissue
Organization:
  • Led by a figure known only as "The Siphon"
  • Operates through legitimate blood donation fronts
  • Uses corrupted Pulse-Wardens as inside agents
Goal: Undermine Mareatha by draining her infrastructure, weakening her hold over the faithful.

Locations: Sangreal Dominion, particularly around Veinspire

---

2. Whisperfoot Cartel

Type: Silent assassins guild Operations: Soundless murder using stolen breath

The most feared assassins in the world, the Whisperfoot Cartel has stolen fragments of Azerach the Whispered King's power. They can kill without making a sound—victims simply stop breathing, their lungs collapsing into perfect silence.

Specialty:

  • Soundless murder
  • Smuggled lung-fragments
  • Breath-theft (stealing final words)
  • Silent infiltration
Methods:
  • Stolen Sibilant techniques
  • Breath-denying poison
  • Acoustic assassination (killing through removing sound from an area)
Goal: Dominate the hired killing market across all capitals

Signature: Victims are found with small ears blooming from their skin, Azerach's mockery of the Cartel's theft.

---

3. The Bonejackers

Type: Organ thieves and skeleton harvesters Operations: Harvesting skeletons for marrow black markets

Operating primarily in Ossathar and the Pale Dune Route, the Bonejackers specialize in "liberating" bones from the living and the dead. They process marrow into oils, drugs, and structural materials.

Trades:

  • Marrow-oil (narcotic and fuel)
  • Skeleton fragments
  • Ossified dust
  • Bone grafts
Tactics:
  • Grave robbery on an industrial scale
  • Kidnapping for skeletal extraction
  • Bribing Ossifiers to look the other way
Goal: Control marrow-oil production, the most valuable substance in bone-trade

Enemy: Vortegas the Bone-Warden considers them blasphemers deserving of eternal imprisonment

---

4. Hollow Coin Triad

Type: Financial crime syndicate Operations: Abstract economic manipulation

The most conceptual of criminal organizations, the Hollow Coin Triad doesn't steal money—they steal value itself. They traffic in concept-debt, metaphysical inflation, and abstract laundering.

Specialty:

  • Moving concept-debt across borders
  • Laundering stolen meaning
  • Creating false economies
  • Debt-binding rituals
Operations:
  • Convert stolen goods into "potential value"
  • Sell futures that never existed
  • Trade in obligations that can't be fulfilled
  • Own the debts of entire cities
Goal: Own the value system itself, making all commerce dependent on their abstract banking

Influence: Economic destabilization in every major capital

---

5. Sky-Leviathan Poachers

Type: Illegal megafauna hunters Operations: Capturing airborne creatures

Based in the floating reefs of Elystra, these poachers hunt the massive flying creatures that drift through resonance currents.

Trades:

  • Sky-leather (lighter than air, impervious to arrows)
  • Levitation organs
  • Gravity bladders
  • Resonance bones
Methods:
  • Sonic nets
  • Gravity harpoons
  • Skyship ambushes
  • Breeding illegal hybrids
Goal: Traffic sky-leather and levitation organs to nobles and military forces

Threat: Their overhunting is causing sky-leviathan populations to collapse

---

6. Sporeback Mafia

Type: Rot-crime families Operations: Illegal fungal grafting and regeneration drugs

Families operating in Talar'Noth who sell illegal fungal grafts, regeneration drugs, and "second lives" through controlled decomposition.

Trades:

  • Regeneration mushrooms
  • Immortality spores (temporary)
  • Resurrection grafts
  • Memory-preserving fungi
Services:
  • Illegal rebirth contracts
  • Black-market regeneration
  • Identity preservation in fungal form
  • Controlled decomposition for the wealthy
Goal: Dominate the regeneration drug market

Methods: Extortion, forced grafting, fungal infection as punishment

---

7. Palewater Corsairs

Type: Dream-pirates Operations: Raiding soul-caravans in the Azure Depths

These underwater raiders prey on vessels transporting bottled dreams, memories, and identities across The Azure Depths.

Targets:

  • Soul-caravans
  • Memory-merchants
  • Identity-preservation ships
  • Psychic cargo
Trades:
  • Stolen identities
  • Bottled dreams
  • Liquid memories
  • Consciousness fragments
Tactics:
  • Submarine ambushes
  • Psychic boarding parties
  • Dream-hijacking
  • Memory-ransom
Goal: Steal and sell bottled identities to those wishing to escape their past

---

MID-TIER CRIMINAL NETWORKS

8. Echo-Forgers

Counterfeiters specializing in resonance metals and sound-based currencies. Create fake harmonic signatures.

9. Redhour Runners

Illegal time couriers who smuggle "frozen seconds" and temporal contraband. Work around Imhuran the Regent of Hours's time regulations.

10. Dream-Launderers

Wash stolen dreams into new forms, removing identifying markers. Make nightmares into pleasant memories for sale.

11. Time-Smugglers

Transport frozen seconds across temporal borders. Sell "extra time" to the dying or imprisoned.

12. Grave-Glass Dealers

Sell crystallized grief and sorrow as luxury goods. Operate grief-harvesting operations at funerals.

13. Spinebreak Syndicate

Bone-breaking extortionists who threaten structural integrity. Particularly feared in Ossathar.

---

MINOR UNDERWORLD GROUPS

14. Marrow Pickers

Petty corpse thieves who strip bodies for marrow before official processing.

15. Pulse-Peddlers

Street dealers selling cheap emotional highs through diluted Arterial blood.

16. Shatter-Scribes

Write collapsing runes that destroy property for insurance fraud or revenge.

17. Shadow-Dice Dealers

Run illegal gambling dens where bets are placed with mirror-lives and alternate selves.

18. Organ-Nappers

Kidnap victims for anatomical ransom—return the person for payment, or harvest organs.

19. Nerve-Thieves

Steal sensory nerves, leaving victims numb. Sell stolen sensation to the jaded wealthy.

---

Underworld Territories

Sangreal Dominion: Dominated by Vein-Cutters and Pulse-Peddlers Ossathar: Bonejackers control the bone trade Dominion of Still Air: Whisperfoot Cartel operates freely Elystra: Sky-Leviathan Poachers own the sky-routes Talar'Noth: Sporeback Mafia runs regeneration rackets Azure Depths: Palewater Corsairs raid underwater trade routes The Clockwork Sanctum: Time-Smugglers and Redhour Runners operate in temporal shadows

---

Criminal Codes

The Breath Pact

Honor among Whisperfoot assassins—never speak a target's name aloud after accepting a contract.

The Marrow Law

Bonejackers never harvest children's bones (though this is often violated).

The Coin Silence

Hollow Coin Triad members never explain their methods, even under torture.

The Spore Oath

Sporeback families never kill with fungus—only transform or preserve.

---

Law Enforcement

Against Criminals:

  • The Arterial Inquisition (Mareatha's paladins)
  • Regulators (Imhuran's temporal police)
  • Silence-Keepers (Azerach's hunters)
  • Osteal Judges (Vortegas's enforcers)
Corrupt Officials: Many law enforcement agents are bribed, blackmailed, or secretly working for syndicates.

---

Related

  • The Thirteen Sovereigns
  • Major Factions & Organizations
  • Locations & Realms
items.raw:
